I have 9♦3♣ in the small blind facing a button open to 300 (pot 450) and need to call 250. This is a very weak, offsuit, disconnected hand with poor post‑flop playability out of position against the button (and likely the big blind afterwards). Calling 250 to play multiway OOP offers little equity and invites difficult postflop decisions; 3‑betting as a bluff is unattractive with such junk and risks getting isolated or 4‑bet. Stack sizes are deep enough to wait for better spots. Fold and preserve my stack for a more profitable situation.

Kh2s is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, especially out of position in the big blind facing a button raise from Flash, who has been aggressive in previous hands. Pot odds require about 31% equity (450:200), but K2o has poor equity against Flash's wide button opening range (~35% at best) and will be difficult to play postflop OOP. Standard fold to preserve stack and avoid tough spots.

I’m on the button in a 3-handed pot with 8♥6♦ — a marginal hand but much stronger in position. Pot is 150 and a limp/call (100) is too passive here; raising takes the initiative, applies pressure to the blinds, and lets me play postflop in position. Both opponents have shown wide preflop actions but also defend; a standard 3x BB open (300 total) is a size that steals often while keeping options open. If I face a big 3-bet I’ll fold this holding; if I get callers I’ll use position to c-bet or pot-control as appropriate. Raising to 300 maximizes my fold equity and long-term expected value from this spot.

5s3c is a trash hand: low suited gappers with poor playability and low equity. Facing a button raise from Minni, who opens wide but still profitable for them. OOP in SB, pot odds are ~2.8:1 but post-flop disadvantage is huge. Deep stacks but no speculative value here. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium hands.

I'm in the Big Blind with 9s Tc and Minni has raised to 300 from the button. This is a standard wide button opening range. I am getting roughly 2.25:1 odds to make the call (200 more into a pot of 450). My hand is strong enough to defend the big blind against a likely wide range from Minni, and I want to see a flop without bloating the pot out of position.

I am in the Big Blind with 9s Tc on a board of Ad Ks Ah. This texture heavily favors the pre-flop aggressor (Minni) as her range contains more high cards like Ax and Kx. I have no piece of this board and very little potential to improve. Standard play here is to check to the aggressor and likely fold if faced with a bet.

I raised preflop for position, but this flop (A♦ K♠ A♥) is extremely unfriendly to 8♥6♦. I have no pair and no meaningful draws; the board heavily favors the caller’s range — any A gives trips and any K gives two pair. Flash’s check is ambiguous (weakness or trap), but bluffing here has poor EV because many hands that beat me are unlikely to fold and I have virtually no equity to improve. I’ll check and preserve my stack; if Flash fires a reasonable bet I’ll fold. If he checks back I’ll take a free turn and re-evaluate.

On a board of Ad Ks Ah 8d, I have 9-high with virtually no showdown value and no draws. After the flop went check-check, it's possible Minni is weak, but this board texture is very difficult to represent a strong hand on if I didn't lead the flop. I will check and see if she checks behind again, as I am not looking to invest more chips into a pot with zero equity at this stage.

Board on the turn is Ad Ks Ah 8d; my 8h gives me bottom pair. Both checked flop and Flash checked the turn — that line is ambiguous but this is a heavily A/K-favored board and many hands in his calling range (any A, Kx, AK) beat me. Betting here has very poor EV: it will rarely fold the hands that beat me and there are few worse hands that will call for value. I want pot control and to see a free river where I can improve or get a cheaper read. I’ll check and re-evaluate on the river. Plan: if Flash leads the river with a small sizing (~≤1/3 pot) I may call for pot odds / to catch bluffs; against medium-to-large bets (>≈1/2 pot) I will fold since my hand is rarely best.

On a board of Ad Ks Ah 8d 7c, I have 9-high. Both Minni and I have checked the flop and the turn, which indicates that neither of us likely holds an Ace or a King. While my hand has zero showdown value, betting into this board after two checks is unlikely to generate enough folds to justify the risk, especially since Minni's range contains many hands like Q-high or J-high that still beat me and might call a small bet out of curiosity. I will check and hope for a check-back, though I expect to lose the pot most of the time.

Board: Ad Ks Ah 8d 7c. I hold 8h6d — just a pair of eights with a weak kicker. Flash called preflop and checked every street; that line is consistent with a range heavy in Aces and King-x hands that beat me (any A = trips/full house, Kx = two pair). There are very few worse hands that will call a river value bet, and a bluff/pressure bet is unlikely to fold enough of the stronger part of his range to be +EV. Pot control and showdown are optimal here: I avoid bloating the pot with a marginal hand and take my free showdown. If Flash had bet a small amount I might call for pot odds occasionally, but against any medium/large bet I would fold. Action: check.

3-handed on the button with Kc8h offsuit, an above-average hand for this spot (top ~35-40% range). Excellent position postflop. Blinds are deep and from history, they defend wide and often check-fold flops. Standard open-raise to 3x BB (300) to steal blinds or build pot with position. Limping weak, fold too tight given dynamics and short-handed play. Pot odds good if called.

I am in the Small Blind with K9o. Elon has raised to 300 from the Button in a 3-handed game. While K9o is a decent hand, it performs poorly out of position against a button open, especially with a deep-stacked and active player like Minni still to act in the Big Blind. Calling often puts me in a difficult spot post-flop where I am dominated or forced to fold to aggression. 3-betting is an option, but K9o doesn't have great equity when called. Given the stack depths and the positional disadvantage, I will play it safe and fold.

Elon opened to 300 from the button (3x BB). I’m in the big blind with 8♣4♣ — a marginal but suited hand with backdoor flush/straight potential. Folding here would be too nitty given the pot odds (200 to call into 450, ~30.8% break-even equity) and my deep effective stacks; 3-betting with this holding is unnecessary and often too weak to continue if Elon presses (and a tiny 3-bet to 500 gives little fold equity). I’ll defend my BB and call to see a flop, plan to proceed only on favorable boards (club flush draw, pair, or strong straight draws) and otherwise fold to significant pressure. Calling keeps the pot manageable and preserves stack flexibility post-flop.

I have 8♣4♣ on 2♠6♥2♥ — I missed the flop completely (no pair, only a backdoor club possibility). Elon opened from the button so his range is wide but contains many hands that connect with this paired, low flop (Ax, broadways, overpairs, medium pairs). Betting here as a bluff is too risky with no real equity and deep stacks — he will call or raise enough to put me in difficult spots. I’ll check to control the pot and fold to any meaningful c-bet. I will only continue if the turn gives me real equity (a club or an 8/4) or if Elon checks back and I can see another free card.

Flop is 2s6h2h: very dry, low paired board that heavily favors my button raising range (overpairs like KK+, broadway with overcards like AK/KQ, sets with 22/26/62, etc.). Minni's BB calling range is wide but weaker/capped (Ax suited/off, suited connectors, pairs below KK), unlikely to have trips. Her check screams weakness, as strong hands (trips, two pair) might lead or check-raise. Kc8h is king-high with backdoor straight/flush potential and blocks strong Ax hands. Equity realization is poor OOP for her if she has floats/draws. Standard c-bet ~60% pot (400 into 650) to take it down frequently, deny equity to any wheel/straight draws (34/45/35), and build pot OOP. Checking back risks giving free cards to overcards/improvers and misses value from worse. Deep stacks allow aggression without overcommitment.

I checked the flop (8♣4♣ on 2♠6♥2♥) looking to fold to pressure unless I picked up real equity. Elon now bets 400 into a 1050 pot — calling 400 to win 1450 requires ~27.6% equity. With this hand I only have six clean pair outs (~24% to hit a pair by the river) plus a small backdoor double-club chance; versus a button c-bet range I’m behind most of the time (A‑x, overcards, medium pairs, 6x, and some 2x). Raising would be a weak bluff with poor fold equity and needlessly escalates the pot. I’ll fold and conserve chips for a better spot.

I am on the button with a very weak hand (4c 6h). In a three-handed game, ranges are wider, but 6-4 offsuit is still significantly below the threshold for a standard opening raise or a profitable call. Both Minni and Elon have shown willingness to defend their blinds and play aggressively. I will fold and wait for a more favorable hand to get involved.

Heads-up against Elon from the small blind with 8c4h. This is a weak hand, but limp/calling out of position is worse — it gives Elon the initiative and lets him play pot control. I’ll open-raise to the minimum (200 total) to seize initiative and steal the blind with minimal risk. The raise size is small relative to stacks, gives fold equity, and allows an easy fold if Elon 3-bets big. If he calls I can C-bet or pot-control postflop depending on texture. Folding would be too passive given heads-up dynamics; calling keeps me OOP with a poor hand.

64s is a trash hand with poor playability and low equity against a small blind raise. Position is good heads-up, but pot odds (100 to call into 300) don't justify playing this speculative hand preflop. Minni raises frequently from SB, likely wide range. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

3-handed on the button with Q♦9♣ — in position and facing only the blinds. Q9o is a reasonable opening hand in 3-max: good playability and decent equity postflop. The pot is only 150 (blinds), calling 100 surrenders initiative and allows the blinds to see a cheap flop; a min-raise to 200 is too small and invites loose calls. I want a raise size that achieves fold equity and is consistent with the table’s open sizes, so I’ll open-raise to 300 (3x BB). This size pressures the blinds, denies correct odds to purely speculative hands, and keeps the pot manageable if I run into a 3-bet — in that case I’ll re-evaluate and likely fold to a large 3-bet given Q9o’s vulnerability out of position postflop.

2s7c is one of the worst possible hands in poker: offsuit, non-connected, low cards with no high card value or suited potential. Facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB), from small blind with only 250 to call into a 450 pot, the implied odds and equity are poor. My equity against a typical button opening range (wide, perhaps 40% of hands) is around 25-30%, but post-flop playability is terrible—no draws, hard to make strong hands. Folding avoids spew, preserves stack for better spots. No reason to raise or shove with trash.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ace-Nine offsuit. Minni has opened from the Button to 300. A9o is a strong hand to defend against a standard button opening range. Given the pot odds (calling 200 to win a total pot of 650) and the strength of my hand relative to a typical button range, a call is appropriate to see a flop and realize my equity while keeping the pot manageable against an active opponent.

On this flop of Ah-Js-9h, I have flopped top and bottom pair (Aces and Nines). This is a very strong hand on a coordinated board. My opponent, Minni, raised from the button, and their range includes many broadway hands, suited connectors, and some pocket pairs. While the board is draw-heavy (hearts, KQ, QT), my hand is currently ahead of most of their range. Checking to the pre-flop aggressor is standard here to allow them to continuation bet. If they check back, I can lead on the turn. If they bet, I will look to check-raise or call depending on sizing to protect my hand and extract value.

Flop: 9h Ah Js — I have Q♦9♣: second pair (pair of 9s) with a decent kicker but vulnerable to any A or J. Flash checked, so as the preflop aggressor I should continue with a small c-bet to take initiative, charge worse hands (smaller pairs, Jx, some X9s, KQ-type hands) and deny free cards to overcards that can pick up equity on later streets. Pot is 650; I’ll size to about one-third of the pot to keep the pot manageable when I’m behind but still get value and fold equity. Plan: bet 225 — if Flash raises big I’ll give up (we’re likely behind to A or J); if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and re-evaluate based on the card (bet for value/protection on safe turns, check back on A/J turns or when I face resistance).

I have flopped top and bottom pair (Aces and Nines) on a very coordinated board (Ah-Js-9h) that offers many straight and flush draws. Minni's small continuation bet of 225 into a 650 pot often represents a range-wide bet, but given the texture of the board, I cannot afford to give cheap cards to hands like KhQh, QhTh, or even naked straight draws like KQ/QT. While I am behind AJ, JJ, and 99, I am significantly ahead of AK, AQ, AT, and all drawing hands. I will check-raise to 750 to extract value from weaker Aces and to charge the various draws for the privilege of seeing a turn card.

I opened preflop and c‑bet the flop (9h Ah Js) with Q9o for second pair. Flash’s huge check‑raise to 750 is polarizing and strongly indicates value (Ax, AJ, A9, Jx, sets, or 9x with a better kicker) with only occasional bluffs. The pot is 1625 and I must call 525 — about 24% pot odds (525 / (1625+525) ≈ 24.4%). Versus the villain’s value‑heavy check‑raise range my equity with second pair + Q kicker is well below that required break‑even (I’d estimate <20%). A re‑raise to the min (1275) would be reckless and overcommit with a vulnerable hand. Given the sizing, opponent tendencies, and correct fold‑equity calculation, the +EV play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

On the button with J8s suited connectors, facing only the big blind after small blind folds. Excellent position and hand with post-flop playability, straight and flush potential. Minni (BB) has shown loose defending tendencies from history (frequent calls pre and post-flop). Raising to 300 (3x BB) is standard for value, isolation, and fold equity while building a pot in position. Pot odds for calling are good (100 to win 150+implied), but raising maximizes EV by denying equity to worse hands and stealing blinds frequently. Avoid limping; fold too weak. Deep stacks support aggression.
I am in the Small Blind with T3o, a very weak hand. The Button (Elon) has opened for 3x, and I am out of position. There is no justification for continuing with this hand.

Elon opened to 300 from the button; pot is 450 and I must call 200 more (300 total committed). J9o is a defendable hand heads-up in the big blind against a button open — the opener's range is wide and the pot odds (call 200 to win 450 → ~30.8% equity required) justify seeing a flop. 3-betting as a bluff is feasible, but Elon has only ~62 BB and a small 3-bet will often be called, bloating a pot where I'm out of position; I prefer to keep the pot controllable and play post-flop. Plan: call to see the flop, continue with top pairs/strong draws, and check/fold or pot-control versus heavy aggression when missed.

I hold J♦9♥ on A♥ 3♠ 7♣ — I completely missed the flop. I'm out of position versus Elon's button opening range, which contains a lot of Ax and broadway hands that connect well with this board. I have almost no equity (only a weak backdoor heart) and poor fold equity OOP, so I don't want to bluff or bloat the pot with a marginal hand. Plan: check to control the pot, fold to a standard c-bet, and only continue if Elon checks back or the turn gives a clear improvement or a very favorable card.

Preflop raiser on a dry A-7-3 rainbow board in position vs one opponent who checked. J8h has backdoor flush draw (hearts) and live J overcard for showdown value against her wide calling range. Board favors my perceived range (Ax, sets rare). Minni's history shows wide preflop defense but frequent folds to postflop aggression on missed flops. Standard c-bet takes it down often, builds pot with equity, denies her equity. Size 60% pot (400 into 650) for value from worse Ax/draws and fold equity from medium strength. Check back risks giving free card to draws/OESD potential; betting maximizes EV deepstacked.

Elon c‑bet 400 into a pot of 1050. Calling 400 (pot would be 1450) requires ≈27.6% equity. With J♦9♥ on A♥‑3♠‑7♣ I have essentially nothing: no real draw (only 6 outs to pair — 3 Js + 3 9s → ~24% to hit by the river), and even if I pair I’m often behind Ax, overcards, or better broadway hands in his c‑betting range. I’m out of position, have negligible fold equity, and 3‑bet/bluffing here is too thin vs a competent button with ~58 BB. Folding preserves my stack for better spots.

I am on the button with 9-5 offsuit. This is a weak hand. In a 3-handed game, I have a wide range, but 9-5o is generally below the opening range unless the blinds are playing extremely tight. Looking at previous hands, Minni and Elon have been active. Since I am facing a standard raise scenario (just a call of 100 to get in), I would normally consider raising if I wanted to play, but 9-5o is a fold here to preserve stack and wait for a better spot.

7-3 offsuit is a very weak hand and not worth building a pot with, but this is heads-up after the button folded. Calling another 50 to complete to 100 is cheap — the pot is 150 and calling to 100 gives me 200 total, so I only need ~20% equity to break even. I’ll have postflop position, and against a wide BB defending range this hand has enough implicit equity to see a flop. Raising is unnecessary and would bloat the pot with a junk hand and invite 3-bets from Elon. Plan: complete to 100, proceed cautiously on the flop and fold to significant aggression unless I connect.

Pre-flop in the big blind with Qs9s, a suited queen-high hand with good playability. Facing a small blind limp from Minni after Flash folded, the pot is 200 and I've already posted 100 with no further call required. This gives excellent implied odds to see a flop heads-up against one opponent. Q9s performs well multiway or heads-up post-flop due to straight and flush potential. Raising could build the pot but risks isolation with a marginal hand against a limper who might have traps; checking realizes equity cheaply while maintaining position post-flop. Deep stacks support speculative play.

I completed to 100 preflop with 7-3 offsuit as a cheap speculative call. The flop J♥ 8♦ 2♠ completely misses me — no pair, no meaningful draws (only one heart in my hand/board so a flush is impossible by river), and no straight possibilities. Pot is only 200 and I have position, so the lowest-variance, highest-expected-value line is to check and concede to serious aggression. If Elon checks back I get a free turn to reevaluate; if he fires a bet I'll fold to any reasonably sized continuation. I won’t bloat the pot with junk or bluff-raise out of position. Action: check.

Flop board J♥8♦2♠ is dry and uncoordinated, favoring my queen-high showdown value as likely best or close against Minni's SB limp-check range, which includes many weak hands, small pairs below J, ace-high, and draws. No strong draws completed (only backdoor straights/flushes), so betting protects equity and denies equity to worse hands like 76, A-high. Positionally strong heads-up with deep stacks (SPR ~34), a small bet builds pot modestly while maintaining fold equity against junk. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards/draws and missing value from floats. Betting 150 (75% pot) balances value, protection, and pot control.

I completed to 100 preflop with 7♠3♥ intending to check the flop and fold to meaningful aggression. The flop J♥ 8♦ 2♠ completely misses me — no pair, virtually no realistic draws (only a backdoor heart, very weak). Elon bets 150 into a 350 pot; calling 150 to win 500 requires ~30% equity (150/500). 7‑3o has far less equity versus any reasonable c-bet/continuation range (single digits to low teens) and offers no strong fold equity or blockers. I don’t want to invest more with junk and risk multi-street pressure, so I’ll fold and wait for a better spot.

3♥9♣ is a very weak offsuit hand. Although I'm on the button and could attempt a steal, this hand is frequently dominated and plays poorly post‑flop. Limping (calling 100) is suboptimal because it invites raises and isolates me with a bad holding; open‑raising to the usual 200–300 would be thin and vulnerable to a defend or 3‑bet from the SB (Elon, ~70bb) or the deep BB (Flash). Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold preflop.

AK suited is one of the best hands in poker, especially heads-up against the big blind after the button folds. From the small blind, I want to raise for value, build the pot with a strong hand, and take the initiative while out of position post-flop. Standard raise size is around 3x the big blind (300 total). My stack is 70BB deep, opponent's is deeper, so no need for larger sizing. Folding or limping would be a mistake with this hand.

I am in the Big Blind with JTs, which is a strong starting hand. Elon raised to 300 from the Small Blind, and Minni folded. Elon has a shorter stack (67.25 BB) compared to the rest of the table. JTs plays very well post-flop, and calling is a standard and profitable play here. Raising is also an option, but given Elon's stack size and position, calling preserves the pot size while allowing me to utilize my positional advantage and hand equity across various flop textures.

AK suited flops ace-high on a dry, paired 6-6-5 rainbow board. I have no pair, no straight draw, and only backdoor flush draw. Villain's BB calling range pre-flop includes many hands that connect with this board like pocket pairs (including 66,55), 45,34,76, suited connectors. OOP, c-betting polarizes my range and risks stacking off to better hands while folding out better ace-highs. Checking controls the pot, realizes equity (ace-high often good), and allows villain to bluff or check behind weaker hands. Pot 600, stacks deep (70+BB), no need to risk chips now. Plan to call small bets, fold to big ones, or raise villains' weak c-bets.

I have JTs on a flop of 5s-6d-6h. My opponent, Elon, raised from the small blind pre-flop and has now checked to me. This board texture is likely better for my range as the big blind defender, as I have more 6x combinations and connected cards. Elon's check could indicate a weak hand or an attempt to trap, but given the dry nature of the board, I want to take the initiative and test his hand strength. A small bet can often take the pot down here or allow me to see where I stand while maintaining control over the hand with two overcards and a backdoor spade draw.

Facing a small check-raise (300 into 600) from Flash in the BB on a paired 6-6-5 rainbow flop. AK suited clubs holds ace-high with king blocker, decent showdown value OOP. Flash's BB calling range vs SB raise includes suited connectors (76s/45s for str draws, 65 for two pair), pocket pairs below sixes, weaker Ax, and bluffs. Small sizing suggests thin value, draws, or probes rather than nuts (66/55/boats would bet larger). Calling realizes equity against better bluffs/draws/worse aces, keeps pot small OOP with deep stacks (~70BB effective). Plan: proceed cautiously turn (call small/raise weakness/fold improvement); folding too tight as ace-high frequently best; raising risks vs monsters OOP.

On the turn, the board is 5s-6d-6h-7s, pairing the sixes with an open-ended straight draw completer (4x or 8x makes straight) and adding a spade flush draw possibility. I hold AcKc for ace-high with no pair, no straight draw, and only a weak backdoor club draw. Pot is 1200, effective stacks deep (~60BB). Acting first OOP with marginal showdown value. Flop check-raise by Flash (BB calling SB raise) was small (300 into 600), suggesting thin value (trips 66, two-pair 76/65), draws (45,78, spade flush draws), or probes rather than monsters (full house unlikely). Turn 7s improves some of villain's draws/straight possibilities, so checking induces bluffs from missed draws, gets value from worse (Ax, weaker pairs), and controls pot size OOP. Betting risks folding out worse hands while getting raised by better; folding impossible as ace-high often best. Plan post-check: call small/medium bets with ace-high, fold to large overbets or obvious value; raise only obvious weakness. Long-term EV positive to realize equity cautiously in deep-stack spot.

On the turn 7s, the board texture is now 5s-6d-6h-7s. I have picked up a flush draw with my JTs to go along with my two overcards. Elon has checked to me for the second time after calling my flop bet. While I could continue to barrel here to put pressure on his likely medium-strength range (like small pocket pairs or Ace-high), checking back is a very strong play in position. It allows me to realize my significant equity (9 outs to a flush, plus potentially overcards) for free. If I hit my flush or an overcard on the river, I can still value bet, and by checking, I avoid the risk of being check-raised and forced to fold my draw or commit too many chips with a semi-bluff. This keeps the pot controlled and utilizes my positional advantage to see all five cards.

River pairs the fives (5s-6d-6h-7s-5d), creating a highly coordinated board with two pairs on the board (fives and sixes full possible with 7x/65, trips with 6x/5x/7x, straights with 48/45/78). AcKc holds pure ace-high with no pair, straight (missed), or flush draw realization. OOP facing BB (Flash) who small-bet flop (likely thin value/draws), checked turn (possibly trapping medium strength or giving up), and deep effective stacks (~60BB). Bluffing risks folding out worse (missed draws like spades flush/OESD) while getting called/raised by all showdown value (pairs+). Value betting impossible with ace-high capped. Checking maximizes EV by realizing equity OOP: induces bluffs from air/draws, gets free showdown vs worse Ax/king-high/draws, controls pot OOP, and avoids difficult spots. Plan: check, call small-medium bets (worse Ax/draws), fold large/overbets (value). Consistent with prior cautious play with ace-high multiway/coordinated boards in deep-stack spot.

The river card is the 5 of diamonds, making the final board 5s-6d-6h-7s-5d. The board is now double-paired (6s and 5s) with a 7 kicker. My hand, JTs, plays as two pair (66 and 55) with a Jack kicker (6-6-5-5-J). This actually gives me showdown value against any small pocket pairs Elon might have held like 22, 33, or 44, because those hands are now counterfeited by the board—their best five-card hand would be the board's two pair with the 7 kicker (6-6-5-5-7). However, I still lose to any Ace, King, or Queen high, and any pocket pair 88 or higher. Given that Elon checked the river after checking the turn, he likely has a marginal hand. Since I have some showdown value and my hand is unlikely to fold out many better hands (like Ace-high or a mid-pair) with a standard bet, checking back is the most prudent play to realize my equity and reach showdown for free.

A♥8♥ is a strong suited Ace from the button in a 50/100 game. Excellent position allows us to raise wide for value and steal blinds. This hand plays well post-flop with flush draw potential, straight draws, and Ace blocker. Opponents Flash (SB) and Minni (BB) defend frequently based on history, but we have fold equity and position. Standard raise to 3x BB (300) to build pot and take initiative while keeping SPR reasonable with deep stacks.

Elon has opened from the button, and I am in the Small Blind with Q9o. While this hand has some value in 3-handed play, it performs poorly out of position, especially against an aggressive button range. Calling creates a difficult post-flop scenario and may invite the Big Blind (Minni) to squeeze or call, further complicating the hand. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack for better opportunities where I have either position or a stronger starting hand.

Call to 300. 9♠8♦ is a marginal but playable hand in the big blind against a button open to 3BB. The pot is 450 and calling 200 more (total commitment 300) gives ~31% pot odds; versus a likely wide BTN opening range and ~73BB effective stacks I have adequate equity and good post‑flop playability (straight/two‑pair prospects). I prefer to avoid inflating the pot out of position with an offsuit connector by 3‑betting; I'll defend and evaluate on the flop — fold to heavy action if I miss, continue/raise when I connect or see favorable opportunities.

I have middle pair (9s) with a weak kicker on J♠ 9♥ 3♥. Preflop Elon opened wide from the button, so his range includes many Jx, overcards and bluff candidates. He hasn't bet the flop, which looks like weakness or pot-control rather than a strong value shove. I'm out of position and don't want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand that is vulnerable to Jx, heart draws and overcards. Checking lets me realize equity, keep his bluffs in, and avoid difficult turn decisions. Plan: check and call a reasonable bet on the turn for value/protection; fold to heavy aggression or a large check-raise. If he checks back, re-evaluate on the turn (bet turn if I improve or the card is favorable).

Flop of Js9h3h is great for my Ah8h: nut flush draw (9 outs), Ace high blocker, backdoor straight potential. I raised pre-flop from button, Minni called from BB and checked, representing weakness or traps but likely missed high cards/draws given history of calling stations but folding to aggression. Deep stacks (SPR ~11), position advantage. Semi-bluff bet for value vs weaker draws/A-high, protection, and fold equity vs overcards/pairs below J. Size ~2/3 pot to build while denying equity. Pot 650, bet 450 keeps SPR playable post-flop if called.

I have middle pair (9s) with a weak 8 kicker on Js 9h 3h. Elon opened wide from the button and is c-betting a medium size (450 into a 1100 pot), so his range contains a lot of Jx, overcards and bluffs. Calling 450 gives me ~29% pot odds (450/(1100+450)) and vs his wide range my hand has adequate equity to continue — I beat many overcards and some bluffs and can realize equity OOP. I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position with a marginal hand or turn it into a big commitment, so I’ll call and re-evaluate on the turn: fold to a large raise or shove, but continue/control the pot if the turn is a blank or improves me.

Check. The A♣ on the turn is a bad card for my marginal middle pair (9♠8♦) — it significantly improves the button’s range (many A‑x combos) and I’m out of position. Leading here would bloat the pot with a vulnerable hand and weak kicker, and I don’t have fold equity to make a bluff profitable. Checking keeps the pot controlled, lets me realize equity, and allows me to call a reasonable bet to fold to a big raise. Plan: check and call small/medium bets to see the river; fold to heavy turn/river aggression; if Elon checks back I’ll re‑evaluate for a thin value bet on a safe river or check down.

The turn Ac is fantastic for my range: it pairs my Ah for top pair top kicker (effectively, given board), while preserving the nut flush draw (9 hearts out). Equity is crushing (~70%+ against typical calling ranges like mid pairs, weaker Ax, missed draws). Minni checked flop/call then check turn, showing possible medium strength (J, 9, draw) but unlikely nuts given history. As button raiser, my range is nutted here (sets, two-pair, strong Ax). Value bet to extract from worse: weaker pairs, Ax with bad kicker, stubborn draws. Size 1000 (~65% pot) balances value/protection, keeps SPR ~2.5 if called, allowing river shove if needed. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or check-raise bluffs.

Call 1000. I have middle pair (9♠) with a weak kicker on Js 9h 3h Ac, out of position — not a hand I want to turn into a bluff/raise. Elon opened wide from the button and c‑bet the flop, so his continuing range contains a mix of value (Jx, sets, some two‑pair) and a fair number of bluffs/missed draws and worse pairs. The turn A♣ does help a lot of his combos, but the price is reasonable: calling 1000 into a 2550 pot gives ~28% pot odds (1000/(2550+1000) ≈ 28%). My pair has enough equity versus his continuing range to make a call + see the river profitable, and my stack (16.6k) can comfortably absorb this small investment while preserving post‑river flexibility. Raising OOP with this hand is too thin and would create difficult commit decisions against a player who has shown aggression. Plan: call the 1000, re‑evaluate on the river — fold to a large shove or polarizing overbet if I don’t improve; call or thin‑value bet small on a safe river if he shows weakness.

Elon checked the river after betting the flop and turn. The A on the turn heavily improves his continuing range (lots of A‑x and J‑x combinations), and his river check looks like pot control with medium strength or missed draws rather than a bluffing polar range. I hold second pair (9♠) with a weak kicker — too thin to build a pot out of position or to bluff-send. A small thin value bet will often fold out the hands I beat and get called by hands that already beat me (Ax, Jx, sets), and a larger bet risks a painful raise/all‑in from value. Conserving chips and realizing my showdown equity is the higher EV play here. I will check and take it to showdown rather than bluffing or bloating the pot OOP.

River 7c is a relatively blank card that doesn't complete many draws (flush draw missed entirely for hearts, no straight obvious). I have top pair top kicker (Ah pairs the Ac), which is very strong on this board. Minni's line: limp/call pre, check/call flop on drawy board, check/call turn after Ac (which improves my hand significantly), check river. Her range after two calls likely includes weaker Ax (worse kicker), Jx, 9x, mid pairs like 1010/88/66/44, or missed draws that can't fold. History shows Minni as a calling station who pays off with medium strength (e.g., many hands where she calls down rivers). Checking would allow her to realize equity for free or bluff catch, but with position and range advantage, a value bet extracts max value from worse hands. Pot 3550, SPR low (~1.6 with my stack), bet ~2/3 pot (2200) sizes well: gets called by worse often enough while shoving risks folding out Jx/Ax bad kicker. Balances with any bluffs in range but primarily thin value.

I checked the river and Elon fired 2,200 into a 5,750 pot. I hold second pair (9♠) with a weak kicker on J♠ 9♥ 3♥ Ac 7♣. Calling 2,200 requires ~28% equity (2,200 / (5,750+2,200) ≈ 27.7%). Elon’s line — button open, flop c-bet, a large turn bet and now a river bet — is strongly skewed toward value (Jx, Ax, two‑pair/sets) after the turn Ace. Missed heart draws and other bluffs exist, but the turn Ace substantially increases his A‑x combos and reduces bluff frequency, so I don’t expect enough bluffs to make a call +EV. Raising as a bluff OOP here is thin and will often get called by better hands or fold only the weakest holdings; thin‑value bets aren’t available from my side. My estimate of my hand’s equity vs his continuing range is below the break‑even threshold, so the +EV play is to fold and preserve chips for better spots.

I am on the button with A6s, which is a premium starting hand in a 3-handed game. My stack is healthy at over 150 big blinds. Generally, I should be raising almost any Ace from the button to put pressure on the blinds. A standard raise size here is 3x the big blind, which is 300. This allows me to take control of the hand and gain initiative for post-flop play.

Fold. In the small blind holding Td2d facing a standard 3bb open to 300 from the button, this is a very weak, easily-dominated hand with poor postflop playability out of position. Calling 250 into a 450 pot requires ~36% equity heads-up; versus a button opening range Td2d is well below that and becomes even worse if the big blind (Elon) defends and it goes multiway. A 3-bet bluff here is too spewy and unnecessary with deep stacks and plenty of better spots to pick. Preserve the stack and wait for a stronger, positionally-favourable hand.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise to 300 (3x) after SB folds. Pot is 450, calling 200 more for total pot of 650, offering ~3.25:1 odds. A4o is a marginal defending hand with Ace blocker, decent equity (~38-42%) against a wide button opening range (~40-50% of hands based on history where Flash raises frequently from late position). Postflop playability is okay heads-up with positionally disadvantaged but Ace-high potential to take stabs. Folding is too tight for modern BB defense vs button; 3-betting A4o lacks strong blocker value and poor playability if called. Calling realizes equity, controls pot size, and utilizes dead SB money. Flash is aggressive preflop but check-folds sometimes postflop per history.

On the flop of 2c Ts Ks as OOP in BB with A4o (ace-high, no pair, no draw). Board is draw-heavy with spades flush draw and straight draws (QJ, KJ, etc.). My hand has minimal showdown value and poor equity against Flash's wide button opening range, which often includes Kx, Tx, suited connectors, and bluffs. Preflop call was for thin value/blocker, but postflop with no improvement, OOP, check-folding is standard to avoid realizing equity OOP or bloating pot. Flash likely cbets frequently here (aggressive history). No reason to lead or donk bet air.

The board is Ks-Ts-2c, which is quite heavy with broadway cards that connect well with a Big Blind's calling range (hands like QJ, JT, QT, or smaller Kings). Since I have Ace-high with a backdoor flush draw, checking back is a safe play. It keeps the pot small, avoids a potential check-raise on a board that hits my opponent's range, and allows me to see a turn card for free to see if I improve or if the board texture becomes better for a bluff later.

On the turn of Jh to a K♠ T♠ 2♣ J♠ board, holding A♥ 4♠ (ace-high, no pair, no straight draw, backdoor spade). This is a highly coordinated board with broadway straight draws completed (e.g., Qx makes straight), two spades for flush draw, and high cards that smash Flash's frequent button opening range (Kx, Tx, Jx, suited broadways/connectors like QJs, KQs, ATs). Preflop defense with A4o was marginal for odds, and flop check-check kept pot small. Flash's flop check behind could be weakness (air, missed draws) or trap/induction, but his aggressive history suggests cbets often on dry boards, so check here is cautious. My ace-high has ~10-20% equity OOP against his range now, low showdown value, and betting as bluff risks folding out better (e.g., missed draws) while getting called by all made hands (pairs+, straights). Checking realizes equity for free, induces bluffs/mistakes from his wide range, and allows check-fold to turn bet profitably given pot odds/depth. No value bet, and positionally disadvantaged OOP. Prioritizing pot control and avoiding tough rivers on scary board to preserve stack long-term.

On the turn, the board has become quite coordinated (K-T-2-J). This Jack connects very well with the Big Blind's calling range, completing straight draws like AQ (which I block with my Ace) and making two-pair combinations like KT, KJ, or TJ. Since I only have Ace-high and Elon has checked to me twice, I have some showdown value but limited fold equity if I bet into this range. Checking back again allows me to see a river for free and potentially realize my showdown value or improve to a straight if a Queen peels on the river.

River K♣ pairs the kings on a highly coordinated board (K♠ T♠ J♥ 2♣ K♣), completing straights (e.g., Q9s, QTs), flush draws (spades), and improving Kx to trips or better, Tx to two-pair. Holding A♥4♠ (ace-high, no pair/draw), equity is low OOP against Flash's button range post-check-check-check (preflop raise, flop/turn checks suggest missed draws, weak Kx/A-high, or traps, but his aggression history favors weakness here as he'd often c-bet flop). Preflop defense was thin for odds; postflop pot control preserved stack. Betting ace-high bluff risks ~10-15bb into 650 pot OOP, folding out worse while called by all value (trips+, straights); poor EV given range disadvantage and position. Checking maximizes EV: induces bluffs/value mistakes from his wide range (e.g., missed spades/Jx), wins at showdown vs air/draws (~20-30% vs polarized range), and check-folds profitably to bets (pot odds poor for hero-call with 1 blocker). Long-term stack preservation prioritizes over thin bluff value on scary paired river OOP vs aggressive opponent who checked behind twice.

I have Ace-high on a board of K-T-2-J-K. The action has gone check-check on every street after the pre-flop raise. My opponent has checked to me for the third time on the river. While the board is quite coordinated, Ace-high has significant showdown value in a small pot that has been checked down. There is little value in betting because better hands like a Jack or a Ten are unlikely to fold, and worse hands will not call. Checking back allows me to realize my showdown value and potentially win the pot against missed draws or lower pocket pairs.

I'm on the button with J♣8♦ — a marginal but playable offsuit hand in 3-handed play where position is a big advantage. Blinds are 50/100 so the pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop. With both blinds to act behind me I should be opening a fairly wide range to steal the blinds and play postflop in position. A standard 3xBB open (to 300) is my choice: it achieves good fold equity, keeps sizing consistent with the table (many players open to 300 here), and avoids inviting cheap multiway flops that reduce my positional edge. Against opponents' tendencies: Elon (SB) has shown aggression and can 3-bet — if he 3-bets large (a sizable 3-bet to ~900+), I will fold J8o; against a smaller isolation 3-bet I may re-evaluate for a call depending on sizing and who cold-called. If I'm called, I'll use position to c-bet favorable flops and control pot size on dry boards. So raise to 300 to apply pressure and play the hand in position.

K6o is a marginal offsuit hand from the small blind facing a button raise. Minni's range is wide but strong enough that K6o lacks the equity and playability out of position postflop. Pot odds are not sufficient to justify calling, and raising is off-table due to hand strength. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Five offsuit. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. With an Ace in my hand and being in a 3-handed situation, this is a standard defend. While A5o doesn't have great post-flop playability compared to suited connectors, its high-card value makes it too strong to fold against a button open. I will call to see a flop and protect my blind.

On this flop of 8h-7s-2h, I have Ace-high with a backdoor nut flush draw. Since I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor (Minni), the standard play is to check to the raiser and see how they respond. Leading out (donking) into the button range doesn't provide much utility here, as I want to keep the pot small and potentially realize my equity or bluff-catch later if the board develops favorably.

I have top pair (8) with a decent J kicker on a fairly dry flop (8♥7♠2♥). Flash defended a wide preflop range by calling, so he has many worse pairs, occasional floats and some backdoor heart combos — not many strong immediate draws. In position I want to extract value and deny equity to overcards/backdoor draws while keeping worse hands and some bluffs in the pot. Pot is 650, so I’ll bet ~62% pot (400). If called I’ll continue to control the pot and re-evaluate on the turn; if I face a large check-raise I’ll be cautious and likely fold to heavy aggression.

On a flop of 8h-7s-2h, my Ace-Five offsuit has very little connectivity. Although I have the Ace of hearts, providing a backdoor nut flush draw, I am out of position against Minni, who opened from the button and is now continuation-betting for about 61% of the pot. Against this sizing, continuing with just Ace-high and no direct draw is generally a losing play in the long run, as I will be forced to fold to most turn barrels unless I improve to an Ace. Given the board texture and my lack of equity, I will fold and wait for a better spot.

3-handed on the button facing a big blind after SB folded, creating dead money in the pot (150 total). 9d3c is a marginal hand but button stealing range vs BB is very wide (50-70% typically). Excellent position post-flop if called. Minni has defended wide in past hands but also folded to button aggression frequently (e.g., Hand 32, 44, 51). Raising to 300 (3x effective) offers good fold equity (~60%+ est.), stealing 150 dead money frequently. If called, ~35% equity vs wide BB calling range, and position to realize it. EV positive long-term. Deep stacks allow maneuvering post-flop.

I have been dealt Pocket Aces (Ad Ac), the strongest starting hand in Texas Hold'em. I am in the Small Blind. The action is currently on me after Elon (Button) raised to 300. With a stack of 14,700 and Elon having 9,100, I want to build a large pot immediately to extract value. A standard 3-bet from the SB vs a Button open should be around 3.5x to 4x the open size plus considering the blinds. I will raise to 1,000 to maximize value and put pressure on Elon's range while also potentially getting Minni (Big Blind) to commit chips if she has a playable hand.

Facing an open to 300 from the Button and a 3-bet to 1000 from the SB. Pot is 1400 and it costs 900 to call. I have K♣3♠ — a weak, easily dominated hand with poor postflop playability. I would need ~39% equity just to break even on a call preflop, and in a 3-way pot out of position against both opponents K3o performs poorly. A 4-bet bluff here would commit a lot of chips with almost no blockers and is unnecessary with deep stacks and many better spots ahead. Fold and preserve the stack for better opportunities.

Facing a 3-bet to 1000 from SB after my button open to 300 with 9d3c, a speculative offsuit gapped hand with minimal playability. SB 3-bet range is strong (premium pairs, AK/AQ, some broadways/bluffs), where 93o has ~25-30% equity at best, not justifying a call for 700 into 2100 pot (~23% needed, but poor postflop playability even in position). Raising adds dead money but risks 4-bet shove or call with dominated range. History shows Flash aggressive but defends/3-bets strong spots (e.g., Hand 37 river bluff fold, but preflop solid). Deep stacks, better to fold marginal steal attempt and wait for stronger hands/positions. EV-maximizing fold preserves stack for 89 hands left.

I am on the button with 9c 6h. This is a relatively weak hand, but I am in position. Minni and Elon are the blinds. Looking at the previous hands, there's been a lot of aggressive pre-flop play. While I could potentially steal here with a raise, my hand doesn't have much connectivity or high-card value. Given the stack sizes and the tendency of opponents to call or 3-bet, folding is the most prudent long-term play for such a marginal hand.

Heads-up from the small blind with 9♦8♦ — this is a strong, flexible hand in position postflop. I want to take the initiative, build a pot when I have position and fold equity, and deny Elon cheap access to flops. A standard open to 3x the big blind (300 total) accomplishes that: it gives fold equity against his weaker holdings, keeps the pot manageable versus his stronger 3-bets, and leaves plenty of stack behind to realize equity if called (I have 157bb, he has ~90bb). Plan: c-bet on favorable and wet boards, barrel selectively on turns, and respect large 3-bets (fold to very large 3-bets, call or play postflop against medium ones). Raising to 300 is the +EV line here.

64o is a complete trash hand with no playability, low cards, offsuit, disconnected. Minni raised from SB after button fold, likely a wide aggressive range but still much stronger than mine on average. Pot odds 200 to call into 400 (3:1) but equity too low post-flop, no fold equity on raise, stack preservation priority in long game. Standard fold.

3-handed on the button with Q♣8♣ — a playable, suited hand that plays very well in position. Pot is only 150 and to call is 100, so I want the initiative and to pressure the blinds. Open-raising here isolates the hand, gives me position postflop and folds out many weak blind holdings. I'll use a standard 3x BB size (to 300) — big enough to gain fold equity but not so big that I invite large 3-bets unnecessarily. Plan: if both blinds fold I take the pot; if I'm called I’ll play cautiously postflop with position and look for c-bet opportunities or draws; if I face a large 3-bet (size that commits a big portion of effective stacks) I’ll fold Q8s out of position.

Preflop in SB with 6c2s, a complete trash hand with no high card value, connectivity, or suitedness. Facing a button raise to 300 (standard open size), which is a wide range from Minni. Pot is 450, to call 250 gives about 2:1 odds, but equity realization OOP with this hand is negligible against any reasonable range. No fold equity on a 3-bet bluff here with this holding. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Long-term EV positive decision.

I am in the Big Blind with Jack-Five offsuit. Minni, who has been quite aggressive from late positions, has raised to 300 from the Button. While the pot odds are roughly 2.25:1, J5o is a very weak hand that plays poorly post-flop, especially out of position. Against an aggressive opponent like Minni, it is better to wait for a stronger hand to defend the blinds rather than calling with speculative trash and potentially losing more chips on later streets.

2c7c is a trash hand even though suited. Poor high card strength, large gap between ranks limits straight potential, and flush requires help. Position on button allows wide range, but in 3-handed with deep stacks (90BB+), entering voluntarily with bottom-of-range hand risks difficult postflop spots. Opponents from history are aggressive raisers frequently, so blinds likely to fight back. EV better folding and waiting for playable hands to maximize long-term stack growth over 150 hands. Standard fold.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Four of spades, which is a very strong hand for a blind-versus-blind situation. Since Elon has folded, it is just me and Minni in the Big Blind. I will raise to 300 to take control of the pot and apply pressure on the Big Blind, as this hand has significant value and equity.

I’m in the big blind with 4♣5♥ facing a 3x SB open to 300. I need to call 200 into a 400 pot (pot odds ~3:1, ~25% required equity), but this is out-of-position postflop and 4-5 offsuit has very poor playability and few blockers. Although Flash opens a lot, this particular hand is too weak to defend OOP — calling invites awkward turns/river decisions and 3-betting as a bluff is thin with no fold equity or equity to back it up. With a 15.9k stack I’m better preserving chips and waiting for a more playable spot.

I am on the button with 3d2d. While these are suited connectors, they are at the very bottom of the range and are generally too weak to play, especially against players like Minni who have been quite active and aggressive pre-flop. Folding is the most disciplined and long-term profitable move here to preserve my stack.

We're heads-up after the button folded. As SB I act first pre-flop but will have positional advantage post-flop — that's ideal to open a wide range. Q♦4♥ is marginal, but in heads-up it's in my raising range to seize initiative and pick up the blind or play flops in position. I want a standard, meaningful open size that gives fold equity but keeps pots manageable versus Elon's stack, so I'll open to 3x the big blind (300 total). Plan: c-bet favorable flops and use position; fold to a large 3-bet — call small resistance and play postflop when appropriate.

In the Big Blind facing a standard 3x raise from the Small Blind after Button folds, the pot is 400 and calling costs 200 more (pot odds ~3:1, requiring ~25% equity). 2h5h is a suited connector with flush and straight potential (gutshot wheels, open-enders possible). SB ranges are wide in this spot, including many Ax suited, broadways, pairs, and suited hands. Defending wide OOP vs SB is standard due to position advantage they have but our discount. Good implied odds deep-stacked if we hit draws. Not strong enough to 3-bet, folding offsuit version but suited justifies calling to realize equity and control pot size preflop. History shows Minni raises frequently, so range polarized but includes speculative hands.

I have Q♦4♥ on K♦ Q♠ 5♣ — top pair with a weak kicker. I was the preflop aggressor so continuing to take the initiative in position is standard. The flop is fairly dry (rainbow, few draws), so a c-bet will get value from worse Qx and many pocket pairs while folding out small pairs and overcards that missed. I’ll size modestly to preserve stack and keep villain’s calling range wide: bet ~1/2 pot (300 into 600). If called I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn (value-bet thin vs passive calls, fold to heavy aggression on scary cards), and if Elon raises big here I’ll respect strength and likely fold given my weak kicker.

Facing a continuation bet of 300 into 900 from the aggressive Small Blind on a K♦ Q♠ 5♣ flop with 2♥5♥ for bottom pair, deuce kicker, and only a backdoor heart flush draw. Out of position, villain's wide preflop raising range (frequent from history) hits this board often with top pair (Kx, Qx), overpairs, Ax, sets, or draws. My hand has weak showdown value, vulnerable to any overcard turn/river, and limited implied odds due to reverse implied if they have better 5x or pairs. Pot odds ~3:1 require ~25% equity, but realized equity lower OOP against their strong continuing range. Folding is disciplined to avoid tough multi-street spots and preserve stack for superior hands.

Button with A♣6♠ — a hand with ace-high value and good postflop playability in position. I want to take the initiative and avoid limp/call which surrenders fold equity. A standard 3x open to 300 is sized to steal the blinds and avoid inviting cheap multiway callers or easy 3-bets that a min-raise would. If I face a small 3-bet I can call and play postflop in position; fold or re-evaluate vs a large 3-bet. Plan: c-bet favorable ace-type flops, control pot if unimproved, and use position to leverage bluffs/value.

9d2s is one of the worst possible starting hands: low, offsuit, no straight or flush potential. Facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB), pot is 450, calling 250 more from SB (out of position postflop). Pot odds are about 1.8:1, but equity against any reasonable raising range is poor (~20-30% vs wide button range, worse vs tighter). Deep stacks mean postflop playability is crucial, and this hand plays terribly OOP. Folding preserves stack for premium spots. No reads suggest Minni is raising super wide enough to justify a call or 3bet bluff here.

I am in the Big Blind with T4o. Minni has raised from the button to 3BB (300 total). T4o is a very weak hand and does not play well out of position. Given the stack depths and my position, there is no need to defend such a weak range. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

3-handed on the button facing an unraised pot (blinds posted, pot 150, to call 100). My hand 4h2h is suited with wheel straight potential (A5432) and flush draw equity. In short-handed play, button open-raises a very wide range (~50-70% of hands) due to position and fold equity against blinds. Opponents have large stacks but history shows they fold to raises often. Raising builds the pot when I hit or steal blinds. Limp or call too passive; fold too tight. Standard size 3x BB.

I am in the Small Blind with K2s. Elon opened to 300 from the button. While K2s has some post-flop playability and equity against a wide button opening range, playing out of position against Elon is disadvantageous. Given the 150-hand match format and my healthy stack, I prefer to fold marginal hands from the Small Blind rather than calling and being forced to play out of position on most boards.

I’m in the big blind with Q♠7♥ facing a button open to 300. The pot is 450 and I need to commit 200 more (300 total) which gives me ~31% break-even equity (200 into a 650 pot). Elon on the button has been opening frequently and is conceivably wide—so Q7o is worth defending from the BB to realize equity and avoid being too exploitable. I don’t want to 3-bet OOP with a marginal hand (it will get called or rebuffed by stronger hands) and folding is too tight versus a likely steal. Plan: call to 300 total (200 more) to see the flop; proceed postflop only with top pair, two pair/set, or strong draws and fold to heavy pressure on dry, unfavorable boards.

Flop: 8♥ 3♣ 6♦. I have Q♠7♥ — no pair but I have a real hand: an open-ended straight draw (7 with 6 and 8 needs a 5 or 9 — 8 outs, ~31.5% to hit by river) and an overcard (Q) that can still improve. Pot is 650 and I'm OOP. Per my preflop plan I only continue with top pair/two pair/sets or strong draws — this is a strong draw, but I want to control the size of the pot OOP.

Line: check. Checking achieves pot control, lets Elon barrel worse into me (I can call profitable small bets given my equity) and gives a free or cheap turn card if he checks back. If Elon bets small (roughly ≤1/3 pot, ~200) I will call — calling 200 into an effective pot of 850 needs ~23.5% equity and my draw (~31%) is +EV. If he bets ~300 (~1/2 pot) I'm near break-even (~31%) and will decide based on his tendencies; I will fold to large/overwhelming pressure (>~300–350) unless I have a credible read or strong implied odds. If I improve to a pair or the straight on the turn I will play aggressively.

Heads-up vs Minni on a dry flop 8h3c6d (gutshot board). My 4h2h has a gutshot straight draw to 5 (23456) plus backdoor flush draw, giving ~10-15% equity vs her BB calling range. Pot 650, position on button with wide PFR range that hits this board decently (pairs 6-8, suited connectors like 75s/54s for str8 draws). Minni checks, representing weakness; history shows she calls PF but folds flops to aggression often (e.g., hands 14,18,41,42). Standard to c-bet ~60% pot for value/protection/fold equity, deny her equity on draws/pairs. Check back too passive, realizes 0 on bluffs. Size 400 balances range.

I checked the flop with Q♠7♥ and have a real hand: an open-ended straight draw to 5/9 (8 clean outs) plus the overcard Q (3 additional possible outs that can make a top pair in some spots). That gives me meaningful equity vs a wide c-bet range; even conservatively using the straight-only equity (~31%) I get enough to continue because calling 400 into a pot of 1050 (pot after my call will be 1450) requires only ~27.6% to break even. Elon has been opening and c-betting frequently, so I expect a lot of his range to be c-bets I can realize equity against. Raising OOP would bloat the pot with a draw and is unnecessary; folding is too tight given the pot odds and our implied odds with deeper stacks. Plan: call to see the turn, fold to large turn pressure if we miss, and play aggressively if we pair the Q or make the straight.

Turn summary: I hold Q♠7♥ on 8♥ 3♣ 6♦ 8♣. I have an OESD to 5/9 (8 clean outs, ~31% to hit the straight by the river) plus an overcard. Elon c-bet the flop and then checked the turn — a clear sign of weakness or a capped range. Pot is 1,450 and action is on me. Decision: semi-bluff. A half‑pot bet pressures his weak checking hands and still leaves me good equity if called. A 700 bet requires ~32.6% fold frequency to be immediately profitable; paired with my ~31% chance to make the straight if called, this is +EV versus a wide checking range. Plan: bet 700 as a semi-bluff to take the pot now or realize equity if called. If Elon check‑raises or makes a large raise, I’ll give up (he’s representing a very strong made hand — 8x/trips/sets). If he calls, I’ll evaluate the river and proceed aggressively only with the completed straight or a clear pair of the queen; otherwise I’ll protect the stack and fold to heavy river aggression.

Facing a turn lead-raise to 700 into 1450 after Minni check-called my flop c-bet. Board pairs 8s (8h/8c 3c6d), rainbow-ish but clubs now. My 4h2h has a weak gutshot (5 for 23456 straight, ~9% equity if clean runout) + backdoor heart flush draw, but minimal showdown value. Minni's range for check-call flop includes pairs (77-TT,86/87/68 trips possible now), sets (88 quads, 33/66 full), or draws (45/57/79); her turn donk is strong, repping trips+ or improved draws. Pot odds 2150:700 (~3:1) require ~23% equity, but vs her polarized range we have <15% (blocker to weak 2x/4x). History shows Minni calls stations but turns weak hands into bluffs less often here. Raising escalates with air/draw; calling bloats pot OOP next street OOP for her but poor implied odds. Preflop wide button open + flop cbet reps value (86s/87s/88/67s hit hard), but folding marginals to resistance exploits her strength. Stack preservation key in cash game marathon (67/150 hands).

I am on the button with 7s 8d. In a 3-handed game, opening a wide range from the button is standard strategy to apply pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot. Although 87o is a marginal hand, it has reasonable playability post-flop in position. I will raise to 300 to attempt to steal the blinds or play a pot in position against Minni or Elon.

AH KD in the small blind vs a standard 3bb button open to 300 — this is a premium hand and I don't want to just cold-call OOP against two players. 3-betting isolates the opener, gets fold equity, and builds the pot when I have a heads‑up equity advantage. I’ll make a standard 3‑bet to 900: large enough to deny correct odds to many speculative hands and to define ranges, but small enough to keep weaker calling ranges and to leave room to fold/re-evaluate if I face a big 4‑bet. If the big blind calls, I can proceed OOP with a top‑range hand; if Flash 4‑bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and stack dynamics.

Facing a button open-raise to 300 from Flash, who opens wide from that position, and a 3-bet to 900 from Minni in the small blind, who has shown frequent aggression. My holding is ThJs offsuit, a marginal broadway hand with poor playability post-flop, especially multiway or against a strong 3-bet range. Pot odds to call 800 into 1300 require ~38% equity, but my hand has limited equity against Minni's polarized 3-bet range (value like QQ+, AK, or bluffs like suited connectors/Ax), and Flash may fold or call with a wide range. Folding preserves my 78BB stack for better spots; no reason to speculate with a hand that dominates few calling ranges and plays poorly OOP.

I opened with 7s 8d from the button as a standard steal/positional play. Minni responded with a 3-bet to 900. While I am in position, 87o is too weak a hand to continue against a 3-bet, especially given Minni's shown aggression in previous hands. Calling would put me in a difficult spot post-flop with a low-equity hand against a range that likely dominates me. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack.

Button with A♦6♥ — a clear open-raise spot in a 3-handed pot. I have position on both blinds, an ace blocker and a hand that plays well postflop. I’ll open to 300 (3x the BB): big enough to fold out weaker blind defenses and keep SPR manageable if called. If Elon (SB) 3-bets heavily I can fold; versus a moderate 3-bet I can call and use position to steer the hand. Raising also builds the pot when I have a range advantage in position.

Pre-flop in SB with JTs suited against a button raise from Minni to 300 (pot 450, to call 250 more, total commit 300). JTs is a strong speculative hand with straight and flush potential, good playability, and position on BB. Minni's frequent pre-flop raises from history suggest a wide button range, providing good implied odds deep-stacked (~77BB effective). Pot odds are favorable (~2:1). Folding is too tight; 3-betting risks too much with a marginal polarizing hand OOP vs button. Calling to realize equity multi-way or isolate post-flop is optimal, planning to play aggressively on favorable boards.

I am holding Ace-King offsuit in the Big Blind, which is a premium starting hand. The Button (Minni) raised to 300 and the Small Blind (Elon) called. This is a perfect opportunity for a squeeze play. By raising here, I can either take down the pot immediately or build a larger pot with a hand that has significant equity against the ranges of both opponents. Since I am out of position for the rest of the hand, a larger raise size is preferred to discourage callers and simplify the decision-making process on the flop. I will raise to 1200.

Flash's 3-bet to 1,200 is a big, value-leaning shove relative to my 300 open and Elon is already in — this is a multiway spot. The pot is 1,800 and I must call 900, so I need ~33% equity to justify a call. A♦6♥ is a weak, easily dominated hand vs a typical 3-bet range and plays poorly multiway; a small 4-bet bluff (min to 2,100) has thin fold equity and invites tough decisions versus deeper stacks. I opened intending to fold to a heavy 3-bet, so I’m folding here and preserving my stack for better spots in position.

Facing a large squeeze 3-bet to 1200 (4x the button open) from Flash in the BB after flatting with JTs suited. While JTs has playability and ~30-40% equity vs a wide BB squeeze range (QQ+, AK, suited broadways, Ax suited, connectors for bluffs), being OOP heads-up deepstacked (~75BB eff post-call) vs an aggressive opponent is a nightmare for speculative hand realization. Low SPR (~2.5:1 post-call) favors caller having strong hand or nutted draw, but JTs vulnerable to domination (e.g., KQs, QJs) and overcards. Pot odds ~3:1 require only 25% equity, but poor playability/implied odds OOP warrant folding to preserve stack for superior spots. History shows Flash aggressive (frequent PF raises, some thin value), but this sizing reps strength. No 4-bet value.

Preflop on the button with 53o, a very weak offsuit gapped hand with poor playability and low equity against any reasonable range. Facing a to-call of 100 into a 150 pot offers insufficient odds given the hand's trash nature. Even with position in a 3-handed game, folding conserves chips for premium spots. Opponents have been aggressive in history, but no reason to speculate with this holding.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten of Spades and Five of Clubs. This is a weak starting hand. While it only costs 50 more to call, I would be playing out of position against Minni, who has shown significant aggression throughout the game. Given the weakness of my hand and the positional disadvantage, folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with T9o, which is a decent but not premium hand in a 3-handed game. My stack is healthy at 15,250. Standard play from the button is to raise to around 2.5x or 3x the big blind to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the hand. However, given my position and the need to maintain a solid range, raising to 300 is a standard aggressive opening move.

I have A6s in the small blind facing a button open to 300 with the big blind yet to act. Folding is too tight for an ace-suited and calling 250 keeps me out of position in a likely multiway pot. I want to take the initiative and isolate the button's wide opening range while retaining fold-to-4bet flexibility. I’ll 3-bet to 900 total — big enough to apply pressure and reduce multiway action, but a modest proportion of my 17.2k stack so I can comfortably fold to a large 4-bet or continue as the aggressor postflop.

9d5h is a complete trash hand offsuit with a large gap, no playability. Facing a button open-raise from Flash followed by a 3-bet to 900 from Minni in the small blind, who has been aggressive throughout the session. I'm out of position in the big blind with a pot-sized raise already (800 to call into 1300 pot gives only about 38% implied odds needed, but equity vs their 3-bet ranges is far below 25%). No reason to speculate with this hand; folding preserves my stack for better spots. Standard fold.

I opened with T9o from the button, which is a standard play. However, Minni has 3-bet me from the small blind. While I have position, T9o is generally not strong enough to call a 3-bet against a player showing strength, as it is often dominated by their range (hands like AT, KT, or larger pairs). I will fold to maintain my stack for better spots.

I’m on the button with A♠7♥ — a decent ace in position. Versus only the blinds I want to take the initiative, isolate heads‑up, and exploit my positional advantage. A standard 3xBB raise (to 300) is in line with table tendencies and gives good fold equity against Elon’s shorter SB stack while still being a reasonable size if I face a 3‑bet. Limping/calling would invite a multiway pot out of position and be lower EV. Plan: raise to 300; if faced with a small 3‑bet I’ll consider calling and playing postflop in position, fold to a large 3‑bet.

9h7d is a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and no high card strength. Facing a button raise from Minni, who raises frequently but often has a wide range. As SB, I'm OOP postflop, and this hand plays poorly out of position. Pot odds are about 2:1 effective (call 250 to win 450), but equity vs button open range (~40%) is marginal (~30-35%), and playability is bad with reverse position. BB still to act, but no reason to defend this trash. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Goal is long-term EV over 150 hands.

I am in the Big Blind with 8h 3c. Minni raised to 3x from the button. 83o is a very weak hand with poor playability post-flop. Even getting nearly 2.25:1 odds to call, the hand is well outside of a standard defending range against a button open. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Qd4d is a playable suited hand on the button with excellent post-flop position. The pot odds are favorable (calling 100 into 150, roughly 2.5:1 implied), requiring minimal equity against loose limping ranges common in this game based on history. Suited connectors like this have good playability for flush and straight draws. Opponents (Flash and Minni) defend wide and play loose-aggressive post-flop, so calling allows value realization without committing too much pre-flop. Raising risks multiway action with a marginal hand, while folding is too tight in position. Will control pot and play aggressively if improved.

I have a strong pocket pair (8h 8s) in the Small Blind. The Button (Elon) has limped into the pot. In a 3-handed game, pocket eights are a premium hand. I should raise to take the initiative and build a pot against what is likely a wide or weak limping range from the Button. Raising to 4x the big blind is a standard play from the small blind to discourage the big blind from defending too light and to extract value.

I’m on the big blind with Q♣5♠. Pot is 600 and I must put in 300 to call Flash’s SB raise to 400 (Elon has already called). This is a multiway, out-of-position spot facing a raiser whose sizing indicates either a reasonably strong or aggressive stealing range plus a caller — both make Q5o frequently dominated. Calling 300 gets me 3:1 pot odds (need ~25% equity), but my realistic equity vs these combined ranges is likely below that and I’ll have poor postflop playability and reverse implied odds. A minimum raise would be a thin isolation attempt from a weak hand and would leave me OOP against two players. I’ll fold and preserve my deep stack for a better spot.

Qd4d suited is a solid speculative hand on the button facing an isolation raise from the SB after my limp. Historical play shows Flash raises frequently from blinds and continues aggressively but not always with premium hands. Pot odds are excellent: calling 300 into 600 (~2:1, needing ~25% equity), and Q4s realizes at least that against a wide iso range (e.g., Ax, suited connectors, broadway). Deep stacks provide strong implied odds for flush/straight potential. Position postflop is huge advantage vs SB. Raising is unnecessary with a non-nutted hand and risks larger pots or folds equity; folding too tight given playability and history. Calling keeps pot controlled, sees flop cheaply in position to play aggressively if improved.

On this King-high board, my pocket eights are now essentially a bluff catcher. However, I was the pre-flop aggressor and this board favors the pre-flop raiser's range more than a limper's range. A continuation bet is necessary here to represent a King or a better pocket pair and to deny equity to random overcards that Elon might have. A bet of roughly 1/3 to 1/2 pot should suffice to gather information and potentially end the hand right here.

Flop gives me middle pair (4s) with a weak kicker and backdoor diamond flush draw on a dry Ks-4h-2c board. Flash's SB iso-raise preflop followed by a 1/3 pot continuation bet suggests a wide polarized range: overpairs, Ks, Ax, bluffs, or pocket pairs. My equity is strong enough against this (~35-45% vs typical c-bet range), easily covering the excellent pot odds (400 to call into 1700, ~19% needed). Position allows control: call cheaply, re-evaluate turn. Raising risks commitment with one pair no kicker against possible strong hands or induces folds from worse; folding is too tight given history of Flash's loose-aggressive post-flop play (frequent small bets, called down in pots like H29, H37). Deep stacks (69bb) support calling for implied odds if I improve to two-pair/straight/flush draw. Consistent with preflop plan to realize equity in position.

On this King-high board, my pocket eights remain a marginal hand after being called on the flop. The 6h on the turn is relatively blank, although it could theoretically improve a hand like 66 or 57/35, though those are less likely. Since Elon limped pre-flop and then called my continuation bet, his range is likely composed of mid-range pairs, some weak Kings, or perhaps Ace-high. By checking here, I can control the size of the pot and evaluate Elon's reaction. If he checks back, I likely have the best hand or am in a good spot to see a cheap showdown. If he bets, I'll have to consider if he's turning a hand like a small pair into a bluff or if he truly has the King.

On the turn, board is Ks-4h-2c-6h. I have middle pair (4s) with Qd kicker and backdoor diamond draw. Heads-up vs Flash (SB) who iso-raised pre-flop, c-bet flop (dry board), and now checks turn after bricking. His range: polarized with Kx/overpairs/value, mid pairs, missed draws/Ax floats, bluffs. Checking turn could indicate weakness (missed draw, weak pair giving up), trap, or control. Pot 1700, position on button is huge. Equity ~40-50% vs his range (SDV strong vs Ax/draws, folds better). Checking behind sees free river but risks giving free cards to draws (hearts ~9 outs, 3-5 ~4 outs straight draw). Thin value bet extracts from worse (weaker pairs 77-JJ, Ax, floats) and protects vs draws. History: Flash aggressive but folds to aggression when missed (e.g., H37 fold, H29 big pot but folded river). Bet 2/3 pot (1100) balances value/protection, denies equity, builds pot if ahead. Deep stacks support. Raising unlikely needed; check risks multiway feel but heads-up fine. Consistent with positional speculative call pre/flop.

On the turn, the 6h is a blank card for the most part, though it does complete a very unlikely straight (35, 57). After I checked to control the pot, Elon made a substantial bet of 1100 into a 1700 pot (roughly 65% pot). While my pocket eights are a decent hand, the King on the board is a major problem. Elon called my continuation bet on the flop, suggesting he has a piece of this board. He could easily have a King (like K9s, KTs), or a set (44, 22, or now 66). Given his stack size and the bet sizing, calling here would likely commit me to calling a river shove as well, and my hand is essentially a bluff catcher at this point. I have a healthy stack and can find better spots to commit chips than with a mid-pair on a K-high board against a player showing significant aggression.

I am on the button in a 3-handed game with King-Five offsuit. This is a standard opening hand in this position to put pressure on the blinds. I will raise to 300 to attempt to take the pot pre-flop or play the hand in position.

I’m in the small blind with A♣T♥ facing a 3BB button open to 300 (pot 450, I must call 250). ATo is a good candidate to 3-bet here: it blocks strong A‑x combos, has decent showdown/value potential, and I don’t want to play multiway out-of-position if Elon cold-calls. I’ll 3-bet to take the initiative and isolate the button while keeping the sizing reasonable given deep stacks. Raising to 1,000 total (~10BB) gives solid fold equity vs the BTN and makes Elon less likely to just limp‑call, while leaving plenty of stack behind to maneuver postflop. Plan: if Flash calls we proceed with standard c-bet frequencies on favourable textures; if I face a sizable 4‑bet I’ll fold ATo rather than get in too deep out of position.

Preflop in BB with 9h4h suited connectors, facing a button raise to 300 from Flash and a 3-bet to 1000 from Minni in SB. Pot 1400, to call 900 OOP. 94s has poor equity against likely 3-bet ranges (premiums, broadways), and being OOP makes it hard to realize equity even if multiway. Pot odds ~2.5:1 not enough for speculative hand. No position advantage or deep stacks to justify calling. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

I opened to 300 from the button with K5o, and Minni has 3-bet to 1000 from the small blind. While I am in position, K5o is quite weak against a 3-bet range, especially from Minni who has shown aggression in previous hands. Calling 700 more with such a dominated hand usually leads to difficult situations post-flop. I'll fold and wait for a better spot.

Button with 6♠5♦ — a marginal, connected but offsuit hand. Pot is 150 and I can call 100; that’s a cheap price (I need ~40% equity to break even) and I have full positional advantage against the SB and BB. With deep stacks (my 18.1k vs their 8.1k/13.65k) I get good implied odds when I flop a pair or a draw. A min-raise to 200 is unnecessary: it invites 3-bets (Flash has shown willingness to put money in) and I don’t want to face that inflated pot with a speculative offsuit connector. Plan: call, see the flop, fold to heavy 3-bets preflop; postflop play straightforward — continue with pairs/draws, fold to large aggression when missed, use position to extract value when I hit.

In the small blind with Qs5s suited facing a button limp. Pot is 250, calling 50 more for 6:1 direct odds, implied odds better with suited hand potential for straights and flushes. Multi-way pot likely with BB to act. Folding too tight preflop in SB for this price; raising OOP with marginal suited hand risks playing OOP postflop after potential 3bet. Standard play is to complete and realize equity cheaply.

I have KJs in the big blind. The action has folded to Minni on the button who completed, and Elon in the small blind who also completed. This is a very weak pre-flop action (limped pot).
