KJs is a premium hand in this scenario, and I want to build a pot and potentially take it down pre-flop. Raising here is the standard play to punish the limpers and realize equity.

I originally planned to call a small open on the button, but Flash’s raise to 450 after I’d already invested 100 changes the math. Pot is 650 and I must call 350 more — roughly 35% equity to break even. 6♠5♦ offsuit is a speculative, poorly performing hand multiway and is frequently dominated by overcards. There’s already a caller and an aggressive raiser; my implied odds are significantly reduced and a 3‑bet/squeeze here would be a thin bluff that risks too much against deep but calling ranges. I’ll fold and preserve my deep stack for a better spot where my positional advantage and implied odds are meaningful.

Facing a raise to 450 from Flash in the BB after I completed SB cheaply with Qs5s and Button limped then folded. Pot 650, calling 350 more gives about 1.85:1 odds (35% equity needed). Qs5s is suited spade queen-high with straight/flush potential, good playability postflop. Flash has been aggressive preflop, often raising light from late positions/BB to isolate, as seen in hands like #50, #54, #61 where he raises/3bets. Vs his likely wide squeezing range (dominated broadways, Ax suited, suited connectors), Q5s has solid equity (~35-40% vs polarized open raise range) and great implied odds on deep stacks (my 80bb, his 133bb). OOP but pot odds too good to fold speculative suited hand; 3betting marginal OOP risks dominated stacks. Calling realizes equity cheaply, sees flop with position closing postflop.

On the flop of 8d Qd 6h, I have top pair (queens) with Qs5s, plus a gutshot straight draw to the 7 (4 clean outs, ~8% equity from gutshot alone, total equity boosted by pair). The board is draw-heavy with diamond flush draw and possible straight draws (5-7, 7-9), so betting protects against draws and gets value from Flash's likely wide preflop raising range from the BB (suited connectors, Ax, weaker pairs, broadway that missed). As first to act OOP, checking risks giving a free card to draws or facing a bet where I have to call OOP anyway; leading thin value builds the pot while denying equity. Flash's aggression (e.g., isolation raises in #50, #54) suggests he'll fold worse or pay off with draws/missed hands. Bet sizing 2/3 pot (650 into 1000) balances value/protection without overcommitting stacks (77BB effective). Deep stacks allow fold equity and room for postflop play.

I raised pre-flop with KJs and was called by Elon in the SB. The flop comes Qd-8d-6h, which misses my range significantly. Elon has led out with a donk bet of 650 into a 1000 pot (65% pot size). This lead into the pre-flop aggressor often indicates a made hand like a Queen or a pair of eights. While I have two overcards, I have no immediate flush or straight draws. Given the size of the bet and the lack of board connectivity for my hand, folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

3-handed on the button with J8o after SB folds (implied by empty log, pot 150, to call 100). This is a standard open-raising spot. J8o is well within button open range (~60-70% in 3-handed), has good playability postflop, and position on BB Minni. Standard raise size to 300 (3x BB). Builds pot, takes initiative, and exploits wide BB defending ranges seen in history. Folding too tight; limping non-standard preflop.

I have a pocket pair (5h 5d), which is a solid hand to play in a 3-handed game against a button open. While being out of position is a disadvantage, the implied odds of hitting a set against Elon's stack are sufficient to justify a call. I will call the 250 more to see a flop and look to set-mine.

I’m in the big blind with 6♥2♥ facing a button open to 300 and the small blind called. Pot is 700 and I must call 200. This is a multiway pot and 6-2s has very poor equity and postflop playability against two ranges — it’s frequently dominated (higher hearts, higher low cards) and performs poorly out of position. Calling requires ~22% equity to be profitable right away, and with two opponents my equity is well below that. 3-betting as a bluff is thin here since the SB has already called and I’d face difficult decisions OOP. I’ll fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

The flop is extremely dry with a pair of Kings (Ks 3c Kc). My pocket 5s are likely the best hand if Elon doesn't have a King or a higher pocket pair. However, as the out-of-position player, I should check to the pre-flop aggressor to see how he proceeds. If he bets small, I can consider calling to see a turn, but I must be cautious of the paired board and his range.

Heads-up on the flop vs SB Flash who checked after calling my BTN open preflop. Board is K♠3♣K♣, a paired kings board with two clubs, relatively dry but with some backdoor draws. My J♥8♦ has no pair, no draw, pure air, but as the preflop raiser my range is perceived to connect well here (AK, KQ, KTs, suited connectors, bluffs). SB calling range vs BTN raise is wide (suited Ax, broadway, pairs, suited connectors), but he checks which is weak and invites a cbet. This is a standard spot to cbet wide for thin value (vs worse kings/Ax/draws) and fold equity. Pot 700, betting ~60% pot (400) maintains sizing consistency, builds pot OOP when ahead, denies equity to floats/draws. Checking back risks giving free cards to overcards/draws and loses initiative. Deep stacks (84bb effective), but cbetting exploits position and range advantage. History shows Flash folds to cbets often when checked (e.g., hands 14,18,42).

The flop is Ks 3c Kc, which is a very dry, paired board. My pocket 5s are ahead of Elon's 'air' (non-pairing cards like AQ, AJ, etc.) but behind any King or any pocket pair higher than 55. Elon's c-bet of 400 into a 700 pot (making the total pot 1100) is a standard sizing for this texture. Given the pot odds and the fact that I'm only losing to a narrow portion of a Button opening range (Kings and 66+), I will call one street to see if he checks back the turn or if I can improve. However, I must be prepared to fold to further aggression on most turn cards.

On this paired board (Ks 3c Kc 8s), my pocket fives are a marginal bluff-catcher. I checked the flop and called a standard c-bet. Now on the turn, the 8s is largely a blank, though it does pick up some backdoor flush equity for some of Elon's range. I will check to Elon to see if he continues his aggression. If he checks back, I am likely ahead or have a safe path to a cheap showdown. If he bets again, I will have to evaluate based on his sizing, but my default plan is to lean towards a fold as a double-barrel on such a dry, static board often indicates a King or a superior pocket pair in a button-vs-blind scenario.

Preflop BTN raise with J8o standard. Flop cbet with air exploiting range advantage on paired kings board. Turn 8s pairs my 8 for middle pair with J kicker (88JJ effectively). Board Ks3cKc8s adds spade flush draw threat. Flash OOP check-calls flop (likely weak Kx like KTs-K9s, Ax suited, small pairs/draws), now double-checks suggesting weakness or giving up floats. My range strong (KK, AK, KQ, sets). Bet for thin value vs worse Kx/pairs/draws chasing spade/club, deny equity, build pot OOP opponent's weakness. History shows Flash folds to continued aggression when checked (e.g., Hand 14,18,42,60). Pot 1500, bet 800 (~53% pot) balanced size for value calls from worse while protecting. Checking risks free river cards to overcards/draws; deep stacks allow multi-street value.

The board is very static (Ks 3c Kc 8s), and Elon is following up his flop continuation bet with a second barrel on the turn. While my pocket fives are ahead of pure bluffs, they lose to all of his value hands (any King, AA, QQ-99, and now 88). In a button-vs-blind scenario, Elon could be bluffing with air or semi-bluffing with a spade draw, but the 800-chip sizing into a 1500-chip pot suggests significant strength on this texture. Given that I am out of position and have a very weak bluff-catcher that will be difficult to play on the river, I will fold now and look for a better spot.

I am on the button with 7d 4c, a very weak hand. While opening the button is often profitable in three-handed games, this specific hand has very little post-flop playability and Minni has been playing aggressively from the blinds recently. With a stack of 126 hands left and better spots to come, I will simply fold this hand and wait for a stronger opening opportunity.

Heads-up to the big blind with K♥6♠. This is a playable hand from the small blind in a 2-way pot — I want initiative and to steal the BB often, so I’ll raise to 200 (2x BB). Size rationale: 200 is big enough to fold some marginal BB hands and keep the pot controllable with deep stacks. Plan: fold to a large, committed 3-bet (I’m not calling big 3-bets with K6); if Elon calls I’ll C-bet favourable flops (K‑high, low/dry boards) ~50–60% pot, check/control on Ace‑high textures, and value-bet when I hit a King or improve. This balances aggression and risk management against an opponent who shows frequent preflop and postflop aggression.

Preflop in the big blind facing a raise to 200 from Minni in the small blind after Flash folds. My hand is 2s9c, a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. It's unplayable out of position. Pot odds are about 3:1 (100 to call into 300 pot), but equity against a raising range is negligible, and postflop play would be disastrous OOP with such a weak holding. Minni has been active and aggressive historically, so folding preserves stack for better spots. Standard fold.

Button with Q♣4♥: marginal but position and fold equity justify a standard steal. Pot is only the blinds (150) and to call is 100; opening here gives me initiative vs two players with deep stacks. I’ll raise to a conventional 3xBB (300 total) — a size consistent with table tendencies that maximizes fold equity while keeping the pot manageable if called. Plan postflop: use positional advantage to continuation-bet favorable, dry boards; on paired or coordinated boards I’ll check/float or pot-control. If I face a sizable 3-bet, I’ll fold this hand — it’s poorly equipped to continue out of position or in a big pot. Overall this is an exploitative open-raise to take the pot or play in position versus one opponent.

Ks5d is a weak offsuit hand with marginal high card value and poor connectivity or suitedness. Facing a button raise to 3x from Minni, who has been aggressive historically, from Small Blind out of position. Pot odds are about 1.8:1 (250 to call into 450 pot), but postflop I'll be OOP with a hand that plays poorly, low equity against a wide button range. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots in this deepstack game.

I am holding the worst possible starting hand in Texas Hold'em (7-2 offsuit) and am facing a raise from the button. While the pot odds are 2.25:1, the lack of playability and the fact that I will be out of position for the rest of the hand makes this an easy fold.

3-handed on the button with Qc4c, a suited queen-high hand with straight and flush potential. Excellent position for stealing blinds. Standard open-raise size from this spot is 3x the big blind to 300, which pressures the blinds who often fold weak hands pre-flop based on history. Deep stacks allow post-flop playability if called. Folding is too tight, calling/limping invites multi-way pots OOP.

I am in the Small Blind with 9s9h, a strong pocket pair. Elon, the Button, has raised to 300. Historically, Elon has been quite active with pre-flop raises. With a pair of nines, I have a clear value-raising hand here. A 3-bet to 1000 or 1100 is standard to build the pot and take the initiative, especially out of position. However, looking at my stack and the flow of the game, I want to discourage Minni in the Big Blind and define the hand against Elon. I'll raise to 1000.

Elon opened to 300 and Flash 3‑bet to 1000. Pot is 1400 and it’s 900 more to call (1000 total) — break‑even equity on a call is about 39%. I have A♦8♠ offsuit: a decent but easily dominated hand vs a 3‑betting range and with poor post‑flop playability out of position to both opponents. 4‑betting as a bluff with A8o is thin (few useful blockers) and a call often creates a difficult multiway OOP pot. Given the sizing, ranges and my position, folding and preserving chips for a better spot is the +EV line here.

Facing a 3-bet to 1000 from Flash in the SB after my button open to 300 with Qc4c. Pot is 1400, calling 700 OOP into a strong 3-bet range from SB (which includes premiums like QQ+, AK, and some bluffs, but weighted towards value). Qc4s has some playability deep-stacked with suited connectors potential, but calling OOP risks playing postflop in tough spot with position disadvantage and reverse implied odds if I hit. Folding is correct GTO play here as equity realization is poor against SB 3-bet range (~35-40% equity at best, but poor playability). Raising all-in or min-raise is overplay; preserve stack for better spots. History shows Flash aggressive but this spot favors fold.

I am on the button with Qh 3h. While Q-high suited can be an opening hand in a three-handed game, the recent history shows that both Minni and Elon have been playing aggressively against my button raises, with Minni frequently 3-betting. Q3s is at the very bottom of a standard button opening range and does not play well against resistance or post-flop without a strong hit. Given the stack sizes and recent table dynamics, I will choose to fold this hand and wait for a better spot to utilize my position.

Heads-up from the small blind with 4♦9♥. Pot is 150, I’m facing a 50 chip call and min-raise to 200. In heads-up play I should open a wide range; 9-4o is weak but acceptable to raise here to seize initiative and apply fold equity. I’ll min-raise to 200 (2x BB) — if Elon 3-bets I’ll fold (hand has poor playability OOP), if he calls I’ll proceed carefully on the flop, c-betting favorable textures and checking/folding when I miss.

9d2h is one of the worst possible hands offsuit, with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Minni's raise to 200 from SB is a min-raise, indicating a potentially wide range, but even against a loose range, 92o has negligible equity and poor post-flop playability. Pot odds are about 3:1 (call 100 to win 300), but equity is far below 25% needed. Deep stacks don't justify speculative defense from BB here. Standard fold to avoid tough spots OOP.

I’m on the button in a 3-handed spot with A♦8♦ — a strong, playable hand with suited ace-block and good postflop equity. Limping is too passive; I want to take the initiative, isolate the blinds, and build a pot I can play in position. Standard sizing in these games is ~3x the big blind; that gives plenty of fold equity without overcommitting against deeper stacks. If I get called I’ll play postflop in position—c-bet favorable textures, proceed cautiously multiway, and look to extract value when I hit. If I face a substantial 3-bet I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to a large 3-bet, call smaller 3-bets selectively for position/implied odds). Raise to 300 (total) to open the pot and apply pressure.

Preflop in SB with AhKc, a premium hand facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). Button opens wide, so AKo has excellent equity and plays well multiway or heads-up. Standard to 3-bet for value, isolate, and build pot. Sizing to 900 (3x their raise) is aggressive but standard given deep stacks (89BB) and history of similar sizing. BB may fold or call light, but we have position advantage postflop OOP only vs button. Folding or calling is too passive.

In this 3-handed situation, I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Nine suited, a very strong hand for this configuration. Minni (Button) opened to 300, and Elon (Small Blind) 3-bet to 900. While cold-calling a 3-bet is often risky due to being out of position, A9s has excellent playability and nut potential (flushes, top pair). In a 3-handed game, 3-betting ranges from the Small Blind are typically wider to isolate the Button, meaning my hand is likely ahead of or flipping against a significant portion of Elon's range. Folding is too tight given the strength of my hand and the pot odds. Calling allows me to close the pre-flop action (unless Minni 4-bets) and see a flop with a hand that performs well post-flop.

I opened to 300 on the button with A♦8♦ — a playable, suited Ace with good postflop playability. Elon (SB) 3-bet to 900 and Flash (BB) called, creating a 3-way pot of 2,100. Calling here costs me 600 more (900 total committed) for good price and the advantage of position on both opponents. A♦8♦ has blocker value to big Ax hands, nut-flush potential, and enough equity vs SB’s 3-bet range and BB’s calling range to realize value postflop. 4-betting thin is too loose in a multiway spot and risks isolating out of position or getting 5-bet; folding would be too conservative given favorable pot odds (~22% to see the flop) and deep implied odds. Plan: call, evaluate flop in position — proceed aggressively when I hit (top pair/flush/strong draws) and fold to heavy action when I completely miss.

Preflop 3-bet with AKo got two callers (button defender and BB), building a 2700 pot OOP on a 3s Ts 6d flop. Board is drawy (spade flush draw, open-enders like 45, 457) but AKo has solid blocker equity (blocks AA, AK, KK), decent high-card strength, and excellent fold equity as the 3-bettor. Their ranges include pairs (smaller than T), suited connectors/draws, and some broadways, many of which we beat or have equity against. No reason to check-fold a premium hand here; standard c-bet for value/protection/fold equity. Size 2/3 pot (1800) balances range, denies equity to draws, and pot-commits no one deeply (my stack 8050). History shows opponents call big but fold to aggression when missed.

In this three-way pot, I have completely missed the flop with Ace-Nine of clubs. Elon, the pre-flop 3-bettor, has led out for a large bet (two-thirds of the pot). This bet into two opponents typically indicates a strong range, likely overpairs, top pair (Ten), or perhaps a strong spade draw. Furthermore, Minni is still to act behind me. Calling with just Ace-high and no backdoor draws would be extremely speculative and likely lead to further difficult decisions on later streets. Folding is the most prudent and disciplined play here to preserve my stack.

I’m on the button with A♦8♦. Preflop I opened and called Elon's 3‑bet; Flash has folded on the flop, so it’s heads‑up. The board 3♠ 10♠ 6♦ is fairly dry and I currently only have Ace‑high with a backdoor diamond draw. Elon’s 1800 into the 2700 pot gives me ~28.6% pot‑odds to call (1800 to win 6300), and my hand has roughly that level of equity vs a 3‑bet/c‑bet range made up of broadways, A‑x, and pocket pairs.

Playing in position is a big advantage here — calling keeps the pot under control, preserves fold equity on later streets, and lets me fold to heavy turn aggression if I miss. A raise as a thin bluff is risky against a 3‑bettor who can shove or call with a lot of hands that crush me (T×, pairs, spade draws), and folding would be too tight given the pot odds and my blocker to some A‑combos. Plan: call, see the turn, and be prepared to fold to strong turn aggression unless I improve (Ace or diamond) or pick up further information.

Heads-up on turn with TPTK (AhKc) on 3s-Ts-6d-Kh board. Pot 6300 OOP after Minni called flop c-bet of 1800 into ~2700. My hand improved from AK high to top pair top kicker, strong value hand that beats most of villain's calling range: smaller pairs (77-JJ), weaker aces (AQ/AJ/AT), flush draws (spades without K), straight draws (45, etc.). Blocks AA/AK/AKs. Board drawy but K turn didn't complete obvious draws much (spades still live, but straight draws like J7 or 45 miss unless backdoors). Minni's button call-call range vulnerable to continued aggression; history shows she calls large but folds when missed or weak. Checking risks giving free card to draws or check-raises, and invites bluffs we beat anyway. Standard value bet ~2/3 pot denies equity, extracts from worse, and sizes well deep (6250 stack). 4200 balances value/protection/overbet denial.

Fold. On the turn (3♠ 10♠ 6♦ K♥) I only have A♦8♦ — effectively just Ace‑high now. Elon 3‑bet preflop, barreled a large flop bet and now fires 4,200 into a 10,500 pot; calling requires ~28.6% equity (4,200 / (10,500+4,200)). My clean outs are 3 Aces + 3 eights = 6 outs ≈ 13% to improve on the river, far below the required equity. The backdoor diamond flush is dead (only one diamond on board and one card to come). Given his line (value‑heavy range: Kx, Tx, sets, big pairs) and my poor equity, a call is -EV and a raise is an overly thin bluff against a polarized, committed opponent (raising to 6,250 would just risk an all‑in with little fold equity). Preserve my stack and fold.

Preflop on the button with K9o, a strong playable hand in position against the big blind. The pot is 150 (blinds posted), to call 100 suggests the small blind folded, leaving an unopened pot. Deep stacks (12.5k effective vs BB). Standard to raise 3x the BB (300 total) to take initiative, build the pot with position, and apply pressure. K9o has good equity and playability OOP for opponent. Opponent tendencies show Minni defends wide in BB.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Ten offsuit. The button (Elon) has opened for 3x. Ace-Ten is a strong enough hand to 3-bet for value and to take control of the pot out of position, especially against a button range. 3-betting also reduces the likelihood of the Big Blind (Minni) entering the pot or squeezing. Given previous hands, 3-betting has been effective in ending the hand pre-flop or gaining initiative.

I have A♣J♠ in the big blind facing a BTN open to 300 and a small-blind 3-bet to 1000. The pot is ~1400 and it costs me 900 more (1000 total). AJo is a strong hand but often dominated by a standard SB 3-bet value range (AK/AQ/TT+), and 4-betting here would be thin and polarizing — I don’t want to bloat an OOP pot versus two players or face a 5-bet shove. Calling keeps worse hands and cold-calls in, gives good pot odds (~39%) to see a flop, preserves stackdepth for postflop play, and lets me re-evaluate based on whether Elon cold-calls or folds. Plan: call to see the flop and proceed cautiously OOP — take down the pot with good flops or fold to heavy aggression when I’m clearly behind.

Facing a 3-bet from Flash in the SB after my button open to 300, followed by a flat-call from Minni in the BB. K9o is a playable opening hand from the button but becomes marginal in a 3-bet pot heads-up, and weaker multiway. The pot is 2300, calling 700 gives good odds (~3.3:1 direct), but postflop I'll be OOP to Flash (acts first) though IP to Minni—no, button acts last postflop (SB, BB, BTN order). Still, ranges: Flash's SB 3-bet vs BTN open is wide (pairs, broadways, suited hands, bluffs); Minni's BB call of 3-bet OOP suggests decent strength (TT+, AJs+, KQs, etc.). My K9o has ~30-35% equity multiway vs typical ranges, but playability suffers offsuit with dominated king potential. Deep stacks mean no need to commit with marginal hand; fold preserves stack for premium spots. History shows Flash aggressive, Minni defends wide but calling 3-bets filters to stronger hands. Standard fold.

I 3-bet pre-flop from the Small Blind with Ace-Ten offsuit and was called by the Big Blind (Minni), which usually indicates a range including pocket pairs, suited connectors, or broadway cards. The flop is Jack-Four-Deuce rainbow. This board is relatively dry. While I missed the flop, I still have two overcards and the range advantage as the pre-flop aggressor. A continuation bet here serves to take down the pot immediately, as Minni will likely fold hands that missed this board entirely (like medium suited connectors or smaller pairs if I apply enough pressure later). However, given the pot size and the fact that Minni called a 3-bet out of the Big Blind, a standard c-bet of about 1/3 to 1/2 pot is appropriate to maintain initiative.

I flatted the SB 3‑bet preflop with A♣J♠ to see a flop. On J♦4♣2♣ I have top pair with the nut Ace kicker and a backdoor club-runner possibility — a very strong hand versus the 3‑bettor's continuation range. Flash's sizing (800 into ~3100, ~25% pot) is very small and looks like a wide/probing c‑bet rather than a polarized huge-value line. Calling would surrender the initiative and give him cheap cards to barrel or float; a raise extracts value from worse Jx/Ax/Kx hands, folds out pure air and many floats, and charges the few backdoor draws. I will make a value/protection raise to the minimum allowed (raise to 1600). If he calls I can continue evaluating on the turn; if he shows heavy aggression I will re‑assess (he may have an overpair/set and I can fold or proceed depending on sizing).

The board is Jack-Four-Deuce rainbow. I 3-bet pre-flop and continuation bet for roughly 1/3 pot. Minni's min-raise from the Big Blind is a very strong signal in a 3-bet pot, typically representing a Jack with a good kicker (AJ, KJ) or a set (44, 22). Although the pot odds are very favorable (calling 800 to win 4700), my Ace-Ten offsuit is only Ace-high with no flush or straight draws. Calling here would put me in a difficult position out of position on the turn, as I would likely have to fold to any further aggression unless I hit one of my few outs, which might not even be clean. I'll fold to preserve my stack for a better opportunity.

I am on the button with Kh 4d. In a three-handed game, this hand is at the bottom of the range and generally too weak to open, especially considering that Minni and Elon have been playing aggressively and calling/3-betting frequently in recent hands. Folding preserves my stack for better opportunities.

KdQh (K-Q offsuit) in the small blind heads-up is a strong, standard open-raise hand. I want to take the initiative, isolate the big blind and avoid playing a marginal limp out of position. Raise sizing should be large enough to get value and fold out weaker hands but small enough to leave room postflop with deep stacks. I will raise to 300 total (≈3x the BB). If Elon 3-bets I will re-evaluate based on sizing and his tendencies (often call or 4-bet light vs a standard 3-bet, fold to an over-aggressive huge 3-bet). For now open to 300 to build the pot and define his range.

AQo is a strong premium hand in the big blind facing a small blind open-raise after the button folds. Minni's small blind raises are often wide-ranging steals, as seen in her frequent preflop aggression across previous hands (e.g., Hands 5,9,13,14,18,20, etc.). My hand has excellent equity against her likely calling range (dominated broadways, suited connectors, Ax, pairs), and 3-betting isolates her, builds the pot when ahead, and applies pressure OOP. Calling keeps pot small but realizes equity poorly OOP against her frequent CBets (as in Hands 6,17,43). Folding is too tight with 6.5:1 odds (200 into 400). Raising to 900 (3x her raise) is standard sizing deepstacked: denies her equity on trash, gets folds from worse, and commits appropriately without overexposing to 4-bets (rare from her history). Long-term EV+ to maximize stack.

I opened to 300 to take the initiative from the small blind. Elon’s 3-bet to 900 is a standard-sized re‑raise that can include both value and some light 3‑bets. K♣Q♦ (KQ offsuit) is too good to fold to a single 3‑bet given the pot odds and deep stacks, but it’s not a clean value 4‑bet candidate (a small 4‑bet to 1500 would be easily continued/5‑bett by his value hands and a large 4‑bet commits too much with a dominated hand). I only need to call 600 more to win a 1800 pot (≈33% equity required), and KQ has reasonable equity vs a mixed 3‑bet range and useful blockers. Plan: flat-call to keep the pot manageable and realize equity; on the flop play according to texture (pot control on dry boards, fold to large strength if I miss on heavy action or proceed cautiously if I hit).

I have top pair (Q) with the best available kicker (K) on a fairly dry Q-T-4 board. Villain 3‑bet preflop so his range contains many overpairs (AA/KK), sets (QQ/TT) and strong Broadway hands (AK/AQ) as well as some lighter 3‑bet bluffs. This is a good spot to extract thin value and deny free cards to the very few backdoor combos that can improve. I’ll make a value bet of ~1/3 pot (600) to get called by worse queens, Kx and AK while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks; if he check‑raises large I’ll re‑evaluate and play cautiously (likely fold to an extreme polarized raise, call smaller raises to probe).

TPTK (pair of Queens with Ace kicker) on a Qd Tc 4s rainbow flop is an extremely strong hand after 3-betting preflop and isolating Minni's SB calling range, which includes QQ+, AK, AQ, KQ, suited broadways, pocket pairs, and some suited connectors/draws. The board has some straight draw potential (J9, KT, etc.) and backdoor flush, but no strong draws hit hard yet. Her donk-bet of 600 into ~1800 pot (~33% pot) is polarized: strong value (QQ, QT, Q4, sets like 44/TT, two-pair like T4) or bluffs/draws (flush/straight draws like JTs, 98s, A5s, air floats). Weak Qx or worse would often check OOP. Calling keeps pot small but allows her to realize equity with draws or outdraw us; flat OOP vs aggressive opponent risks getting outdrawn or check-raised turn. Raising builds pot for value from her worse Qx (KQ, QJ, QT), pairs, draws while protecting vs gutshots/backdoors. Fold equity is high given her history of folding to postflop aggression after calling 3bets (e.g., Hand 14,18,41,42,60). Sizing to ~3.5x her bet (2100 total) denies her clean odds on draws, gets value from medium strength, and sets up river shove if called. Stacks deep (11k effective), implied odds huge when ahead (~85% equity vs calling range). Long-term EV+ to deny equity and extract value.

I have top pair with the best kicker on a fairly dry Q-T-4 flop. I led for 600 and Elon check‑raised big to 2100 after 3‑betting preflop; that line is value‑heavy (QQ/AA/KK/AQ) but also can contain missed AK/Ax combos and occasional bluffs. Calling 1500 more (putting 2100 total into the pot this street) gives me ~25% pot odds to continue and lets me realize equity without bloating the pot against a likely polarized, strong range. Raising here would unnecessarily commit a lot of chips and can get me 4‑bet or priced in by better hands; folding is too weak with top pair/top kicker. I’ll call, control the pot, and re‑evaluate on the turn — folding to further heavy aggression if the turn strongly favors his value combos, or extracting more value if he slows down.

Elon 3‑bet pre and check‑raised the flop — a very value‑heavy, polarized line. I called to control the pot and realize equity. The turn (T♦) pairs the board and actually turns many of his flop sets into full houses/quads, but he checked the turn, which can be pot control with hands like A Q / A K or a weakened range, or a trap. With top pair, best kicker (K♦ Q♥), I want to extract thin value from worse Qx/Ax/Kx hands while keeping the pot from ballooning against his stronger holdings. A modest value bet (~1/3 pot) achieves that: it gets called by worse hands I beat, charges cards, and keeps my folding decision manageable if he raises. I will bet 2,000; if he raises big I will fold (he's very likely polarized and ahead), if he just calls I will re‑evaluate on the river for further value or protection.

On the turn, the board is 4s Tc Qd Td, pairing the Tens. I hold AsQc for top pair (Queens) top kicker, plus the nut spade flush draw (As + 4s = 9 flush outs). This is an absolute monster with massive equity (~85-95% vs her polarized range of two-pair Tx, sets, Qx, or draws like J9/KJ flush/straight draws). Pot is 8000, she bets 2000 (~25% pot), which continues her aggression but OOP after calling my flop raise—likely value (TT/44/set, QT/T4 two-pair, Qx) or draws/protection. Calling realizes equity poorly OOP vs her frequent turns/rivers (e.g., Hands 6,12,17), risks free cards, and invites multi-street bluffs. Folding is insane. Raising extracts value from weaker Qx (KQ/QJ/QT), Tx that she turns into bluffs, and draws that chase; fold equity high given her fold to flop raise here and postflop pressure (Hands 14,18,41,42,60,65). Sizing to 5000 (2.5x her bet, ~60% pot) denies odds on 8-12 outs draws (~25% equity), builds huge pot when ahead (implied odds massive deepstacked), sets up river shove/value, and commits without all-in (effective ~9k). Her history: calls 3bets/c-bets but folds to turns after resistance (e.g., Hand 81 shove got fold). Long-term stack-maximizing EV+ play OOP with god-tier hand.

Elon’s line is extremely polarized and value-heavy: 3-bet pre, a huge check‑raise on the flop and now a massive turn 5,000 raise to my 2,000 bet. He’s representing sets/full houses or strong overpairs (TT/QQ/KK/AA and the small number of two‑pair/full‑house combos) — only a few bluffs are plausible. The pot is 13,000 and I must call 3,000 (≈18.8% equity required). Against his likely range my K♦Q♥ (top pair, best kicker) has well under the required equity and calling would commit a large chunk of my stack with poor odds. Raising/reshoving is also negative EV here — it either gets called by better hands or only folds the few worse hands. I’ll preserve the stack and fold to this very large, polarized raise.

I’m on the button with T♠4♣ — a weak, frequently dominated hand. There’s only 100 to call into a 150 pot, and calling preserves my positional advantage versus both blinds while keeping the pot small. Raising (to 200/300+) would bloat the pot and likely invite a 3-bet from Elon or Flash, which I don’t want to face with this hand. Folding surrenders good odds for seeing a flop; calling is a cheap way to realize equity and use position to control the hand postflop. Plan: see the flop; if I connect (pair, good draw) play proactively; if I miss I’ll give up to significant aggression — otherwise look for spots to bluff in position.

Preflop in the small blind with Q2o, a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity multiway. Minni's button limp suggests a wide range, potentially trapping or speculative. Completing cheaply offers implied odds but commits me to playing out of position postflop in a likely 3-way pot against deep stacks, where Q2o realizes little value and is vulnerable to domination. Folding the blinds is standard to preserve stack for better spots, especially with 172x BB depth allowing premium hand selection. History shows opponents like Minni play aggressively postflop, making this a losing spot long-term.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Nine offsuit. The action has come to me after Minni (the button) just limped for 100 and Elon (the small blind) folded. Since there are no raises, I have the option to check and see a free flop, or raise to take control of the pot. A-9 is a reasonably strong hand in a three-handed game against a limper. However, I want to keep the pot small and realize my equity, or see how Minni reacts to a small raise. Given the limp, checking is the safest way to see the flop with a mid-strength hand, but raising can punish weak limps. Looking at the previous history, Minni has been quite active and sometimes folds to aggression or calls with a wide range. I'll check to see a flop and keep the pot manageable.

On the flop of 3d-8d-4h, I have Ace-high (Ac 9d) with one overcard and a backdoor diamond draw. The pot is very small (250) after checking pre-flop. Minni limped from the button, which often indicates a wide, mediocre range. My hand has some showdown value and can certainly improve on many turns. Since I am in the Big Blind and it has been checked to me, checking is the most standard play to see a free turn card. Betting here doesn't accomplish much as I'm unlikely to fold out better hands like small pairs, and I'd rather not bloat the pot with just Ace-high.

I hold T♠4♣ and flopped a pair of fours on 3♦ 8♦ 4♥. It’s heads-up and Flash checked — the board has two diamonds so there are many flush/draw combos that want to see a free turn. I should bet to get value from worse pairs/one-pair hands and to deny equity to diamond draws/overcards. I’ll make a ~60% pot protection/value bet (150 into the 250 pot) — large enough to charge draws and fold out pure air/overcards but small enough to preserve fold/call decisions on a check-raise. If he calls I’ll reevaluate turn action based on card and his tendencies; if he check-raises big I’ll be prepared to fold unless the sizing/board gives correct odds to continue.

On a flop of 3d-8d-4h, I have Ace-high with two overcards and a backdoor diamond draw. Minni's lead of 150 into a 250 pot is a relatively small bet, and since she limped from the button, her range is extremely wide and contains many hands that I currently beat or have good equity against. Folding to such a small bet with Ace-high and a backdoor draw would be too weak. I will call to see a turn card and re-evaluate, keeping the pot manageable for my hand's current strength.

On the turn, the Queen of Hearts is an overcard to the previous board. Since I am out of position with Ace-high and no significant improvement, checking is the most prudent play. Minni led the flop after limping pre-flop, and while the Queen might not have helped her specifically, I need to see her reaction to this card before committing more chips. My Ace-high still has some showdown value if the hand goes to check-check, and I don't want to bloat the pot without a made hand.

Turn brought Q♥ which is a classic scare card — my T♠4♣ is now a weak one-pair hand (pair of 4s) with only two clean outs (the two remaining 4s, ~4%). Flash called my flop bet and now checked the turn, so his range contains many hands that just improved (Qx) or have enough showdown value (8x/3x/pocket pairs) that will happily call a turn barrel. Betting here would get value rarely (only from worse pairs) while getting called by hands that beat me and risking a check-raise I can’t profitably call. With deep stacks and a small improvement chance, pot control is optimal — let the hand go to the river and re-evaluate. Plan: check back; if Flash fires a small river bet I can call for pot odds or fold to large, polarized river aggression unless I hit.

On the river, the board is 3d-8d-4h-Qh-7c. I am holding Ace-high (Ac 9d). Pre-flop was a limped pot, and on the flop, Minni bet small and I called. The turn went check-check after the Queen appeared. My Ace-high has significant showdown value in this specific line, as Minni's turn check often indicates a marginal hand or a missed draw. Betting doesn't achieve much as better hands aren't likely to fold and worse hands aren't likely to call. Therefore, I will check and hope to win at showdown if she checks behind.

I have T♠4♣ — a weak one-pair (pair of 4s) on 3♦ 8♦ 4♥ Q♥ 7♣. Line so far: I bet flop (150 into ~250) and Flash called, then both checked turn. Flash’s line looks like a medium-strength calling range (8x, 3x, small pairs, missed diamond draws, or Qx that slowed down). On the river the 7♣ is a blank for me and a lot of hands that beat me (Qx, 8x, pair+ or better) will gladly call a value bet; worse hands that would fold to a bet are relatively rare. Given pot control and stack preservation over the long session, thin-value betting here is negative EV — I’ll get value from worse very rarely and will get called by better hands more often than I’ll fold them. Checking to showdown minimizes risk and keeps my stack intact. Action: check.

98h suited connectors on the button with deep stacks (effective ~10k). Excellent position and implied odds if we hit. Pre-flop, pot 150, to call 100 (limp), min-raise 200. Standard play is to raise to isolate or build pot, as blinds defend wide but I have position. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is balanced, shows strength, and takes initiative. Folding trash, limping weak; 98s too strong to limp. Opponents from history are aggressive, so raising folds weak hands and gets value.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Trey suited. Elon (Button) has raised to 3x. While suited, this hand is too weak to call out of position against an aggressive player like Elon, especially with the Big Blind still to act. Conserving my stack for better spots is the preferred strategy here.

Elon opened to 300 from the button, Flash folded — I'm heads-up in the BB with Ks6h and a ~127BB stack. Calling 200 more (300 total committed) gives me ~30% breakeven equity to continue, which is reasonable versus a wide BTN opening. K6o is marginal but playable heads-up; I don't want to 3-bet a dominated, offsuit King out of position and bloat the pot, nor fold a hand that realizes equity cheaply. Plan: call to see a flop, play for pot control OOP — continue with top pair or strong draws, fold to heavy pressure on dry boards. So I will call.

I missed the flop (K♠6♥ vs A♣ Q♣ J♦). I'm out of position and the board heavily favors the button’s raising range (lots of A‑x, Q‑x, Jx and broadways). I have no pair and no real draw, so I should keep the pot small and not bluff OOP into an aggressive opponent. Check for pot control and to see Elon’s action. Plan: fold to a substantial c‑bet; consider calling a very small c‑bet as a float only if I can credibly take the pot away on the turn or pick up equity (pairing a K, runner club, or straight cards); if he checks back I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (bet only if I improve or get a very favorable card).

Heads-up on the flop with 98hh after my preflop button raise to 300 and Minni's BB call (her range is wide including Ax, suited broadways, clubs, connectors). Board AcQcJd hits her range moderately but is draw-heavy (flush draw, gutshots, open-enders like KT, T9). I have a gutshot straight draw to the 10 (making 89TJQ straight) for ~9% equity on turn, backdoor flush draw, and great position. Pot 650, as preflop aggressor with fold equity vs her check (often weak or marginal), semi-bluff c-bet to take it down or build pot for implied odds when deep (~12-17BB effective no, stacks deep 100+BB). Minni sticky historically but folds to continued aggression often. Bet 2/3 pot (~400) balances value (overpairs, AJ/QJ) and bluffs. Checking realizes equity but loses initiative and initiative advantage on scary board.

I called preflop to realize equity cheaply, but I completely missed this flop. On A♣ Q♣ J♦ my K♠6♥ has no pair, no club backdoor and only three clean king outs — essentially no meaningful equity versus a BTN c‑bet range that contains lots of A‑x, Q‑x, J‑x, K‑T straights and club draws. Elon’s bet is 400 into a 1050 pot, so I’m getting ~28% pot odds (400 / 1450). My realistic equity vs his raising/c‑betting range is well below that (likely in the low teens), and I’m out of position with no fold equity and meaningful reverse implied odds if I call. Raising as a bluff is too thin and will just bloat the pot OOP. Preserve the stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

I am on the button with 9s6h. This is a weak hand. While being on the button gives me a positional advantage, 9-high offsuit is generally a fold in most pre-flop scenarios, especially when there are aggressive players like Elon and Minni at the table. I will fold and wait for a better spot to commit chips.

Heads-up from the small blind with Jc5h and deep stacks (~125bb). With the button folded I should be opening a wide HU range to seize initiative — J5o is marginal but fine to raise here rather than passively completing and playing OOP. Table standard open sizes have been ~3x the big blind; I’ll raise to 300 total to apply pressure, reduce cheap defenses, and give myself the initiative to c-bet favorable flops. If Elon 3-bets I’ll fold this holding; if he calls I’ll continuation-bet on dry flops and be cautious on Ace/King-high or coordinated boards. Raising to 300 maximizes EV in this spot compared with a passive call or folding.

A2 suited in BB facing a standard raise to 300 from SB after BTN folds. Pot is 400, to call 200 (3:1 direct odds, plus implied odds). Minni raises frequently pre-flop from history (often steals or plays aggressively), so her range is likely wide including weaker aces, suited hands, broadway, pairs. A2h blocks AA/AKs and plays well suited for flush draws/straight potential. Post-flop OOP but deep stacks (17k effective), good implied odds if connect with top pair or draw. Folding too tight vs SB open; 3-bet bluff marginal as A2s isn't ideal blocker and OOP 3bet complicates. Standard defend by calling for multiway potential (though heads-up) and control pot.

I was the preflop aggressor and I opened to seize initiative. The flop A-T-4 is an Ace‑high board that interacts well with a calling BB range (lots of Ax, broadways and some pairs), so my J♣5♥ has very little showdown value — I can’t rely on cards to win at showdown. In position I want to apply measured pressure to fold out many of the missed hands (Kx, Qx, small pairs, unpaired broadways) while keeping the pot manageable if called. I’ll make a small c‑bet ~1/3 pot (200 into 600). Plan: if Elon folds I take the pot; if he calls I will proceed cautiously and re‑evaluate on the turn; if he raises big (shows strong value or very large pressure) I’ll fold given my lack of equity.

Top pair top kicker? No, pair of aces (Ah pairs Ad) with weak 2 kicker, but still strong top pair on Ad Ts 4s board. Board has spade flush draw and some straight draws (e.g., J5, KJ gutshots). Facing small 200 bet into 800 pot (25% pot), Minni's SB c-bet range is wide: Ax (worse kickers blocked by my Ah), sets unlikely, flush draws, pair Ts/4s, bluffs. Excellent pot odds to call (4:1), but OOP as BB, better to raise for value from worse Ax/draws, protection vs draws, charge them. Deep stacks (12k effective), implied odds if ahead. Minni aggressive history but folds to resistance sometimes (e.g., hands 49,52,55). Raise to 800 (4x her bet, ~pot-sized post-call), builds pot while keeping fold equity vs draws/marginal. Avoid flat OOP allowing cheap turns/draws completing.

I opened preflop and c‑bet 200 into a ~600 pot on the A‑T‑4 rainbow/paired spade flop to take the initiative. Elon’s reraise to 800 makes the pot 1600 and requires me to call 600 to continue. Calling 600 to win 2200 (post‑call pot) requires ~27% equity. J♣5♥ has almost no showdown value on A‑T‑4 and no meaningful draws (no two‑spade combo, no straight draw), so its equity versus a typical BB 3‑betting range here (lots of Ax, Tx, sets, some two‑pair/strong combos and only occasional semi‑bluffs) is well below that threshold (single‑digit to low‑teens % at best). Elon’s sizing is polarizing and I’ve seen him play aggressively/value heavy — he doesn’t need to be bluffing enough to make this a profitable call. With poor equity, no reliable fold equity on later streets and deep stacks that would commit me to difficult decisions, the correct play is to preserve my stack and fold.

I’m on the button with K♦8♥ in a three-handed pot (blinds 50/100). Pot is 150 and it’s cheap to see the flop, but I want to take the initiative and use position. K8o is well within a standard button opening range in 3-handed play — it blocks some strong Kx hands and plays well postflop. I’ll open to 3x the big blind (300 total) to maximize fold equity against the blinds and to play postflop in position if called. Plan: vs a single call continue with c-bets on many dry or King-high flops; fold to a sizable 3-bet out of position with this marginal hand and re-evaluate if action and sizing suggest a bluff or light call.

Pocket Jacks (JJ) is a premium hand pre-flop, especially against a button raise which is likely wide-ranging including many weaker hands, Ax, suited connectors, etc. From the small blind, out of position, a 3-bet is standard to isolate the button, build the pot for value, and deny equity to worse hands. Historical play shows an aggressive table with frequent raises, but JJ crushes villain's calling range. Raising to 900 (3x the 300 raise) is a balanced size, committing 900 total to the pot (50 SB already posted + 850 more). This pressures Minni to fold her weak opens while allowing sets or better to continue. Calling risks multi-way pot OOP; folding is too tight.

I am in the Big Blind with 7-6 offsuit. The action has been a raise from Minni on the button to 300 and a 3-bet from Elon in the Small Blind to 900. 7-6 offsuit is far too weak to defend against a 3-bet, especially when I would be out of position against the 3-better. Folding is the only sensible play here to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

I opened to 300 on the button with K♦8♥ and Elon 3-bet to 900 from the SB. Folding is too weak here — I'm in position and the price to continue is reasonable — and 4-betting with K8o would be spewy and unnecessarily polarizing. Pot is ~1300 and I must call 600 more, which gives me roughly 31–32% immediate pot‑odds; combined with positional advantage, K‑8o has enough playability and blocker value to justify continuing. Plan: call to 900 total, see a flop in position, and play the turn/river based on texture — c‑bet on many dry or King‑high flops, check and control the pot on heavy/ACE‑high or coordinated boards, and fold to extreme aggression or a large 4‑bet shove. This keeps the pot manageable while preserving post‑flop leverage.

Flop 3s 3c Js gives me Jacks full of threes (Jd Jc with Js 3s 3c), an extremely strong full house, near the nuts (only beaten by 33 for quads). Heads-up vs Minni's button calling range after my SB 3-bet, which includes Ax, broadway, mid pairs, suited connectors, but rarely 33. Board is paired and dry with backdoor flush/straight draws, but my hand crushes her value range (AJs, KJs for two pair/trips, smaller pairs, bluffs). OOP first to act, betting for value while protecting against draws and charging worse. Pot 1900, bet ~80% pot (1500) to get value from weaker hands/draws without scaring off too many, leaving room for river play. Her stack 11150 allows calling.

Elon 3‑bet preflop and now fires a large c‑bet (1500 into the ~1900 pre‑bet pot). Board 3♠ 3♣ J♠ — I have K♦8♥: no pair, no backdoor flush/straight, only 6 clean outs to make a pair. To call 1500 I’d need ~31% equity (1500/(3400+1500) ≈ 30.6%), while my two‑card chance to hit by river is only ~24% — not enough. Given Elon's preflop 3‑bet + sizable flop bet, his range is weighted to Jx/overpairs/sets and he won't fold enough to a raise for a profitable bluff. Preserving a healthy stack and waiting for a better spot is the +EV line here.

On the button in a 3-handed pot preflop with Ac7o, a strong playable hand with position and ace blocker. Blinds posted (pot 150), no raises yet. Standard to raise wide from button to steal blinds or take initiative. Deep stacks allow postflop playability. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is a balanced size to isolate or build pot.

In a 3-handed game, an Ace in the Small Blind is generally strong enough to continue against a Button open. Although Ah4d is a relatively weak Ace-x hand, the pot odds (250 to call into a pot of 450) and the nature of short-handed play make folding too tight. While 3-betting is an option to regain the initiative, Elon has shown significant aggression recently, and I prefer to see a flop and minimize variance with a marginal hand rather than bloat the pot out of position.

Call to 300 total (i.e. call 200 more). Facing a 300 open from the button with the SB already calling, A♠3♦ is marginal but has a useful Ace blocker and acceptable playability multiway. Pot is 700 so I’m getting ~28.6% to call; folding is too tight and a small 3-bet here is thin — it’s unlikely to fold out both opponents and would commit us out of position vs the button. I’ll call, see a flop, and proceed cautiously: value-bet or pot-control with any top pair (watch kicker) and fold to heavy aggression when I miss.

On a paired board of Kh-Ks-6s, my Ace-high has some showdown value but is very vulnerable in a three-way pot. Since I am out of position against both the pre-flop aggressor (Elon) and the Big Blind (Minni), the most standard and prudent play is to check. This allows me to gauge the strength of my opponents' hands based on their actions. Betting here would likely only be called or raised by hands that beat me (like any King, pocket pairs, or 6x), and I have very little equity to continue if I'm raised. I will check and see how the action develops.

Flop is K♥ K♠ 6♠ and I hold A♠ 3♦. This is a three‑way pot where the BTN opened and both SB and I called. I only have Ace‑high with a backdoor spade and an Ace blocker to some strong AK combos, but I have no pair and a very vulnerable kicker. Betting into two opponents on a paired‑King board is risky — a c‑bet will rarely fold out hands with a King or strong draws and exposes me to raises where I’m dominated. I’ll check for pot control, preserve my stack, and only continue vs reasonable-sized c‑bets (call small bets, fold to large aggression). If checked to the turn I’ll re-evaluate based on the card (bet or check/call showdown).

Preflop raiser with Ac7d on a KK6s flop, checked to me in position with pot of 900. Board is paired kings with a spade flush draw, favoring strong made hands but also vulnerable to draws. My ace-high has decent blocker value against AK/AQ and no draws of my own, making it a good semi-bluff c-bet candidate. Opponents' calling ranges from blinds include weak kings, Ax suited spades, and speculative hands that I can charge. Deep stacks (mine 18k+, pot 900) allow for bet sizing; 600 is about 2/3 pot, standard for value/protection/denying equity while keeping pot manageable if check-raised. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws or weak hands. History shows opponents check/call stations, so betting extracts value from worse.

On a paired board of Kh-Ks-6s, my Ace-high (Ah4d) has very little equity against Elon's continuation bet, especially in a three-way pot. Elon, as the pre-flop aggressor, is likely to have many Kings, pocket pairs, or even better Ace-high hands in his range. Calling 600 chips (40% of the pot) with only Ace-high and a weak kicker, while still having Minni left to act behind me, is too risky and likely negative EV. Since I don't have a spade for a flush draw and the board is quite dry for my specific holding, folding is the most disciplined and prudent play to preserve my stack.

I fold. I’m OOP (BB) with A♠3♦ on K♥K♠6♠ after checking the flop; Elon (preflop raiser) bets a large 600 into ~1500 and Flash has folded, so it’s heads‑up. Calling 600 needs ~28.6% equity. Given Elon’s preflop raise + sizable flop bet, his range is heavily weighted toward hands that already beat me (any K, sets, strong pairs/overpairs, some AX, and value combos) — my only real outs are remote backdoor spades (need both turn+river) or catching runner‑runner two‑pair, so my true equity is well below the required threshold. I planned to check for pot control and fold to heavy aggression when I missed; this is that spot. Folding preserves my stack and avoids being dominated on later streets.

On the button with Q9o, I'm in a good position to open-raise and take control of the hand. While Q9o isn't a premium hand, it's strong enough to open in a three-handed game from the button. Minni (SB) and Elon (BB) have both been active, but a standard raise to 3x the big blind (300 total) is a typical play to fold out the blinds or play in position post-flop.

I’m in the small blind with K♦T♣ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, to call 250). KTs is strong enough to play aggressively from the SB — I don’t want to limp-call and play multiway out of position with a hand that’s often dominated. I’ll 3-bet to take the initiative, gain fold equity and either isolate the button or define ranges preflop. A raise to 900 (≈3x the open) is a standard size here: it applies pressure, is big enough to discourage cheap multiway flops, but doesn’t overcommit me with a marginal holding. Plan: fold to a large 4‑bet, proceed postflop with position-based c-bets vs single caller and re-evaluate if both players continue.

5s8s is a speculative suited connector hand with decent playability multiway but poor equity against a strong 3-bet range from Minni after Flash's button open. I'm OOP in the BB, pot odds to call 800 into 1300 (~2.6:1) require solid equity, but vs likely JJ+, AK range, it's marginal at best and worse if Flash continues. Folding preserves my deep stack for better spots in this long game (hand 89/150). No history suggests Minni is overly loose here.

I opened from the button with Q9o, which is a standard play in a 3-handed game. Minni has 3-bet me from the small blind. While I have position, Q9o is a weak hand to continue with against a 3-bet, especially since Minni has shown a high frequency of 3-betting my button opens in recent hands. I will fold and wait for a better spot to avoid being dominated or forced to play a large pot with a marginal hand.

I’m on the button with QcTc (a well-connected, suited broadway) and both blinds are still to act. With pot = 150 and only 100 to call, this is a prime spot to take the initiative: raise to pick up the blinds or play a multiway pot in position. I’ll make a standard 3xBB-sized open to 300 (total) — big enough to generate fold equity against the SB/BB but small relative to our 11250 stack so we keep postflop maneuverability. If called, we’ll use positional advantage to see flops, c-bet favorable textures and extract value where appropriate. If faced with a large 3-bet (a polarizing shove or a >~900 sizing), I’ll fold to preserve stack; I’ll consider calling smaller 3-bets to realize QTs’s playability. Overall raising maximizes expected value here compared to simply limping or folding.

K9o is a decent speculative hand against a button raise, which is likely wide given Minni's aggressive history (frequent preflop raises with marginal holdings). Equity vs a broad button opening range (~30-40% hands) is around 40-45%, and pot odds are favorable: calling 250 into 450 gives ~35% implied odds needed, with deep stacks for postflop play. Out of position but king-high blocker and playability make it a standard defend. Raising is too strong for this hand OOP; folding is too tight in SB vs BTN. Flash in BB may check or squeeze, but we'll see. Plan to play postflop cautiously, c-bet defense or fold to aggression if miss.

I am in the Big Blind holding 8h 2c. Minni raised from the button to 300, and Elon called from the small blind. I need to call 200 more to see a flop in a pot that would be 900. While the pot odds are 3.5:1, 82o is a very weak hand and plays poorly post-flop, especially out of position against two active opponents. Folding is the most disciplined and standard play here to preserve my stack.

Top pair with a weak kicker (Kh9c on Kd-6h-2s) on a dry rainbow board. This is a strong hand that beats most of Minni's button raising range, which is likely wide (pairs, suited connectors, broad Kx, Ax, etc.). Few draws or strong hands in her range hit this board hard; sets are possible but rare. OOP first to act heads-up after Flash folded pre. Betting protects against draws (e.g., 54, 43, any 7), gets value from worse Kx (KJ-KT, suited), floats, and weaker pairs. Checking risks giving free cards or facing a tough decision later. Minni's history shows aggression, but she often calls or folds to c-bets. Bet 2/3 pot (450) sizes well for value/thin value, denies equity, builds pot with our deep stacks (effective ~10k). Raising pre was fine with K-blocker; now value bet. Plan: If she calls, continue aggression on turns without higher cards/draws; barrel or check turns with villainous cards.

I opened to 300 on the button and Elon (SB) called. Flop Kd 6h 2s is very dry and he C‑bets 450 (~60% into the pot). I have QcTc — missed the flop but have 6 clean outs (3 queens + 3 tens) plus backdoor club/straight potential and I'm in position. Pot after his bet is 1150; calling 450 to see the turn gives me a pot of ~1600, so I need roughly 28% equity to justify a call. Versus a SB calling/c‑betting range my combined two‑card equity + positional realization is around that break‑even point, and calling keeps the pot manageable while allowing me to fold to heavy turn aggression or extract value when I improve. Raising as a bluff is too risky (I don't block Ks and will often be dominated by Kx/pairs), and folding surrenders too much equity. Plan: call 450, re-evaluate on the turn — bet/raise for value if I pair Q/T or see a favourable card, fold to large turn pressure if I miss.

Turn Ace of Diamonds is a disaster card for our top pair weak kicker. It brings the nut flush draw completion potential (Kd-Ad), hits tons of Ax in Minni's button calling range (AK, AQ, AJ, AT, A suited), and improves any Adx to two pair or better. Our K9 blocks some KK/AA but is crushed by most value hands she continues with. Flop call range was wide (Kx, pairs, draws), but turn improves her strong hands massively while adding draws. OOP first to act into 1600 pot with deep stacks but vulnerable hand; betting risks getting raised off equity or called by better. Thin value bets get called by worse? Unlikely post-Ace; mostly sets/monsters raise, Ax call. Checking allows her to bluff or value bet, where we can fold profitably to aggression (as showdown value is low anyway). Her history shows she bets value but also bluffs; however, checking extracts max value by inducing bluffs while protecting. Plan: Check, fold to bet unless tiny; check behind weak hands fine as river stacks allow fold equity exploitation if she checks.

Elon led the flop and I called; he checks the Ace on the turn. My QcTc is a complete miss on this K-6-2-A board with only 6 clean outs (3 queens + 3 tens), ~13% to improve on the river and no realistic backdoor flush/straight now. Betting the turn as a bluff is thin — his check can be pot‑control with Kx or Ax or simply a giveup, and I have very little fold equity and essentially no value to extract. With position and such poor equity, I should keep the pot small and realize my outs — check back the turn, see the river, and only take aggressive action if I actually pair up (or face a very small river bet I can call).

River 3h is a relatively blank card that doesn't significantly change the board dynamics—no new straights complete (board A-K-6-3-2 has no five-card straight possible), no flush completes (only two diamonds and two hearts on board). My Kh9c remains top pair with a marginal kicker (beats KJ-K8, QJ floats, weaker pairs like 66/22/TT/99 preflop callers, some bluffs/draws). Villain's flop call of my 450 c-bet from button was wide (Kx, Ax, pairs, gutshots like 43/54, diamond draws), but her turn check-behind after my check caps her range somewhat—no AK/AQ/AJs (would often bet turn for value/protection on Ad), leaving more medium strength (worse Kx, mid pairs, missed draws). Aggressive history suggests she continues with speculative hands but folds weaker to pressure; check-behind indicates not super strong. Pot 1600, deep effective stacks (~10k). Betting 1100 (2/3 pot) gets thin value from worse (KQ/KT/KJ, 77-99, Ax showdown like AQo), protection vs any unlikely river draws/gutshots, denies her equity with air. Checking risks free showdown (loses to Ax, better K) or her bluff/value bet where we fold, but bet exploits her capped range post-check-behind, builds pot OOP. Folds crushed (AA/KK rare), calls by better manageable with fold equity and SPR. Standard value bet to maximize stack.

Board K♦ 6♥ 2♠ A♦ 3♥ — I have Q♣T♣: no pair, no backdoor. Elon called my preflop raise, c‑bet the dry flop and then checked the Ace on the turn: that line is consistent with a range full of Kx, Ax and pocket pairs (many of which beat my Q‑high). He now bets 1,100 into the 1,600 pot (pot is 2,700 including his bet). To call 1,100 and win 3,800 I need ≈29% equity, but I only beat rare pure bluffs (missed floats/air) and don’t block his most likely value combos. A raise as a bluff is thin — I have poor blockers and would be risking a large chunk of my 10,500 stack for little fold equity. Calling is negative EV against his line and sizing, so I fold and preserve chips for better spots.

On the button in a 3-handed pot with T8o (offsuit but decent connectors). Deep stacks (204bb effective). Position is excellent for realizing equity post-flop. Blinds are loose-aggressive based on history (frequent raises and calls). Raising to 300 (3x BB) is standard to attempt a steal or build a pot OOP for opponents while maintaining initiative. Folding too tight, calling invites multiway pots OOP. Pot odds good but raise has higher EV.

I have been dealt pocket Queens (Qd Qc) in the Small Blind. This is a premium hand. Elon, who has been active and has a large stack, opened with a raise to 300 from the Button. I need to build the pot and protect my hand, especially out of position. A 3-bet is the standard and most profitable play here. I will raise to 1000 to put pressure on Elon and potentially the Big Blind (Minni), while also defining my hand strength.

Button (Elon) opened to 300 and the small blind (Flash) 3‑bet to 1000. I’m in the big blind with A♠3♣. Pot is 1400 and it’s 900 to call (1000 total committed), min‑raise to 1700. 

Decision logic:
- A3 offsuit is a marginal hand here: dominated by most Ax combos and by many hands in a normal SB 3‑bet range (QQ+, AK, AQ, and some suited Axs). My kicker is weak and I’ll run into domination a lot.  
- Pot odds to call are poor: I must invest 900 to win 2300 (need ≈39% equity). Versus a realistic 3‑bet range my equity is well under that, and if the button calls it becomes a multiway pot which further reduces my relative equity and leaves me out of position to the button.  
- 4‑bet bluff to 1700 is thin here — I don’t have enough blocker/fold equity to credibly take it down against two players and I don’t want to commit more with such a marginal holding. A shove would be wildly overleveraged and exploitable.  
- Table dynamics: no strong read that Flash is 3‑betting super wide from the SB to justify a call or light 4‑bet with A3o. With deep stacks, preserving chips for better spots is preferable.

Given domination risk, poor pot odds and positional liabilities, the +EV play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

T8o is a speculative hand with good playability, suited connectors-like (offsuit but connected). Facing a 3-bet from aggressive SB Flash after my button open. Deep stacks (80bb+ effective). Position advantage post-flop is key; calling gets good pot odds (~2:1 direct, building to larger pot IP). Flash's history shows wide ranges and bluffs (frequent raises, light calls/showdowns). Folding loses too much EV from wide opening range; 4-bet risks bloating pot OOP for him or commitment with marginal hand. Call to realize equity IP, control pot, use implied odds vs his aggro tendencies.

I hold an overpair (pocket Queens) on a coordinated flop of 8d-Js-9d. While this board connects with many parts of Elon's 3-bet calling range (like JTs, QJs, 88, 99, or diamond draws), I still likely have the best hand and need to protect it while extracting value from weaker Jacks and various draws. I have the Queen of diamonds, which blocks some of the nut flush draw combinations. I will place a continuation bet of 1100, which is roughly half the pot, to maintain initiative and charge drawing hands.

Flop hits perfectly with top pair (eights) plus open-ended straight draw (8-9-10-J, 8 outs to straight, plus pair for more equity). Roughly 35-45% equity vs. Flash's 3-bet range (JJ, AJs, KQs, bluffs like Axs, blockers). Board is draw-heavy (diamond flush draw, backdoor spades), so protection needed. Position on aggressive SB who c-bets wide; his 55% pot bet reps value or semi-bluff but range over-realizes less than mine. Raising builds pot for value from worse pairs/draws (e.g., Jx, Tx, 9x, flush draws), applies fold equity to air/overs, denies equity. Call too passive, invites free river draw completion OOP for him. Fold absurd with this strength. Size to 2500 (2.25x his bet, ~3/4 pot post-raise) balances value/protection without overcommitting effective stacks (~49bb behind). History shows Flash folds to post-flop pressure when behind.

I am facing a raise to 2500 from Elon after my continuation bet of 1100 on an 8d-Js-9d board. My pocket Queens are still an overpair, and I hold the Qd which is a crucial blocker to the nut flush draw. However, this board is extremely wet and hits Elon's 3-bet calling range hard (specifically sets like 88, 99, or two-pair combos like J9s). The pot is currently 5700 and it costs me 1400 to call, giving me roughly 4:1 odds. Folding an overpair here would be too exploitable given the price, but raising further might only isolate me against hands that beat me. Calling allows me to see the turn and reassess the strength of my hand depending on the next card, while keeping Elon's bluffs or semi-bluffs (like QT, T9, or diamond draws) in the pot.

On the turn, the 4d completes a flush for my pocket Queens (Qd Qc). The board is now 8d-Js-9d-4d. While the Ad and Kd are still potential threats, a Queen-high flush is extremely strong in this 3-bet pot. On the flop, Elon raised my continuation bet, which indicates he likely has a very strong hand like a set (88, 99, JJ), two pair (J9), or a powerful draw. Now that the flush has arrived, many of his value hands are in a difficult spot. By checking, I allow Elon to continue his aggression or attempt to represent the flush himself if he was semi-bluffing. Given that my remaining stack (5600) is less than the pot (7100), I am looking to get all the money in, but checking is the best way to induce a bet from his range.

Turn board 4d completes some diamond flushes (those with two diamonds who flatted flop raise) but is somewhat blank otherwise. My hand remains very strong: top pair top kicker (8h over 8d) + open-ended straight draw (8 outs to 7-8-9-T-J straight, near-nuts). Equity ~45%+ vs Flash's 3-bet/call range (JJ+, AJs, KQs, sets, bluffs/draws that held up). Flash checks after calling flop raise on connected/drawy board; likely weak Jx (AJ/KJ/QJ), 9x, missed draws, or trap with set/flush/straight. History shows Flash aggressive pre/flop but folds post-flop pressure when behind (multiple examples). Position IP, pot 7100, his stack 5600 (~80% pot). Value bet thinner Jx/draws, protect vs river draws, deny equity. Half-pot ~3500 builds pot, leaves him ~2100 behind for river jam possible but callable with equity/SPR low. Check risks giving free card to draws or check-raise trap, lower EV long-term.

On the turn, the 4 of diamonds completes my Queen-high flush. While the Ace and King of diamonds are still potential concerns, a Queen-high flush is an extremely strong hand in a 3-bet pot. Elon's aggressive line (raising the flop and betting the turn) suggests he has a very strong range, including sets, two pairs, or straights, most of which I am now beating. My current stack of 5600 is less than the current pot size, making me pot-committed. I will move all-in to maximize value from his sets or lower flushes and to ensure I realize my equity before the river.

Facing Flash's check-raise all-in to 5600 total on turn (2100 more into 16200 pot, ~8:1 odds). My 8hTs holds top pair top kicker + OESD (8 outs to nuts straight, ~17% turn/river, plus pair equity). Board 8d-Js-9d-4d completes diamond flushes but my hand blocks 8d, Ts blocks some straights. Flash's range (3bet pre, call flop raise, check-raise): polarized to flushes (AdXd, unlikely flat flop raise), straights (Q7s, T7 suited?), sets (88/JJ/99), J9 two-pair, or bluffs (missed OESD like 75/KQ, air). History shows Flash aggressive pre/flop but folds to pressure post-flop (e.g., hands 37,82); wide ranges, light calls. Equity >40% vs polarized range (crushes Jx/9x/draws that check-raise, live vs nuts). Excellent pot odds require ~10% equity to call profitably; check-raise likely semi-bluff/value thin given his style. SPR <1 post-call, committed anyway. Folding top end of my range too nitty OOP history; call realizes massive EV.

I am on the button with A9o, which is a strong starting hand in a three-handed game. I will make a standard opening raise of 3x the big blind to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot early.

Holding Kc2c (K2 suited) in the small blind facing a 300 open from the button. To call I must put in 250 into a 450 pot (about 36% pot odds), but I’d be out of position against the raiser and still have the big blind behind who often defends or can 3-bet. K2s is easily dominated by button’s raising range and has poor postflop playability multiway or OOP. A 3-bet bluff is unnecessary and would bloat the pot OOP with a weak holding. Preserve stack and fold to wait for a better, more playable spot.

Qc2h is a very weak offsuit hand with no playability, dominated by any queen or better in Flash's likely button raising range. Facing a standard 3x raise from an aggressive button opponent, the pot odds (200 to call into 450, ~31% needed) do not justify playing this trash from the big blind out of position. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities.

I’m on the button with 3♦8♥ in a three-handed pot (SB 50 / BB 100 assumed). This is a marginal hand but in 3-max I should be opening very wide to exploit positional advantage and collect the blinds. The table’s common open-sizing has been ~300, so I’ll raise to 300 (3x BB) to maximize fold equity while keeping the pot manageable against deep stacks. Plan: fold to a serious 3‑bet with this junk; if called I’ll play postflop in position — c-bet favorable dry flops, control size on wet boards and release to heavy resistance.

Pocket 66 is a strong hand from the SB facing a button open-raise to 300. Button ranges (Minni) are typically wide in this spot based on history of frequent raises. 66 has excellent equity against a wide opening range (~55-65%) and good playability post-flop. OOP calling risks difficult spots, especially deep-stacked, but 3-betting provides fold equity, isolates vs BB, and builds pot with a premium hand. Sizing to 900 (3x the raise) is standard for value/protection. Minni folds to 3-bets often in history (e.g., Hand 26). Avoid flat-calling OOP with deep stacks where implied odds are present but position hurts. Raising maximizes EV.

I am in the Big Blind with 9d 2d. Minni (Button) raised to 300, and Elon (Small Blind) 3-bet to 900. While I have a suited hand, 92s is extremely weak and plays poorly against the strong ranges indicated by a 3-bet from the SB. My stack is healthy at over 18,000 chips, and there is no reason to risk chips out of position in a bloated pot with a bottom-tier hand. Folding is the standard and correct play here.

3♦8♥ is a very weak, often dominated hand facing a 3‑bet. Elon 3‑bet to 900 after my 300 open — calling 600 into the ~1300 pot requires ~31.5% equity to break even. Against a reasonable 3‑bet range (QQ+, AK, some JJ/TT, AQs/AJs, maybe suited broadways), 3‑8o has well under that equity and will be crushed postflop. A min‑4bet to 1500 as a bluff is too loose here given Elon’s aggression and stack depths; I don’t want to inflate the pot with junk out of position. I’ll preserve my stack and fold, look for better spots in position or against weaker defenders.

3-handed on the button first to act pre-flop with Qc9h offsuit. This is a strong position with a playable hand in a short-handed game. Q9o has decent high card value, good playability, and blockers to strong hands. Standard play is to raise for value and to steal the blinds frequently, as opponents' defending ranges are wide. Raising to 300 (3x the BB) is a standard sizing that builds the pot while maintaining fold equity. Limping is weak and invites multiway pots OOP; folding is too tight given position and hand strength. Opponent stacks are deep, but position advantage is key.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace of Spades and Six of Diamonds. Elon, the button, has raised to 300. Ace-six offsuit is a marginal but playable hand in a three-handed game, especially from the blinds. However, I am out of position for the rest of the hand. Elon has been playing aggressively recently. Calling is an option to see a flop, but folding is also reasonable given the poor playability of A6o out of position against a button raise. Given my large stack size and the desire to protect it while maintaining a solid image, I will fold this hand and wait for a better spot to engage.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with Q7o — I only need to put in 200 more (300 total) to see a flop. Calling costs 200 to win a pot of ~650, so I need ~30.8% equity to break even. Versus a button opening range Q7o has reasonable equity and decent post‑flop playability (can make top pair, backdoors, and blocks some Qx). 3‑betting here is suboptimal — Q7o is a weak bluff candidate, I don’t want to inflate a pot OOP with a marginal hand, and I lack fold equity. Folding is too tight given the pot odds. With deep stacks I’ll call and play cautiously postflop: check/call small c‑bets, fold to large sustained aggression on dry boards if I miss, and pursue value/raise when I improve to top pair or better.

Flop: 5d 5s 7s. I hold Q7 — top pair with a decent kicker. Pot is ~650 and I act first heads‑up. Button’s opening range is wide, so there are more worse one‑pair and marginal hands in his range than pure monsters (though combos like A7/K7/77/55 beat me). I want to extract value and deny free cards, so I’ll bet roughly half the pot to charge worse sevens/one‑pair hands while keeping the pot manageable if I face resistance. If he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn for value; if he check‑raises big or shoves I’ll put him on very strong hands (trips/full) and fold.

Preflop raise with Q9o from button was standard for position and stealing in 3-handed play. Flop 5d5s7s hits BB defender's calling range hard: pairs 5s/7s/trips, flush draws (spades heavy), straight draws like 65/68. My Qc9h has queen-high, zero draws, no pair—pure air with <20% equity vs realistic range. Minni's small OOP lead (325 into ~650) could be value (trips/set/draw) or thin, but size suggests strength/draw not pure bluff. Pot odds ~4:1 (325 to 1300) require ~20% equity, which I lack without fold equity. Raising bluff risks stacks deep (Minni ~9k covered), and board texture favors her calling station tendencies seen in history (e.g., Hand 43 river call). Calling bloats pot OOP next with trash. Best: fold, preserve stack for better spots over 150 hands.
I'm on the button with Ac4c, which is a strong starting hand in a three-handed game. I'll raise to 300 to take control of the pot and potentially take down the blinds.

Flash opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the small blind with Kc4s — a weak offsuit king that is frequently dominated by the button’s opening range and plays poorly out of position. Pot is 450 and I must call 250 more (300 total) or raise to at least 500. Elon (BB) still has to act, so calling risks a multiway pot OOP with very little equity or postflop maneuverability. A 3-bet bluff isn’t attractive either (poor blockers, risk of a 4-bet or getting isolation behind). Preserve my stack and fold — wait for a better spot or position.

Qd4d is a suited queen in the BB facing a button min-raise to 300 after SB folds. Button opens wide from late position, often stealing blinds.
