Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into 450 (pot becomes 650), requiring ~31% equity, which Q4s achieves against a typical 30-40% button opening range due to flush and straight potential, live cards, and postflop playability despite OOP. Folding a suited Qx OOP is too tight; 3-betting has some fold equity but Q4s isn't a great 3bet candidate without strong blockers. Prefer calling to realize equity and control pot OOP with position advantage later if multiway (though SB folded). Stack depths support speculative defend (11k effective vs 18k). Long-term EV positive play.

Flop board 2d Th 3h is somewhat drawy with two hearts and low cards, but uncoordinated. Qd4d gives queen-high with backdoor diamond flush draw and gutshot wheel draw (to 5 for 2-3-4-5), plus live queen. No made hand. Vs button's wide range (Ax-Kx suited/offsuit, broadway, pairs, suited connectors), we have decent showdown equity OOP but vulnerable to c-bets. Preflop call was speculative for multiway potential/set-mining/flush equity realization, now single-way. Standard OOP check to pot control, induce bluffs, realize equity. Donk betting too thin without strong draw/pair. Will check-call small bets (~1/3-1/2 pot) for odds/live cards/backdoors; fold to large bets or raises. Stacks deep (11bb effective? Wait, 11k at 50/100 is 110bb, very deep), supports seeing turn. Long-term +EV vs aggressive button.

On this dry, low board (Th-3h-2d), as the pre-flop aggressor on the button, I have a range advantage. Elon checked to me after calling from the big blind, which often signifies a weak or capped range. Although I only have Ace-high, I have a backdoor nut flush draw and a backdoor straight draw. A standard continuation bet of about half the pot should be enough to take the pot down immediately or set up a barrel on later streets if the board develops in my favor.

Facing a small 1/3 pot c-bet of 325 into 975 on a drawy flop (2d Th 3h) with Qd4d for queen-high, gutshot straight draw to the wheel (needing a 5), backdoor diamond flush draw, and a live queen. Preflop call was speculative and correct OOP vs button open. Button's wide continuing range (Ax suited/offsuit, broadway, small pairs, suited connectors) hits this board somewhat but often weakly; many bluffs/missed draws. Pot odds excellent: calling 325 to win 1300 (~25% equity needed), and Q4d realizes 30-40%+ vs polarized cbet range with showdown value, implied odds deep-stacked (110bb effective), and backdoors for turn development. Raising thin OOP risks bloating pot with marginal hand and poor postflop playability; folding too tight given odds and live cards. Check-call realizes equity, controls pot, induces bluffs on turn, sets up folds to bigger bets if behind. Consistent with preflop/flop plan vs aggressive button (Flash raises/folds frequently in history). +EV long-term.

On turn board 2d Th 3h 7c, holding Qd4d for queen-high (no pair, no completed draw; previous gutshot to 5 unimproved, backdoor diamond draw dead). OOP vs button aggressor Flash who c-bet flop small into drawy board. Pot 1300, deep effective stacks (~10k). Queen-high has ~25-35% equity vs his polarized turn range (pairs 88-JJ, draws like heart flush draws, missed broadway, bluffs with Ax suited/offsuit, connectors). No value to lead OOP with marginal showdown hand; thin value-bet gets called by better, check allows pot control and induces bluffs/missed draws. Raising dead money bad without strong equity. Check realizes equity, sets up cheap river showdown or fold to big bet if behind (plan: call small/medium bets for live Q + blockers, fold to large). Consistent with speculative pre/flop defense vs wide button range; history shows Flash aggressive but folds to resistance often. +EV OOP check vs exploitative c-bettor.

The 7c on the turn is a relatively blank card. I currently hold Ace-high with a gutshot straight draw (to the wheel). Since Elon checked to me again, I have the option to continue betting or take a free card. While a double barrel could potentially fold out some of Elon's weaker pairs or floats, checking back allows me to realize my equity and potentially hit my straight or an Ace on the river without risking a check-raise. Given the board texture and my position, a check back is a solid, lower-variance play.

River board 2d Th 3h 7c 7h completes heart flush draws and pairs the sevens. Holding Qd4d for queen-high (no pair, no flush, no straight), pure showdown value vs bluffs/missed draws from Flash's wide button range that cbets flop small and checks turn (suggesting medium strength like Ax/Kx/Tx/small pair or missed draw, less likely strong value that checks turn). OOP in BB after speculative pre/flop defense vs aggressive button. Pot 1300, deep stacks (~10k effective). Betting thin value or bluff bad: queen-high gets called by better, bluffs too thin OOP on scary river vs opponent who can have flushes/77/TPTK. Checking realizes equity: induces bluffs/bets from worse (J-high, missed hearts/diamonds), pot control, cheap showdown if he checks behind (common vs checked turn). Plan: check, call small-medium bets (~1/3-1/2 pot) with live Q/blockers/live cards equity ~30%+ vs polarized range; fold to large bets. Consistent with history of Flash's aggression but folding to resistance; exploits by not bloating pot OOP with marginal hand. +EV long-term.

On the river, the board has paired with the Seven of Hearts, which also completes a possible heart flush. Elon has checked for a third time. My Ace-high (Ac4c) has some showdown value against missed straight draws like 54 or 65. However, since Elon called my flop bet, he likely has at least a small piece of the board (a Ten, Trey, or Deuce) or was on a draw. If he has a pair, he is unlikely to fold to a single river bet after I checked the turn. If he has a missed draw, I win by checking anyway. Given the board texture and the action, checking back is the most prudent play to realize my showdown value without risking more chips against a potential better hand or a check-trap.

I'm on the button with J♣3♥ — a weak, unsuited hand. Pot is only 150 and the cost to see the flop is 100 (very cheap relative to my 10,350 stack). I prefer to take advantage of position rather than iso-raise with such a poor holding: calling keeps the pot small, lets me realize equity in position, and lets me fold to significant aggression if either blind shows strength. If I connect (a jack or backdoor draws) I can play the hand aggressively; if I miss I'll check and fold to big bets. A raise to steal is an option in 3-handed play, but J3o is too marginal to risk building a bigger pot out of position against possible 3-bets. Call 100.

Preflop in small blind with Jd5h offsuit, a weak speculative hand with poor playability. Minni has limped from the button, pot is 250, and I need to call 50 more, but facing a likely multiway pot with no position postflop. Flash in BB will likely check or raise. Hand has minimal equity against calling ranges and doesn't justify the investment. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots after reviewing opponent tendencies in prior hands where loose calls led to trouble.

Minni limped from the button and the small blind folded. I am in the big blind with Queen-Six suited. While this hand is decent, there is no need to bloat the pot out of position against a limp which could occasionally be a trap, although it's likely a marginal hand. I will check and see the flop for free to realize my equity.

On this J-8-2 rainbow board, I have missed completely with my Queen-Six diamonds. However, in a limped pot, the range of the button (Minni) is often quite wide and weak. Checking is the standard play here to see if I can improve on the turn for free or potentially take the pot away later if the action remains checked down. There is very little value in betting here as a bluff without any real equity or backdoors.

I have top pair (J♣3♥) but a very weak kicker on a 2♣-8♥-J♠ flop. It's heads-up and Flash checked, so I should take the initiative in position to (1) get value from worse jacks and weaker hands, (2) deny free cards to potential backdoor draws, and (3) keep pot control since my kicker is poor. A half-pot bet balances extracting value and protecting the hand without committing too much of my deep stack — if I face a sizable raise I can fold and preserve stack with a marginal holding. Bet half-pot (125).

I have Queen-high on a Jack-high rainbow board with no straight or flush draws. Minni limped pre-flop and is now betting half-pot. While the price to call is small (125 chips to win a total pot of 500), I have almost no equity or potential to improve aside from hitting a Queen, and I would be playing the rest of the hand out of position. It is better to fold now and wait for a more favorable spot rather than floating with a weak hand.

3-handed on the button with K8o, an offsuit king-high hand that is well within the profitable opening range from this position. Button opens wide against blinds, and history shows frequent opens to 300. Raising standard size of 300 for value and fold equity against likely weak blind defenses. Limping is passive and suboptimal; folding too tight. Deep stacks support this play to build pot OOP advantage postflop.

I am in the Small Blind with 9h 3d, which is a very weak hand. Elon has raised from the Button. Defending such a weak hand out of position against a range that likely dominates it is not profitable. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Facing a BTN open to 300 from my big blind with Tc3s (offsuited). Pot is 450 and I must call 200 to continue — I’d need about 200/(450+200)=200/650 ≈ 30.8% equity to break even. Tc3s is severely dominated by a standard BTN opening range and performs poorly out of position postflop. A light 3-bet bluff here would be thin (Elon has shown aggression and will call or shove enough to make it unprofitable) and calling OOP with this rag is a losing play long-term. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot, so I fold.

I am on the button with Queen-Jack offsuit, which is a strong starting hand in a three-handed game. I will put in a standard open-raise to 300 to take control of the hand and build the pot while in position.

Fold. Facing a button open to 300 from the small blind with K♣5♥ off (K♥5♠) I’m out of position and very often dominated by the opener’s range (lots of Kx/broadways). Calling 250 into a 450 pot requires ~35.7% equity to break even — K5o has well under that vs a reasonable open and will have poor playability OOP postflop. A light 3‑bet to 500 would be a pure bluff with mediocre blockers and invites a cold 4‑bet or a call from the BB, creating tougher spots. With deep stacks and many better spots to invest chips, folding is the lowest variance + highest EV choice here.

93h is a marginal suited hand at best, with a large gap and low high-card strength. Facing a button raise from an aggressive player like Flash, who frequently opens wide from late position and often takes it down uncontested (e.g., Hand 92, 95). Out of position in BB, post-flop playability is poor without hits, and equity vs. a typical button ~35-40% range is around 35%, borderline even with pot odds of ~3:1 (200 into 450). No strong blockers or polarization for a 3-bet bluff OOP. Folding preserves stack for better spots in a long session (150 hands).

I'm on the button with A6o (Ad6c) facing the small and big blinds. With only two opponents and position, A6o is a reasonable open-raise candidate: it has an Ace blocker, decent playability in position, and good fold equity. Table tendencies show standard open sizing around 300 and opponents often fold to button pressure, so I want a standard-size raise to make stealing the blinds easier while leaving room to maneuver postflop. Plan: raise to 300 total (3x BB). If both fold I pick up the blinds; if called I use position to c-bet favorable A-high or dry boards and control pot size on coordinated boards; if I face a large 3-bet I will fold A6o (it’s easily dominated), and only consider calling small 3-bets if odds and implied odds are attractive.

Pocket Tens is a strong premium hand pre-flop, well ahead of a typical button opening range, which is wide in late position. Facing a 3x BB raise from Minni on the button, a 3-bet is standard for value and to isolate, denying equity to worse hands and folding out some bluffs. Calling risks multi-way pot with poor post-flop position as SB. Folding is too tight. Sizing to 900 (3x their raise) is balanced, building the pot while leaving room for 4-bets. Effective stacks are deep (~100BB+), so TT plays well post-flop. Opponent tendencies from history show Minni raises frequently from late position but folds to aggression often.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Four offsuit. Minni opened from the button and Elon 3-bet from the small blind. While I have an Ace, it's a weak kicker and I'm facing significant strength from the small blind. Calling 800 more to play out of position in a three-way 3-bet pot with a marginal hand is not profitable. Folding is the disciplined play here to conserve my stack.

I opened to 300 on the button with Ad6c and Elon (SB) made a substantial 3‑bet to 900. Calling 600 into a 1,300 pot would put me in a marginal post‑flop spot: A6o is often dominated by the SB’s 3‑bet value range and has poor playability if I miss. A 4‑bet would be a thin bluff with poor blockers. Per my plan to fold A6o to large 3‑bets and preserve stack/position for better spots, I fold here.

3-handed on the button with Ks2h, deep stacks (~110bb effective). Pot 150 (blinds), unopened (assuming SB folded or limped minimally, but to call 100 indicates standard BB). Button open-raising range is very wide, around 45-55% of hands, including all Kxo due to position, fold equity, and king blocker. K2o plays well postflop in position. Raising to 300 (3x BB) is standard sizing to take initiative, build pot with position, or fold out blinds. Limp weak, fold too tight for maximization over remaining 50 hands.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten-Four offsuit, which is a very weak hand. Elon has raised from the button to 300. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here, as calling out of position with such a weak hand would be a significant mistake.

Elon opened to 300 from the button and I’m in the big blind with Qh6d. The pot is 450 and it’s 200 to me (300 total) — calling gives ~31% pot odds, which is reasonable to defend heads-up. Q6o is marginal but playable in the BB vs a wide button opening; it has enough equity to call and see a flop. Elon has shown frequent preflop aggression and strong postflop betting in prior hands, so 3-betting this weak, poorly‑playable hand out of position would bloat the pot and often put me in difficult spots. I’ll call to keep the pot manageable, see a flop, and then play aggressively only if I connect (top pair or good draws); otherwise control the pot or fold to heavy pressure.

Board is Jd-7d-3s and I hold Qh6d — no pair, only a small backdoor diamond and weak showdown value. Elon is an aggressive preflop and postflop bettor, so I don’t want to inflate a marginal holding out of position. I’ll check to control the pot and let him c-bet; plan is to call small-to-medium continuation bets (roughly <= ~1/2 pot) to realize equity and fold to large pressure or raises. If I improve (pair or a real draw) I’ll start playing more aggressively on later streets.

Flop of 3s7dJd is somewhat drawy with a diamond flush draw possible and some straight draw potential (e.g., T9, 86s), but low cards make it relatively dry for many BB calling ranges. Minni's check from BB vs button c-bet range suggests weakness or trapping, but history shows Minni checks and often calls small but folds to continued aggression postflop frequently (e.g., hands 52,55,58,85). With position, deep stacks (~100bb effective vs Minni's 97bb), and pot of 650, standard to c-bet wide (~65-75% frequency) for thin value/protection/fold equity. Ks2h has king-high showdown value (blocks strong KJ/KQ), backdoor spade flush potential, and decent equity vs missed draws/pairs. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws/improvers and forfeits initiative. Bet 2/3 pot (425) balances value (beats missed Ax, low pairs) with bluffs, targets Minni's fold equity, and denies equity. Pot control if called but realize position OOP later.

Preflop I defended Qh6d in the BB vs Elon's button open. The flop Jd-7d-3s completely misses me — I have Q-high and a single diamond (only a backdoor flush that requires two more diamonds). Elon bets 425 (~40% of the pot). That sizing gives me reasonable pot odds: calling 425 into the 1,075 pot makes the pot ~1,500 and requires roughly 28% equity to continue. Versus a BTN c-bet range (Jx, some diamonds, overcards and bluffs) I have enough equity to justify a call, and raising with such a weak hand has poor fold equity and just bloats the pot out of position. Plan: call to realize equity and keep the pot manageable; fold to large turn shove/raise unless I pair a Queen or pick up a meaningful draw, and convert to value aggression if I hit top pair or a strong two‑card draw.

I have Qh6d on a 3s-7d-Jd-Kc runout — I missed completely. The backdoor diamond draw that existed on the flop is dead after the club turn, so I have no realistic equity and only queen-high. Elon bet the flop and I called; now he’s checked to me on the turn (To Call = 0). Betting OOP here would be a pure bluff with very limited fold equity against an opponent who is often willing to call down; it would bloat the pot with a hand that rarely improves and put me in difficult spots on the river. Pot control is the high-expected-value line: check and see the river. If Elon bets the river I can fold to significant aggression; if he checks back I may get to showdown cheaply or pick a better spot to bluff. I’ll check to avoid unnecessary risk and preserve my tournament equity.

Turn card Kc pairs my king, giving me top pair (Ks) with a weak 2h kicker on a board of 3s7dJdKc. Pot is 1500, Minni checks again from BB, showing continued weakness or trapping, but her flop call range includes Jx (overpair to my perceived range), 7x, low pairs, diamond flush draws (Jd), spade draws (3s), and some straight draws (e.g., T9, 89). The Kc improves me significantly for value against floats, weak pairs, and missed draws while blocking some Kx hands. Position is huge; deep stacks (~93bb effective vs Minni). History shows Minni calls small flop bets but often folds turns/rivers to aggression (e.g., hands 52,55,58,85,91). Betting ~2/3 pot (1000) extracts value from worse (AJ, QJ, 87dd/sd, floats), protects vs gutshots/draws completing river, denies equity, and applies fold equity vs her capped range. Checking risks free river cards to draws or bluffs; pot control unnecessary with top pair + position. Size balances value/bluffing frequency in our button vs BB dynamic.

Board is 3s-7d-Jd-Kc; I hold Qh6d — no pair and my backdoor diamond draw is dead with one card to come. Elon has bet 1000 into a 2500 pot; calling 1000 would require ~28.6% equity (1000 / 3500). My realistic outs to improve are three queens + three sixes = 6 outs, ~13% to hit the river (and many of those outs are non-clean or still lose to Kx/Jx). Given Elon's multi-street aggression and the sizing, his range is weighted to value (Jx, Kx, better) and he can have enough showdown strength to continue against a call. Raising as a bluff here would bloat a pot out of position with very little fold equity and is unnecessary with both stacks deep enough to find better spots. Folding preserves my stack for better +EV spots. I will fold.

I am on the button with 8c 4s. This is a very weak hand. While I have the chip lead and position, there is little incentive to play such low-equity cards against active opponents like Minni and Elon, who have shown a willingness to defend blinds and 3-bet. Folding is the standard and safest play here.

Heads-up in the SB with A♠7♦ — an Ace is a strong high-card hand and plays well in position postflop. I want to take the initiative and apply pressure to the BB rather than limp and face a wide defending range. A standard open to ~3bb (300 total) here fits the table pattern (many players open to 300) and gives good fold equity while keeping the pot manageable. If Elon 3‑bets large (well above a single-min raise), I’ll respect that and likely fold A7o; if he calls I’ll play postflop in position, c-betting favorable flops and exercising pot control on dangerous textures.

Preflop in BB facing a standard open-raise to 300 from Minni in SB after Button (Flash) folded. My hand 3c4c is a suited connector with good playability, decent equity against a wide SB stealing range (Minni raises frequently from history). Pot odds are favorable: calling 200 more into 400 (pot becomes 600), getting over 2.5:1 direct odds, plus strong implied odds given effective stacks (~9k for Minni, 11k for me) if I hit straights/flushes/draws. Position postflop allows realization. Folding is too tight vs SB; 3-bet not strong enough. Standard defend/call.

Check. I opened preflop with A♠7♦ and Elon called from the BB. The flop Jh–2c–Jc is a very bad texture for my hand: I have no pair, no club draw, just ace-high. Elon's defending range from the BB contains many hands that now beat me (any J for trips/quads, pocket 2s for a full house, almost any pocket pair for two pair, and lots of broadways that will continue). A c-bet here would be a pure bluff that only folds the very weakest holdings; given his tendency to defend and the size of the pot (600), I don't want to invest chips bluffing into a range that often has at least a pair. I’ll check to control the pot, avoid getting raised off the hand, and plan to fold to a meaningful bet. If Elon checks back I get to see the turn for free and can re-evaluate; if he bets small I may call sometimes to keep bluffs in his range, but I will fold to large pressure.

Flop: Jh 2c Jc. Paired board with two clubs, giving me a strong flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity against most ranges). No pair or straight draw, but excellent playability. Preflop, defended wide vs SB min-raise with suited connector. Minni checks SB-first, representing weakness or trap, but vs frequent SB raiser, likely weak Ax, Kx, or pocket pairs below J. Betting semi-bluffs: protects equity, denies equity to weaker draws/hands, builds pot for when we hit flush, and takes it down often given board texture favoring bluffs. OOP, but check risks giving free card to overcards/straight draws. Half-pot bet sizes well vs Minni's calling station tendencies (history shows calls large with marginal hands). Pot 600, bet 400 good SPR control (~15:1 effective). Favorable vs call/fold dynamics.

Elon’s bet of 400 into a 1000 pot is fairly small (~40% pot) so the immediate pot odds are roughly 28.6% (call 400 to win ~1400). I checked the dry, paired Jh–2c–Jc to control the pot and planned to fold to large, meaningful pressure, but this sizing is small enough to call. My A♠7♦ has blocker value to some Ax combos, I’m in position to see a turn, and Elon’s called-from-BB range contains a lot of missed broadways and small pockets that will c‑bet here. Calling 400 keeps bluffs in, realizes equity cheaply, and lets me fold to significant turn aggression—so I’ll call and re-evaluate on the turn.

I raised preflop and called Elon’s 400 c-bet on the J♥‑2♣‑J♣ flop. The 6♦ on the turn is a blank for my A♠7♦ — I still have no pair. Elon’s flop bet and call-range contains a lot of hands that already beat me (any J, sets like 22, and many medium pocket pairs and two‑pair/overpair types), while the hands I can realistically fold out with a bluff are a relatively small subset. I have position and can realize my equity by checking and seeing the river for free rather than bloating the pot with a marginal bluff. Checking keeps control of the pot, allows me to fold to significant river aggression, and preserves stack depth for better bluff or value opportunities on the river if a favorable card falls (or if Elon barrels small). Plan: check, call only a small river bet if it looks like a bluff, fold to large river pressure.

Turn board 6♦ is a somewhat blank card that doesn't complete obvious draws beyond my flush draw and doesn't pair low cards significantly improving many hands. I have a strong nut flush draw (9 outs, ~19% equity on clean river) with no showdown value, but excellent playability post-river. Preflop defended wide vs aggressive SB raise; flop c-bet 400 called by Minni, who now checks again OOP, representing weakness (possible Jx, underpairs, weak Ax, missed draws) vs her loose calling tendencies observed in history (frequent calls down with marginal holdings). Betting semi-bluffs for protection against runner-runner straights/overcards/improvers, fold equity vs her capped range after check-check-check patterns, and to build pot/target her calling station nature when we improve. Pot 1400, SPR ~6:1 effective with her 8625 stack; 2/3-pot bet of 900 maintains balance, denies equity, and sets up river shoves if called. Checking risks giving free cards to her equity or inducing unnecessary bluffs we beat; aggression maximizes EV long-term in this spot.

Pot is 2,300 and Elon has bet 900 on the turn. I hold A♠7♦ on J♥–2♣–J♣–6♦ (ace-high, no pair, no draw). Calling 900 to win 2,300 requires ~28.1% equity (900/(2300+900)=900/3200). Elon’s line — cold‑call in the BB, a small-ish flop bet (400) on a double‑jack board, then a sizeable turn bet (900) — is heavily weighted toward value (Jx, 22, other pocket pairs/captured ranges) with some polarized bluffs. My hand has almost no real outs and only blocks a few A‑x bluffs; vs his continuing range my actual equity is well below the required ~28%. Raising as a bluff is unattractive: I lack strong blockers, Elon has shown willingness to apply pressure and to call/raise large, and a raise here will often be called or shoved over. Preserving stack and waiting for better spots is the +EV play. I fold.

I have Js7s on the button in a 3-handed pot — a very positionally strong, playable hand. With deep stacks (~86bb) and both blinds to my left, the highest EV line is to take the initiative and open a wide stealing range. I will raise to 300 (3x the BB) — a standard sizing that maximizes fold equity, keeps weaker hands out, and gives me favorable postflop position to realize equity. If I face a large 3-bet from Elon or Flash I will usually fold J7s; versus a small 3-bet I may occasionally call to see a flop in position, but my default is to avoid getting overly committed with a marginal hand. Postflop plan: c-bet favorable flops, use position to control the pot, and apply pressure selectively while preserving stack for higher EV spots.

83o is an extremely weak hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or high card value. Facing a button raise from Minni, who raises frequently, in the small blind out of position against the big blind still to act. Poor pot odds (250 to call into 450 pot, ~2.8:1) don't justify playing this trash hand post-flop from OOP. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with 8h 2d. Minni has raised to 300 from the button. Although I only need to call 200 more into a pot of 450, 82o is an exceptionally weak hand and performs poorly out of position. Given the stack sizes and the tendency of Minni to be aggressive pre-flop and post-flop, there is no reason to defend with such a low-equity hand. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here to preserve my stack.

Preflop on the button with Ks4h, a speculative king-high hand with good position. No raises from earlier positions (implied by the log and pot size of 150 from blinds), so this is a standard steal spot. Raising to 3x the big blind (300 total) applies pressure on the blinds, who have shown willingness to fold in previous hands when faced with button aggression. My deep stack (125BB) allows flexibility postflop, and if called, I have position and kicker potential. Folding is too tight, limping plays a multiway pot OOP poorly for this hand.

I have a strong suited broadway hand (KcTc) in the Small Blind. Elon has been opening frequently from the Button. 3-betting here allows me to take the initiative and potentially win the pot immediately, or isolate the Button. Calling would put me out of position against both the Button and potentially the Big Blind, which is less ideal. A raise to 1,000 is a standard 3-bet size for this situation.

Facing a 3-bet to 1000 over Elon’s 300 open while I’m in the big blind with 2♣7♠. Pot is 1400 and the call is 900 (900 / (1400+900) ≈ 39% break-even). 2-7 offsuit is the worst starting hand — very poor equity, terrible playability and no meaningful blockers. I’d be out of position against two opponents and would frequently be dominated or unable to realize equity on most flops. A min 4‑bet to 1700 is an unnecessary bluff with almost no fold equity or blocker value. Preserve the stack and fold to this 3‑bet.

Facing a large 3-bet (to 1000, 3.3x my open) from Flash in the SB with K4o. While I have position on the SB postflop and deep stacks (122BB), K4o is a marginal opening hand that plays poorly against a 3-bet range. It suffers from reverse domination (loses to Kx better), poor playability on paired boards, and limited bluffing potential. Equity vs a polarized SB 3-bet range (pairs, AK/AQ, suited aces/broadways, some bluffs) is subpar (~30-35%), and calling 700 into 2100 pot odds are ok but postflop SPR too high for this hand's playability. 4-bet bluff possible with K blocker, but Flash's stack is deeper, and history shows aggression from him without clear fold equity guarantee. Folding preserves stack for premium spots in this deep cash game, consistent with GTO vs aggressive SB 3-bets.

I am on the Button with Ten-Seven offsuit. While this is a marginal opening hand in a three-handed game, the blinds (Minni and Elon) have shown significant aggression recently, frequently three-betting pre-flop. Given my substantial chip lead and the high frequency of three-bets from the blinds when I open from the button, I will choose to fold this weak hand to avoid putting myself in a difficult spot out of position or facing a re-raise with low equity.

Heads-up in the small blind with 8♣3♣ (8h3c). Pot is 150 and I need to put in 50 more (100 total) to see the flop. Calling 50 gives me ~4:1 pot odds (need ~20% equity) and 8-3o has enough raw equity to defend cheaply heads-up. Raising to 200 would bloat the pot and play out of position with a very weak, often-dominated holding; Elon has shown willingness to defend and 3-bet in prior hands, so I don’t want to commit extra chips unnecessarily. Plan: call to see a flop and fold to heavy aggression unless I connect or get a strong draw; play straightforward postflop to control pot size.

In the big blind facing a small blind limp into an unraised pot, K2o is a weak offsuit hand with limited playability. Checking is optimal to see a flop cheaply with great pot odds (pot already 200 with 100 invested). Raising risks domination by better kings or pairs, and post-flop play out of position would be difficult with this hand. Folding discards dead money unnecessarily. Will evaluate board and Minni's range post-flop, leveraging deep stacks (mine 12k, hers 8.5k). Historical play shows Minni aggressive but limping occasionally with speculative hands.

Flopped a pair of eights (8♥) on K♥-2♥-8♦ after defending the small blind. Heads-up this is a reasonably strong, but vulnerable, hand vs a wide check-behind range. I want thin value and to deny equity to overcards/backdoor hearts without bloating the pot, so I’ll bet about half the pot (100). This sizes to get calls from worse pairs and some draws, but keeps the pot manageable if Elon responds aggressively. If I face a large check-raise or shove I’ll fold (my kicker is weak); if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn.

Flop hits top pair with Ks on Kh2h8d board, giving top pair weak kicker. Out of position vs Minni's small bet (100 into 200), which likely represents a wide limping range including weaker pairs, draws, or air after SB limp preflop. Ks blocks her premium kings (KQ/KT suited). Raising for value against worse pairs/draws, protection against hearts/straights, and to deny equity. Her history shows aggression but limps speculative hands. Deep stacks favor raising thin value OOP rather than calling and playing tricky spots with bad kicker. Raising to 400 (3x her bet) sizes pot appropriately for range advantage.

I have 8♥3♣ on 2♥ K♥ 8♦ — second pair with a very weak 3-kicker. I c‑bet 100 to get thin value and deny overcards, and Elon check‑re‑raised to 400 (I must call 300). Pot is 700; calling 300 into a total 1000 requires ~30% equity. Elon’s large check‑raise polarizes to strong value (Kx, K8/A8 two‑pair combos, sets, or 8x with better kicker) plus some two‑heart semi‑bluffs. Versus that range my hand is frequently behind or easily counterfeited; I have very few clean outs (two remaining eights to make trips and only backdoor heart possibilities). Raising would bloat the pot with a marginal holding and committing more chips out of position is unnecessary. Per my plan to fold to heavy aggression with a weak kicker, I drop this hand and preserve my stack for better spots.

I’m on the button with J♦3♥ — a weak, marginal hand, but position is a big asset here against only the SB and BB. Folding throws away positional advantage; calling (limping) gives up fold equity and invites the blinds to play for cheap. A small, standard button open to the min (200 total) applies pressure, wins the pot a lot of the time, and keeps risk limited if either blind 3-bets aggressively — in which case I’ll fold this hand. If called, I’ll play the flop in position and use fold equity / board texture to continue or give up. Raise to 200 to attempt a steal while preserving stack vs 3-bets.

AJ offsuit is a strong hand from the small blind facing a min-raise from the button. Button ranges are wide in late position, so AJ has excellent equity against their calling range and strong fold equity on the 3-bet. Out of position, but we want to thin the field and take initiative. Standard 3-bet sizing around 3x the raise (600) into a pot of 350. Deep stacks allow for post-flop flexibility if called. Opponent tendencies from history: Minni raises frequently but folds to aggression often.

I am in the Big Blind with 7s6c. Minni raised from the Button and Elon 3-bet from the Small Blind. Given the strength represented by a 3-bet and the fact that I am out of position with a weak connector, folding is the most disciplined and profitable long-term play.

I opened to 200 as a standard button steal, but Elon’s 3-bet to 600 puts me in a tough spot. J♦3♥ is a weak, often-dominated hand with poor postflop playability against a 3-bet range. Calling 400 requires ~31% equity to break even, and against Elon’s typically value-heavy/polarized 3-bets my real equity is well below that. A light 4-bet bluff here is unnecessary risk with plenty of spots left in the orbit. Preserve the 8k+ stack and fold to the 3-bet.

9c5c is a weak suited connector with low cards and limited playability. On the button, position is good, but facing the big blind with no prior action (SB folded), this hand doesn't warrant calling or raising. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in a long session. Opponents' stacks suggest no need to speculate here.

Ace-five offsuit is a strong enough hand to play aggressively in a blind-vs-blind situation. With the button folding, I will raise to 300 to take the initiative and apply pressure on the Big Blind, aiming to either win the blinds now or define the hand range for post-flop play.

I’m in the big blind with A♦Q♥ facing a small‑blind open to 300 (I need 200 more). AQ is one of the strongest non‑pair hands heads‑up and I don’t want to play a big pot out of position by just flatting. Flash has been opening and playing aggressively from the SB, so I want to take the initiative, get fold equity against weaker broadways/medium pairs, and extract value from worse hands (KQ, AJ, suited broadways). A 3‑bet to ~1000 is a standard, constructive size here: big enough to force folds and isolate, but small enough to preserve stack flexibility if I face a 4‑bet. Plan: 3‑bet to 1000 total; if Flash 4‑bets very large (commits a huge portion of stacks) I will re‑evaluate and likely fold; if he calls we proceed to C‑bet favorable flops and control the pot on dangerous runouts.

I opened with Ace-five offsuit from the Small Blind, which is a standard play in a three-handed game to steal the blinds or take initiative. Minni (Big Blind) has responded with a 3-bet to 1000. Looking at the match history, Minni has been very aggressive in 3-betting my Small Blind opens (Hands 13, 32, 68, 71, 74). While I suspect Minni is widening her range to exploit my frequent opening, Ace-five offsuit is a poor hand to defend with out of position. It lacks the post-flop playability of suited connectors or stronger broadway cards. Although the pot odds are roughly 2.8:1, the disadvantage of being out of position with a hand that is often dominated or difficult to play on most flops makes calling -EV in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better hand to combat Minni's aggression, preserving my chip lead.

I am on the button with 92o, which is a very weak starting hand. While I have the largest stack at the table, there is no strategic reason to open this hand into the blinds, especially since Minni and Elon have shown aggression in previous pots. Folding is the most standard and disciplined play here.

I’m on the button/small blind with Qc8h heads-up vs the big blind. The pot is 150 and I only need to put in 50 more (100 total) to see a flop. Q8o is a marginal but playable hand in heads-up and I’ll have position post-flop, so I prefer to complete/call rather than inflate the pot and risk a 3‑bet from an active opponent. Calling gives good pot odds to see a flop, preserves stack depth for post‑flop play, and lets me use position to barrel with equity (top pair, backdoor draws) or release to strong resistance.

7h2s is one of the worst possible hands in poker: offsuit, unconnected, low cards with poor playability. Flash has folded, leaving heads-up vs Minni's SB limp, which is a wide range but still favors checking in BB to realize equity cheaply if the flop hits (unlikely but free). Raising risks committing dead money with a hand that struggles post-flop against any resistance, and pot odds are great to check (pot 200 to see flop). Discipline dictates checking weak hands rather than spewing chips. Long-term EV positive to check and fold most flops unless massive hit.

Flop: 6c 6d Qs — I hold Qc 8h: top pair with an 8 kicker. This is a fairly dry, paired flop; the main hands that currently beat me are trip sixes/quads (rare) or a stronger Q (AQ, KQ). Heads-up with position, a value/protection c-bet is the right play: it extracts value from worse queens and many unpaired hands, denies free cards, and keeps the pot manageable given deep stacks. A half-pot bet (~100 into 200) is large enough to get value but small enough to fold out pure air and leave room to maneuver on later streets. If Elon check‑raises big I’ll give it credit and fold or re-evaluate depending on sizing; if he calls I’ll continue to control the pot on the turn and reassess. Bet 100.

72o is a complete bluff catcher failure on this flop: paired 6s with Qs kicker, no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw. Minni's SB limp preflop is wide (any two), but her small flop bet (100 into 300) after checking through pre suggests trapping value like QQ, 66, Q6, or semi-bluff with flush/straight draw blockers. Calling offers ~3:1 pot odds but near-zero equity against her range (~5-10% at best, no showdown value). Raising as a bluff is disastrous: no fold equity on paired board (opponents call light with TP/TK/draws), exposes stack to 3-bet shove from shorter Minni (8375), and burns dead money from preflop limp pot. Discipline: fold trash postflop, realize EV by avoiding spew. Preserve stack for better spots in long cash game.

On the button with A♥2♣ in a three-handed game this is a clear open-raise spot. A2o has decent playability in position and lots of fold equity against the blinds. Limping hands here lets the blinds see cheap flops and invites multiway action — I want to isolate and play postflop in position. Table habit shows standard opens of ~300, so I’ll open to 3x the big blind (300) to look strong and buy the pot with position. Plan: continue versus one caller with standard c-bet sizing on dry flops; fold (or frequently fold) to a large 3-bet — I’ll re-evaluate if a small 3-bet gives pot odds to call, but a big 3-bet will be a fold given the weak kicker.

6d4s is a trash hand: low, offsuit, gapped connectors with poor playability OOP. Minni's button raise range is wide, but this hand has negligible equity against it (dominated often, poor postflop potential). Pot odds ~2:1 not enough for speculative call OOP in SB. Effective stacks allow waiting for premium hands to maximize stack over 150 hands. Folding preserves chips for better opportunities.

In this 3-handed game, Minni is opening from the button and I am in the Big Blind with T8o. The pot odds are favorable (200 to call into a pot of 450), and T8o has enough equity against a wide button opening range to justify a defense. Although I will be out of position, the price is right to see a flop.

I am in the Big Blind with a gutshot straight draw (needing a 9) on a board of Ks-Jh-7s. The pre-flop aggressor is Minni, who opened from the Button. This board likely connects well with a Button opening range (Kx, Jx, and various spade draws). Being out of position, the standard play is to check to the aggressor and see how they react. Betting into them here is unlikely to generate many folds from better hands and puts me in a tough spot if raised.

I opened to 300 from the button and got one caller in the BB. The flop Ks-Jh-7s is reasonably dry for continuation — there are two spades but no paired board with connected middle cards — and I have A♠? (correction: A♥2♣) Ace-high plus the heart blocker. In position vs a single caller I want to seize initiative, fold out weaker pairs, missed broadways and some draws, and charge spade draws. A half-pot c-bet is the right balance: it applies pressure while keeping the pot manageable with a marginal holding. Bet ~½ pot (325). If I get called I'll re-evaluate on the turn; if I face a large raise I'll assume a strong made hand or a disguised draw and give it up with A2o.

On a board of Ks-Jh-7s, I currently hold Ten-high with a gutshot straight draw (needing a 9). Minni, the pre-flop aggressor from the button, has placed a half-pot continuation bet. This board texture is very favorable for a button opening range, likely containing many Kings, Jacks, and spade draws. With only a weak gutshot, no flush draw, and being out of position, I do not have sufficient equity to justify a call. Folding is the most disciplined and profitable play in the long run.

Ah9s is a strong suited ace hand from the button with excellent position. It has good playability post-flop, blocks strong ace hands like AA/AK/AQ, and performs well against typical calling ranges from blinds. With deep stacks and history showing opponents often fold to pre-flop raises (e.g., many folds to 300 raises), raising to steal the 150 pot or build a pot OOP is optimal. Standard size of 3x BB (300) balances value and fold equity without overcommitting.

I am in the Small Blind with a pocket pair of sixes. The Button (Elon) has opened for 300. Historically, Elon has shown a willingness to fold to my three-bets (Hands #61, #79, #103). Three-betting here allows me to take the initiative, potentially win the pot immediately, and prevents the Big Blind (Minni) from entering the pot cheaply. If I am called, I still have a solid hand that can flop a set. I'll raise to 1000.

I’m in the big blind with 7h2d — the worst starting hand. Elon opened to 300 and Flash 3-bet to 1000 from the SB. Pot is 1400 and it costs me 900 to call, so break-even equity is ~900/(1400+900) ≈ 39%. Versus a button opener and an SB 3-bettor my equity with 7‑2o is effectively zero and I’ll be out of position postflop. A 4‑bet bluff is thin (I have no meaningful blockers) and calling is a large, negative‑EV commitment. With deep stacks but no justification to get involved here, the profitable, low-variance play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

Facing a 3-bet from Flash in the SB after my button open to 300. A9s suited is a strong hand with the nut flush potential, Ace blocker reducing villain's AA/AK/AQ combos, and excellent playability in position (button acts last post-flop heads-up). SB 3-bet ranges vs button opens are often polarized and wide, including bluffs like suited connectors, weaker Ax, or pocket pairs. Equity is solid (~40%+ vs typical range), pot odds are great (700 to call into ~2100 effective pot for 3:1), and deep stacks allow flexible post-flop play: fold to c-bets when missing, float/raise bluffs, or get value with top pair. Raising risks bloating pot OOP with marginal hand; folding is too tight given position/history of opponents folding post-flop. Call to realize equity and exploit position.

I have flopped bottom set on a board of Qd-Kc-6h. This is an extremely strong hand, particularly in a 3-bet pot. The board texture is likely to have connected with Elon's pre-flop calling range, which contains many broadway cards like KQ, KJ, or KT. Since I am out of position and took the lead pre-flop, I will lead with a continuation bet to build the pot and get value from any Kx or Qx hands Elon may be holding. A bet of 1000 (roughly half the pot) is a standard sizing that keeps his marginal hands and draws in while extracting value.

A9s completely misses the K-Q-6 rainbow flop, holding only ace-high with no draws, pair potential, or blockers to many of villain's value hands beyond AK/AQ. Flash's SB 3-bet range pre-flop is strong and polarized (e.g., KK+, QQ+, AK, AQ, KQ, some bluffs like suited connectors or weak Ax), and this board hits the top of that range hard with top pair/top kicker combos, two-pair, sets, etc. His half-pot c-bet is standard value/balance. While in position on the button with ~3.1:1 pot odds requiring ~24% equity to call profitably, realized equity is low (~25-35% vs. typical range, less vs. value-heavy), and future streets offer little improvement outs (3-4 to A/T/J, runner-runner FH/SD). Raising as a bluff risks commitment with deep stacks and villain's history of aggression/stationing (e.g., Hand 91 big pot), while calling bloats pot OOP (wait, IP actually) to 4100+ with marginal hand vulnerable to turn barrels. Folding preserves stack for better spots; villain's range strength + board texture make this a clear fold despite position.

I am on the button with 4h 5d, which is a very weak starting hand. Given the recent aggressive play and frequent 3-betting from both Minni and Elon, attempting a steal with this hand is unnecessarily risky. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

I’m in the small blind with 2s8s — a very weak but suited hand. There’s no raise and I only need to add 50 more (100 total committed) to see the flop. The pot is 150, so calling 50 gives about 4:1 pot odds (~20% equity required), and heads-up vs the big blind this hand has enough equity to justify a cheap defend. Raising to the minimum (200) is too loose out of position with garbage. Plan: call to see a flop, fold to a sizable 3-bet preflop, and proceed cautiously postflop — continue only on favorable spade/connected boards or when I pick up clear equity.

Preflop in the big blind with 5c8c suited connectors against a small blind limp from Minni. Flash has folded, so it's heads-up. Suited connectors have good implied odds and play well post-flop in position effectively (as BB acts last post-flop). Pot is already 200 with no further investment needed, providing excellent odds to see a flop. Raising risks bloating the pot with a speculative hand that doesn't have great showdown value or fold equity against Minni's range, which often includes limped hands that connect well. Checking is standard to realize equity cheaply and control the pot.

Check. On K♠ Q♥ 3♥ with 2♠8♠ I have no pair and only a backdoor spade draw that requires both turn and river (≈4% to make a flush). I’m out of position and the pot is small — a c-bet here is a thin bluff against an unknown, often-aggressive BB. I’ll check and fold to any meaningful bet; if Elon checks back I can see a free turn and only continue if I pick up a spade, a pair, or another clear improvement.

Flop board of 3h Qh Ks is somewhat coordinated with two hearts and broadway potential, but Minni's SB limp-check range is wide and weak (low pairs, Ace-x, suited connectors, offsuit broadway that missed). She checked to me after I checked preflop, indicating no strong hand. My 5c8c has no pair, no draws, and minimal showdown value, making it a pure bluff. However, in position heads-up with a small pot (200), a small bet has high fold equity against her range. Betting ~60% pot (125) pressures her weak holdings and wins the pot immediately most of the time. Deep stacks (mine 11450, hers 8825) allow for post-flop flexibility if called. Checking behind risks giving free cards to draws or improvements, and my range appears capped without this bluff. Strong EV play in multi-hand game to accumulate small pots consistently.

Elon bets 125 into a 325 pot; calling 125 requires ~28% equity. With 2♠8♠ on K♠ Q♥ 3♥ I have no pair, only a two-card backdoor spade possibility (~3% to make a flush) and six low-card outs to pair by river (≈24% to pair by river) — but many of those pair-outs will still be second-best versus a standard c-bet range of Kx/Qx/heart-draws/pocket pairs. Realistic showdown equity vs his range is well below the required ~28%, and I’m out of position with deep stacks so there’s little implied value in calling. I stick to my preflop plan: fold to meaningful aggression and wait for a better spot.

I’m on the button with J♠7♣ in a 3‑handed pot against the blinds. In short‑handed play the button should open a wide range; J7o is marginal but fine to steal. A raise to 300 (≈3×BB) is standard here — it gives good fold equity, keeps my range balanced (I’ve been opening to this size previously), and avoids playing a weak hand multiway. If I face a big 3‑bet from Flash or Elon I’ll fold J7o; if called I’ll c‑bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously. Raising is preferable to limp/call because it takes the initiative and often isolates one opponent.

Preflop in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call into 450 pot). My hand is T8o, which is offsuit with only one gap, but poor post-flop playability out of position. Button ranges are wide, but calling OOP with this hand leads to difficult spots without initiative or strong equity. Pot odds are about 1.8:1, but implied odds are low due to reverse implied odds with a mediocre hand. Folding preserves stack for better spots. No history suggests Minni is super weak here to justify a call or 3-bet.

I am in the Big Blind with Jack of Clubs and Five of Clubs. Minni, on the Button, has raised to 300. While the pot odds are roughly 2.25 to 1, calling with a weak hand like J5s out of position against an aggressive player like Minni is often a losing play in the long run. J5s has limited playability and I would likely be forced to fold on many flops if I don't hit a strong draw or top pair. Given my significant stack lead, I prefer to fold and wait for a more advantageous situation.

3h7d is a complete trash hand: low offsuit cards, no straight potential, no flush draw, dominated by any decent holding. Preflop on button with pot 150 and to-call 100 gives poor implied odds even positionally. Opponents (Flash aggressive raiser, Minni frequent raiser/caller) likely defend wide but still beat this hand overwhelmingly. No fold equity worth pursuing a steal here. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots in this deep-stack cash game.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Trey offsuit. In a 3-handed game, an Ace in the Small Blind is a strong starting hand, especially after the Button (Elon) has folded. I want to put pressure on the Big Blind (Minni) and either win the pot immediately or take control of the hand. Calling (completing) is an option, but raising is generally preferred to maximize fold equity and build a pot when I have an hand with high card strength. I will raise to 300.

Call to 300 (put in additional 200). K♥5♥ is a playable, suited king in the big blind heads-up — good equity and blocker value against a wide SB opening. Pot odds are strong (200 to call into a 400 pot ≈ 3:1) and I don’t want to 3-bet OOP with a marginal hand; a small reraise invites larger action and puts me in tough spots out of position. Plan: see the flop and evaluate — look to continue with top pair or strong draws, give up to heavy aggression on dry boards. If Flash shows weakness postflop I can seize the initiative; if he 3-bets pre I’ll fold unless sizing is unusually small.

The flop is Jack of Clubs, Nine of Clubs, Eight of Diamonds, which is a very coordinated board that connects well with Minni's likely calling range from the Big Blind (containing many suited connectors and middle-rank cards). While I have the Ace of Clubs for a backdoor nut flush draw, my Ace-high has limited showdown value and is vulnerable on this texture. Betting is unlikely to generate many folds from hands that currently beat me or from draws. Checking allows me to see how the action develops and potentially realize equity if the turn is favorable or if the hand goes check-check.

Flop Jc 9c 8d vs my K♥5♥: I have only an overcard and a backdoor heart; no pair and no club or straight draw. Flash checked after opening from the SB — his range is wide, but this texture hits lots of his openings (clubs, J/9/8, T/Q/7 for straights). Betting here would be a thin c‑bet out of position that can get raised or put me in tough spots with little equity. Pot control and equity realization is the priority: check back to see a turn card for free and only invest if I improve (pair/heart) or he shows clear weakness. If Flash bets a small probe I can consider a call vs a high frequency stealer, but I will fold to strong raises on this dry/connected board. Action: check and re-evaluate on the turn.

The turn card is the Queen of Diamonds, making the board Jack-Nine-Eight-Queen. This is an extremely coordinated texture that heavily favors the Big Blind's range, as it includes many hands like Ten-X (making a straight), Queen-X, and two-pair combinations. My Ace-high has almost no showdown value here and the board doesn't provide me with any significant draws. I checked the flop and will check the turn as well, intending to fold to most bets as this board is far too connected to attempt a bluff or a thin call.

I have only K♥5♥ — no pair on a connected Jc‑9c‑8d‑Qd board. My backdoor heart draw is dead after the diamond turn, and Flash checked both flop and turn, showing either weakness or a slowplay. This is a very marginal hand to bluff with on a board that hits a large portion of his raising range (J/9/8/Q/T and clubs). Pot control and avoiding unnecessary bluffs OOP is the best line: check and let him show strength. I’ll fold to a sizable bet, but can call a small probe given pot odds and re‑evaluate on the river if a safe card (K or low card) appears or if he shows clear weakness.

The board is extremely coordinated with Jack, Nine, Eight, Queen, and King. Any Ten makes a straight, and many other combinations result in two-pair or better. My Ace-high has no showdown value, but after checking both the flop and the turn, representing a strong hand with a river bet is difficult and unlikely to force a fold from even marginal hands in Minni's range. I will check and concede the pot.

River paired me with the King (Ks) giving K♥5♥ one pair, but it’s a very dry/connected Jc-9c-8d-Qd-Ks texture that hits a lot of Flash’s opening range (Qx/Jx/9x, pocket pairs, clubs). Flash checked flop, turn and river — showing either weakness or a slowplay — so I don’t want to manufacture a bluff on a card that helps many of his likely hands. A small value bet might fold out some air but will also fold the hands I beat and get called (or raised) by Kx with better kickers or the two-pair/sets that legitimately crush me. Pot is only 600 and I can get to a cheap showdown with a vulnerable but real pair; pot control is the highest EV line here. Action: check to showdown and avoid getting raised off my rivered one-pair.

Holding Kd 6d on the button in a 3-handed game is a standard opening hand. I have a significant chip lead and want to put pressure on the blinds. Raising to 3x the big blind is consistent with my previous betting patterns.

I’m in the small blind with A♠6♥ facing a button open to 300. I’ve already posted 50, so calling 250 more (300 total) keeps the pot controlled and lets me realize equity against a wide button opening range. A6o is marginal to 3-bet for value and a tiny 3-bet (to 500) won’t fold many hands — I’d rather see a flop and play post‑flop with position chances if the BB folds. If the BB 3-bets I’ll reassess (likely fold to a large 3-bet, consider calling/sizing-dependent for a small squeeze); if BB cold-calls I’ll proceed post-flop for value on ace-highs or with relevant draws. Call to 300 total.

Preflop in BB with Th5h suited. Flash (button) raises to 300, Minni (SB) calls 250, pot 700, to call 200 (3.5:1 odds). T5s is a playable speculative hand multiway due to suitedness and straight/flush potential. Deep stacks, loose opponents from history (Flash aggressive raiser, Minni frequent caller). Defending BB wide here for implied odds if we hit. Folding too tight; raising too strong. Call to see flop cheap OOP.

I’m out of position with A♠6♥ on 3♠ Q♠ 7♣. I have only Ace-high (no pair) and only a remote backdoor spade possibility (would require both turn and river to be spades). This is a three-way pot — c-betting into two opponents with a marginal hand is inefficient and will often get called by Qx, spade draws or better. I’ll check to control the pot and see how the opener reacts. If faced with a small c-bet I can call to realize my ~12–13% chance to pair the Ace by river (and the tiny runner-runner flush chance); I’ll fold to large aggression out of position. If checked around I’ll reevaluate on the turn (look to bet only if I pair/receive a favourable card or use position on later streets).

Flop board 3s Qs 7c gives me complete air with Th5h: no pair, no flush draw (hearts don't hit), no straight draw (5-T doesn't connect meaningfully with 3-7-Q). Multiway pot OOP after calling pre with speculative suited hand that missed entirely. Minni checks first (SB, likely weak), but betting here as bluff is poor: scary board with spade flush draw and Q-high, Flash (aggro button raiser) likely to continue with wide range. No fold equity strong enough multiway, better to check and realize equity cheaply or fold to bet. Preserve stack for better spots; goal is long-term max stack over 150 hands.

I opened pre-flop with King-Six of diamonds on the button and received two callers. The flop is Queen-Seven-Trey rainbow, which misses my range largely. However, as the pre-flop aggressor in a three-way pot where both opponents checked to me, a continuation bet (C-bet) is often effective here. The board is relatively dry, although there are some straight draws and a Queen in the opponents' ranges. By betting around 1/3 to 1/2 of the pot, I can fold out many of their air hands and potentially take the pot down immediately. If I am called, I still have two overcards to the 7 and 3 and backdoor diamond possibilities.

I’m out of position with A♠6♥ on 3♠ Q♠ 7♣ after the button (preflop raiser) bets 400. I only have Ace‑high and a very remote backdoor spade plan (need both turn+river spades). Pot was 900 before the bet; after the 400 it’s 1300, so calling 400 would require ~23.5% equity to break even. My realistic equity to improve by river is ~12.5% to pair the Ace plus ~4% to hit a runner‑runner flush (~16–17% total), well below the required pot odds. This is effectively a multiway spot (Elon checked) which further reduces my playability and fold equity. A raise would be a thin bluff with little fold equity and high risk of getting committed OOP. I’ve already invested 300 (sunk cost), so the correct decision is to preserve my deep stack and fold this marginal hand.

Post-flop in BB with Th5h on 3s Qs 7c board after checking through to Flash's 400 bet into 1300 pot (pot odds ~3.25:1). Hand is complete air: no pair, no flush draw (hearts missed), no straight draw (T5 doesn't connect to 3-7-Q), minimal backdoor potential. OOP multiway originally, now heads-up vs aggressive button Flash who continuation bets wide after two checks (his range: wide preflop raise including Ax, Kx, suited connectors, bluffs). Calling commits 400 without equity or fold equity, poor implied odds on dry-ish but drawy board (spade flush draw heavy). Raising as bluff risky OOP vs aggro opponent likely to 3-bet or call wide. Folding preserves stack for premium spots; speculative preflop call realized no value, standard discipline to muck air here. Long-term EV positive to avoid spew over 150 hands.

Button with K♠3♥, stack 8,975. Pot is 150 and it only costs 100 to see the flop — a very small investment relative to my stack. K3o is a marginal hand to open-raise facing two active, somewhat aggressive blinds (likely to defend or 3-bet), so a raise would often create uncomfortable action or isolate me out of position vs a 3-bet. Folding is too nitty for such a small pot. Calling keeps me in position to see a flop cheaply, lets me realize equity, and I can fold to strong action post‑flop if I miss. I’ll call 100 and play the flop in position.

Preflop from SB facing a button limp. Jc6s is a suited connector with straight and flush potential, playable multiway or heads-up. Rather than calling OOP and getting multiway pot awkwardly, raise to isolate the likely weak limper. Minni on button limps often show weakness (from history). Size to 300 (3x BB) standard to take initiative. If called, still decent equity; BB may fold or we play postflop. Folding misses dead money value; calling passive OOP. Raising builds pot with position advantage postflop if isolated.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Deuce offsuit (Kd 2s). Minni limped from the button, and Elon raised to 300 from the Small Blind. Although I have a King, my kicker is extremely weak and the hand has very poor playability post-flop. Furthermore, being caught between the Small Blind and the Button in a multi-way pot is the worst positional disadvantage. Given the current action, it is best to fold and wait for a stronger starting hand.

I already limped/called on the button with K♠3♥ and Elon (SB) made it 300. Calling the additional 200 is a cheap way to see the flop in position — the pot is 500 and the call is small relative to my ~8.9k stack. K3o is too weak to 3-bet for value or to bluff‑raise; folding is too nitty given pot odds and position. I’ll call to realize equity and play the flop in position, folding to heavy post‑flop aggression unless I pair my King or pick up a strong draw.

Preflop raise with Jc6s as suited connector to isolate Minni's limp, which often indicates weakness based on history. Flop 6h Js 2h gives me top two pair (Js and 6s), a monster heads-up. Board is draw-heavy with heart flush draw and possible straight draws (e.g., 3-4-5, T-Q). Minni's limp-call range is wide: weak pairs, suited aces, connectors/draws. As OOP, betting protects against draws, builds pot for value from worse pairs/one pair (e.g., Jx, 66, 22), floats, or weak draws. Checking invites free cards or bluffs we beat. Half-pot bet sizes down better hands or induces calls from worse. Bet 450 (~2/3 pot) for value/protection, pot odds poor for draws to continue.

I have K♠3♥ on 6♥ J♠ 2♥ — no pair and only a lone heart (a very slim runner-runner flush possibility). Elon opened preflop and is continuing here with a standard-sized c-bet (450 into the displayed 1,150 pot). Calling 450 to win ~1,600 (after my call) gives me roughly ~28% pot-odds. In position against a likely wide c-bet range (Jx, Ax hearts, Kx, broadways and some bluffs) I can realize equity cheaply and use position to fold to heavy turn aggression if I miss. I don't have enough equity or fold equity to make a credible raise/bluff, and folding would be too nitty for the price. Plan: call to see the turn, continue only if I pair the King or pick up a legitimate draw; fold to strong turn shoves/raises when I have nothing.

Turn board completes potential straights (3x4x for 2-3-4-5-6) and strengthens the heart flush draw (now three hearts on board). I hold top two pair (JJ and 66), blocking the top end of villain's value range (one J and the 6 on board). Minni's limp-call preflop and flop call range includes weaker pairs (TT-77, 22), worse Jx (AJ-QJ suited), flush draws (AhXh, KhXh), straight draws (34s, T9s, etc.). Checking OOP risks giving free cards to draws we beat or check-raise all-in from made hands; betting extracts value from worse made hands/one-pair types, charges draws, and builds pot with our strong hand. Her history shows calling stations tendencies in multi-street pots. Pot 1600, bet ~2/3 pot (1100) denies odds to draws (~30% equity for flush draw) and gets calls from ~20-30% equity hands we dominate. Stacks allow (10550 effective vs her 8225), preserving fold equity if she has monster.

I hold K♠3♥ on 6♥ J♠ 2♥ 5♥. I currently have no pair and one heart — there are three hearts on board so I need one heart on the river to make a flush. Elon bettors/raisers on flop and now 1,100 on the turn into a 2,700 pot. Calling 1,100 gives me ~28.9% break‑even odds (1,100/(2,700+1,100)). My raw outs to improve are about 14 (nine remaining hearts + two non‑heart kings + three threes) → 14/46 ≈ 30.4%, slightly better than the pot‑odds threshold. That said many heart outs are vulnerable if Elon already holds a heart (he may have the nut or a higher flush), so effective equity is a bit lower. Given I’m in position, the stake relative to my stack, and the marginal but positive equity, I’ll call to realize equity and see the river. I won’t raise — I don’t have a made hand or credible blocker to bluff‑raise profitably and a raise would commit too much with little fold equity. Plan: call 1,100; on the river fold to heavy action if I miss, but if I hit a king/three or a clean heart I will re‑evaluate for value or thin‑value raise.

River 9h completes obvious heart flush draws (four hearts: 6h,2h,5h,9h) and potential straights (e.g., 7-8 for 5-6-7-8-9). Top two pair (JJ66) blocks some Jx/6x but loses to any flush (AhXh, KhXh, suited connectors like T8h, 87h from her wide limp-call range), 7-8 straight, or better like J9/J6 trips/set. Minni called preflop raise OOP (limp-call from button, weak-wide range: suited connectors, Ax suited, mid pairs), flop bet (top pair/draw-heavy board), and large turn bet (three hearts, straight draws live). Her continuing after turn likely means strong made hand/draw improvement; calling stations like Minni hit here. Pot 3800, effective stacks ~7k. Betting thin value risks raise from nuts (flush likely) or call from better; bluffs unlikely from her vs my aggression. Checking first OOP: value bets/flushes bet, we fold profitably; bluffs we snap if small but expect value-heavy range. Preserves stack for better spots in 150-hand game vs exploitable field. No showdown value as villain reps strength.

I made a flush on the river (3♥ + 6♥,2♥,5♥,9♥ = 9‑high heart flush), but it’s the weakest possible flush on this board and is very vulnerable to any A♥/K♥/Q♥/10♥ that Elon could have. Elon opened pre, barreled the flop and turn (450, then 1100) and then checked the river — that line is consistent with medium strength hands (Jx, single pairs) or missed/turned draws; it’s less likely he’s always checking a big nut flush. Betting a small amount extracts value from worse hands (pairs, Jx, stray rivers) while keeping the price reasonable if he raises with a better heart. Pot is 3,800; a ~25–30% pot bet (~1,000) is big enough to get value but small enough to fold to meaningful raises and protect my stack. Plan: bet for thin value; if Elon raises strongly I’ll fold my weak nut flush to a likely better heart.

Facing a 1000 bet into 4800 pot on a river completing obvious flushes (four hearts: 6h,2h,5h,9h) and straights (3-4 for 2-3-4-5-6, 7-8 for 5-6-7-8-9). Top two pair (JJ66) is strong but loses to any flush, most straights, sets, or better. Minni's limp-call preflop OOP to raise (wide range: suited connectors, Ah/Kh suited, mid pairs), called flop/top pair board bet, large turn bet on scary draw-completing board (three hearts then), now bets after my check. Her range polarized to nuts (flushes like AhXh, 78h straight-flush draw hit flush, 34 suited) or occasional bluffs, but history shows calling station tendencies, less bluff-heavy post-oak, especially vs aggression after checks. Pot odds ~15% needed (1000 to 5800), but <10% equity vs her continuing range (crushed by 70%+ flushes/straights). Checking pre-planned OOP to realize fold equity and preserve stack; calling risks spew to value, no future outs likely. Multiway history shows she pays off but here heads-up vs my image. Fold preserves 9450 stack for better spots in 150-hand game vs exploitable field.

Preflop on the button with 2s6c, a complete trash hand with no connectivity, low cards, and offsuit. Facing a to-call of 100 into a 150 pot gives pot odds of about 1.5:1, but this hand has very poor equity against any reasonable range (worst than 30% vs calling ranges). Even in position, speculative hands need suited connectors or better for implied odds; this has none. Opponents have been aggressive, but folding marginal trash preserves stack for better spots. Long-term EV play is to fold.

I am in the Small Blind with Deuce of Hearts and Five of Hearts. The button has folded, leaving only me and Minni (the Big Blind) in the hand. While 5-2 suited has some speculative value, it is a very weak hand to play out of position against an opponent who has shown a willingness to defend their blinds and play post-flop. Given that I have a significant chip lead and we are at hand 115 of 150, there is no need to commit chips with such a marginal hand. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I am on the button with a solid starting hand, Queen-Jack offsuit. This is a standard opening hand in this position. I will raise to 300, which has been my consistent opening size, to take control of the pot and leverage my positional advantage over Minni and Elon.

I have T5 offsuit in the small blind facing a standard button open to 300. This is a weak, offsuit hand with a poor kicker and very limited post‑flop playability out of position. Calling 250 into a 450 pot requires roughly 36% immediate equity; against a BTN open range (and with the BB still to act) my realistic equity and ability to realize it OOP is low. 3‑betting as a bluff is unattractive here — I have poor blockers, I’ll face difficult decisions vs a call or 4‑bet, and multiway action makes realization worse. With deep stacks but no clear implied‑odds or fold equity for this specific hand, the +EV play is to fold and wait for a better spot in position or with a more playable holding.

Facing a button raise to 300 from Flash, who has been aggressive in previous hands. My J9o is a decent speculative hand with straight and flush potential, good blockers, and playability. Pot odds are favorable: calling 200 more into a 450 pot gives about 3.75:1 (pot will be 650). Vs a wide button opening range (20-40% hands), J9o has around 40%+ equity and strong implied odds deep stacked. OOP but manageable postflop with position advantage in multiway if SB calls (but folded). 3-betting risks too much without premium; folding is too tight in BB defense. Calling to realize equity and control pot size.

Preflop call with J9o from BB vs BTN raise was standard for speculative hand with position and deep stacks. Flop Kd Tc 7h is fairly dry rainbow board, missing my hand completely—no pair, no strong draw, just Jack-high with a weak gutshot backdoor (needing runner-runner 8Q or similar for straight). Equity vs Flash's wide BTN c-betting range (~50-70% freq) is around 30-40%, sufficient for check-call to one barrel but OOP requires caution. Donk betting is suboptimal OOP with no showdown value or strong semi-bluff; better to check and pot control, realize implied odds if he checks behind, or fold to big bets while calling small ones. Deep stacks (90+BB) allow maneuvering postflop. History shows Flash aggressive on button, likely cbets often, but check induces bluffs or check-backs.

On the flop of King-Ten-Seven rainbow, I have an open-ended straight draw with my Queen-Jack. Elon checked to me after calling my pre-flop raise. This board texture hits my perceived range as the pre-flop raiser quite well, even though I don't have a piece of it yet. A continuation bet is appropriate here to represent a King or a strong Ten, and to build the pot in case I hit my straight on the turn. I will bet 350, which is slightly more than half the pot, to apply pressure.

Preflop call with J9o was reasonable as a speculative hand from BB vs BTN open, with good playability, position postflop, and favorable odds (calling 200 into ~550 pot). Flop Kd Tc 7h completely misses: rainbow board, no pair, only a weak gutshot straight draw to the 8 (4 outs for ~8% equity), plus negligible backdoors. Facing Flash's 350 bet into 1000 pot OOP (~3.85:1), equity vs his likely wide c-bet range (60-75% freq on this dry board, including bluffs/air) is around 25-35%, but with poor playability postflop (few bets we can call, no strong semi-bluff), implied odds limited deep stacked but realized reverse implied if he has showdown. History shows Flash aggressive on BTN (frequent opens/cbets), but calling commits us to tough spots on turn OOP without improvement. Folding preserves stack for better spots; checking was pot control, now fold to single bet. No value in raising as bluff (weak blockers, board favors his range). Long-term EV positive to fold here in high-volume game.

6♦2♥ is a garbage hand, but I'm on the Button in a three-handed pot where open-stealing wide is standard. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to call; a standard 3xBB open to 300 buys a lot of fold equity and keeps me in position when called. I will be prepared to fold to a sizable 3-bet — this hand has poor postflop equity — but if called I'll proceed cautiously and c-bet appropriate flops or check/fold to heavy aggression. Raising is better than limp/call (too passive) or flat fold (gives up easy opportunities).

Pocket threes is a standard set-mining hand against a button raise from an aggressive player like Minni, who raises frequently with a wide range. Pot odds are favorable: risking 250 more (total 300 into growing pot) to win 450 currently, with excellent implied odds given deep stacks (effective ~10k) if I hit a set. Out of position but BB acts behind and may squeeze or fold, improving my equity. Folding is too tight preflop; 3-betting risks playing OOP with a marginal hand postflop against a potential 4-bet. Calling keeps the pot multiway potentially and realizes equity well.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ace-Six offsuit. The Button (Minni) raised to 3 levels, and the Small Blind (Elon) called. I am getting very good pot odds (calling 200 to win a pot that will be 900) to see a flop. While my hand is not particularly strong and I will be out of position against Minni, the price is too good to fold, and 3-betting into two players with A6o is unnecessarily risky given my chip lead. I will call to see the flop.

Pocket threes on a paired 7s board with a J. I called preflop for set-mining but whiffed completely. Board is 7h Jd 7s, giving me an underpair with no draws (one spade but no flush draw). Pot is 900, OOP first to act in a 3-way pot against Minni's button raise range (wide but can include 7x, Jx, overpairs) and Flash's BB calling range. No value to bet, and bluffing OOP multiway into aggressive opponents is poor EV. Check to pot control and likely fold to any bet, preserving stack for better spots. Effective stacks deep (~9k/10k/20k), but no reason to commit here.

On a paired board like 7h-Jd-7s, checking is the standard play from the Big Blind in a multi-way pot, especially when the pre-flop aggressor (Minni) is still to act. My Ace-high has some value, but I should play it defensively and wait to see how the other players react to this texture.

I raised wide preflop to steal in position, but the flop completely misses my 6♦2♥. Board is 7♥ J♦ 7♠ — a paired, fairly connected flop that heavily favors the callers' ranges (7x, Jx, pocket pairs). Both opponents checked, so bluff equity is low against two players on this texture and a c-bet is likely to be called or raised by many hands that beat me. I will pot-control and preserve my stack: check and see a turn card. If faced with significant aggression on a later street I'll fold — no equity to continue as a caller or to call down big bets with this hand.

Pocket 3s remain a complete whiff; pair of 3s is bottom pair at best on a board of 7h-7s-Jd-9c, crushed by any 7x, Jx, 9x, overpairs, or completed straights like T8. Preflop call was for set-mining with implied odds, but no hit, and flop checked through suggests caution from opponents but doesn't improve my equity. Turn 9c adds straight possibilities (T8) and pairs 9s, further deteriorating the board for my weak holding. OOP first in 3-way pot (pot 900, deep effective stacks ~9k), betting has zero value and bluffing is disastrous multiway against aggressive ranges (Minni button raiser, Flash BB defender). Check to pot control, induce bluffs if any, or see free river; fold to bets as equity is minimal.

On a board of 7-J-7-9, everyone checked the flop, indicating a likely weak range for all players. I am holding Ace-high (Ac-6h), which retains some showdown value in this spot. Since Elon checked the turn, checking behind remains the most prudent play to control the pot size and see a river card for free. Betting would likely only get called by better hands (like a Jack or a slow-played 7) and fold out hands I already beat.

Check. My 6♦2♥ missed the board entirely and I have no backdoor equity; this is a three‑way pot on a paired, middle‑card texture (7♥ J♦ 7♠ 9♣) that heavily favors caller ranges (7x, Jx, pocket pairs). Both opponents checked flop and turn, which reduces my bluff equity — getting two opponents to fold to a turn bluff is unlikely — and a bet here either gets called by better hands or invites a check‑raise I can’t profitably continue against. Pot is 900 and effective stacks are deep, so preserving stack and controlling the pot is the highest EV line: check and see a river, fold to meaningful aggression, and only look for a river bluff if the card and action create a believable story (e.g. a 9 or J pairing the board or a safe card where one opponent shows weakness).

Pocket 3s on a dry but paired and draw-heavy board (77J96s) remains a weak underpair after whiffing the set-mining preflop call.
